entries.name: Ankhtepot's Domain
entries: Har'Akir is altered by Ankhtepot's presence, creating the following effects:

items.name: Blistering Sun
items.entry: Any outdoor area of {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in Har'Akir is an area of {@hazard extreme heat|XDMG} (see the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|XDMG}).

items.name: Closing the Borders
items.entry: While in his domain, Ankhtepot can close or open the borders to Har'Akir at will. While the borders are closed, whirling sands fill the Mists surrounding Har'Akir. A creature that enters the Mists for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there takes {@damage 2d6} Slashing damage.

items.name: Control Undead
items.entry: Undead of CR 5 or lower in Har'Akir have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition and obey Ankhtepot's orders.

entries: If Ankhtepot dies, these effects end immediately.

entries.name: Ankhtepot's Lair
entries: A mountain-size black pyramid, Ankhtepot's tomb—called Pharaoh's Rest—looms over the City of the Dead. It can be seen from all across Har'Akir.

entries.name: Pharaoh's Rest Features
entries: Ancient and ornate, Pharaoh's Rest holds countless carvings of mysterious deities and Ankhtepot as an idealized pharaoh. A labyrinth of trapped tombs, embalming rooms, and treasure chambers fills the majority of this massive pyramid. Broad paths used by Ankhtepot's servants lead past deadly side passages to the heights of the monument. Prominent locations are depicted on {@book map 2.14|RHW|2|Map 2.14: Pharaoh's Rest} and have the following features:

items.name: Ankhtepot's Tomb
items.entry: The burial treasures and reliefs lining Ankhtepot's tomb's walls are shattered, having suffered ages of Ankhtepot's frustration. Hidden behind a bejeweled throne is a thoroughly defaced sarcophagus. Within, Ankhtepot awaits word of his ka's discovery, rising only when disturbed by trespassers or at the call of servants from the funeral barge chamber below.

items.name: Funeral Barge
items.entry: Ankhtepot envisions his trip to the afterlife taking place on a river. In an ornate ritual site, he has prepared a funeral barge and other ceremonial trappings necessary for his journey after his ka's recovery.

items.name: Lesser Tombs
items.entry: Throughout the pyramid are shrines, mummification chambers, and the crypts of Ankhtepot's servants. These include the burial chambers of several Children of Ankhtepot and the Darklord's son, Thutepot.

items.name: Shrine of Ankhtepot
items.entry: A vast, open shrine holds ominous statues of Har'Akir's gods and columns etched with exaggerated and fictitious events from Ankhtepot's rule. On the night of the full moon, a hidden magical elevator activates, granting access to the tombs above.

entries.name: Adventures in Har'Akir
entries: Adventures set in Har'Akir involve ancient secrets, manipulative cultists, the ancient dead, and the search for Ankhtepot's missing ka. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

entries.title: From the Temple of Ese, the priests of Pharaoh Ankhtepot control the people of Muhar

entries.name: Horror in Har'Akir
entries: Adventures in Har'Akir often include the following elements.

entries.name: Children of Ankhtepot
entries: Ankhtepot resurrected the priests who died alongside him as {@creature Mummy Lord|XMM|Mummy Lords}, granting each the head of an animal or monster corresponding to the fictitious gods he imposed on Har'Akir. The Children of Ankhtepot style themselves as harbingers of these gods while they manipulate cults or the domain's priesthood. Some have grown tired of Ankhtepot's detachment and seek to depose the pharaoh—particularly the treacherous mummy lord Senmet.

entries.name: Gods of Har'Akir
entries: Ankhtepot has destroyed all evidence of the deities the people of Har'Akir worshipped in ages past, replacing that pantheon with gods of his own creation, as summarized on the Gods of Har'Akir table.

entries.name: Pharaoh's Priests
entries: The priests of Har'Akir work Ankhtepot's will, not realizing their deities are false. They keep alert for strangers and omens related to the treasure their pharaoh covets without knowing its true nature. Promising strangers are brought to High Priest Isu Rehkotep at the Temple of Ese in Muhar. The decadent high priest wants to possess what her pharaoh seeks, planning to use it to manipulate Ankhtepot.

entries.name: Pharaoh's Soul
entries: Ankhtepot seeks his ka, the missing piece of his soul. It's up to you what form this takes. Roll on or choose a result from The Darklord's Soul table to determine the form of Ankhtepot's ka.

entries.caption: The Darklord's Soul
rows: An ankh holy to the old gods of Har'Akir.
A blessing granted to those who survive trials within the domain's endless sandstorms.
A canopic jar containing the pharaoh's heart hidden within a forgotten tomb.
A hawk or giant eagle that circles the sun.
Manifested in a joyous song sung by Muhar's people.
Pieces of a jewel hidden within each of the Children of Ankhtepot.
The soul of a character, perhaps one with the {@feat Echoing Soul|RHW} feat.
The spirit of an innocent healer who resembles the pharaoh as he was in life.

entries: Once the Darklord recovers his ka, Ankhtepot can restore his soul by completing an elaborate 1-hour ritual involving the sacrifice of a living person. Only then might he journey to the afterlife—whether he succeeds is up to the Dark Powers.

entries.title: Frightful monuments such as Ousa's Pyramid rise from the haunted, black-sand deserts of Har'Akir

entries.name: Har'Akir Campaign Arc
entries: Campaigns set in Har'Akir often involve the domain's treacherous priests, its ancient tombs, and the hunt for Ankhtepot's ka. A campaign here might follow this outline:

items.name: Levels 1–4
items.entry: The characters help a priest named Basras search a trap-laden, buried palace complex for a mysterious treasure. Within, they discover a set of oversize canopic jars. They take them to Isu Rehkotep in Muhar, but the high priest deems them worthless. Basras is thrown into the Mouth of Oru.

items.name: Levels 5–10
items.entry: Isu Rehkotep hires the characters to search for the pharaoh's missing treasure. She sends them to Senmet's Tomb to recover an ark full of ancient scrolls, claiming these are what the pharaoh desires. (She doesn't actually know the object of the pharaoh's search.)

items.name: Levels 11–15
items.entry: After the characters return the scrolls to Isu, Basras (now an Undead), reveals that the high priest seeks to use them to control a {@creature Mummy Lord|XMM} and oppose Ankhtepot, as detailed in the "Scrolls of the Dead" adventure.

entries.name: Scrolls of the Dead
entries: {@i Adventure for Level 12 Characters}

items.name: Situation
items.entry: High Priest Isu Rehkotep claims to have found the treasure Ankhtepot has been searching for and prepares to offer it to him during a ritual at the pyramid of Pharaoh's Rest. In truth, she plans to use a scroll that controls the Child of Ankhtepot called Senmet to destroy the pharaoh and claim his throne.

items.name: Hook
items.entry: The priest De'rah fears the pharaoh will vent his wrath on the people of Muhar when the high priest is discovered. She offers the characters 5,000 GP and a random magic item from the {@table Relics - Rare|XDMG|Relics—Rare table} in the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|XDMG} if they stop Isu.

entries.name: Encounters
entries: This adventure uses map 2.14 and consists of these encounters.

entries.name: Exploring the Pyramid
entries: Rehkotep's ritual takes place during the full moon, when the entrance to Pharaoh's Rest stands open. Pharaoh's Rest is a vast structure, but the path Rehkotep and her followers followed through the thick dust and sand of the lower reaches is obvious. Details of the pyramid's lower levels are beyond the scope of this adventure.

entries.name: Pyramid Heights
entries: When the characters reach the Shrine of Ankhtepot in the upper levels of Pharaoh's Rest, and when they enter the Lesser Tombs, roll on the following table to determine what lurks within.

rows: A {@creature Dao|XMM} compelled to defend the pyramid
Three {@creature Invisible Stalker|XMM|Invisible Stalkers} that chant curses
Four {@creature Mummy|XMM|Mummies} and two {@creature Death Dog|XMM|Death Dogs}
Two {@creature Mummy|XMM|Mummies} and two {@creature Shield Guardian|XMM|Shield Guardians}
Two {@creature Stone Golem|XMM|Stone Golems} dressed as pharaohs
Four {@creature Wraith|XMM|Wraiths} made of black sand

entries.name: Ritual Site
entries: On the funeral barge level of the pyramid, Isu Rehkotep (Medium, Lawful Evil {@creature Archpriest|XMM}) readies to call forth Ankhtepot from his tomb using a ritual involving a gong on the ceremonial ship. Meanwhile, two {@creature Priest|XMM|Priests} and two {@creature Death Cultist|XMM|Death Cultists} defend her. Isu avoids battle, but if any of her allies fall, she produces an ancient papyrus scroll and reads it, summoning Senmet.

entries.name: The Mummy
entries: If Isu reads her scroll, 1 round after her allies are defeated, the {@creature Mummy Lord|XMM} Senmet appears. Isu orders him to destroy the characters, and he obeys. Senmet fights until he or the scroll the high priest holds is destroyed. Isu doesn't join the battle and attempts to escape if Senmet is destroyed.

entries.name: The Scroll
entries: A character adjacent to Isu can steal the scroll from her by succeeding on a {@dc 16} Dexterity ({@skill Sleight of Hand|XPHB}) check or by taking an action if she has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} or {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition. Any character can take an action to destroy the scroll but can't use it to command Senmet. If it is destroyed, Senmet turns {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} toward Isu.

entries.name: The Pharaoh
entries: Once Isu and Senmet are dealt with, {@creature Ankhtepot|RHW} (see earlier in {@area this chapter|16e|x}) appears at the path leading up to his tomb. He demands the characters depart immediately but return with his missing ka. If they do so, fabulous riches will be theirs. If they don't, they will know his wrath. Characters who escape the tomb after the encounter with Ankhtepot earn a bonus 4,700 XP.

entries.name: Hazlan
entries: {@i Domain Doomed by Magic}

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: {@area Hazlik|19a|x}

items.name: Genres:
items.entry: {@area Dark fantasy|360|x} and {@area disaster horror|384|x}

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Amoral spellcasters, magical experiments, magic-ravaged environment, wild magic

items.name: Mist Talismans:
items.entry: Eye of Hazlik amulet, gremishka foot, scrap from a red robe

items.name: Aligned Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Diviner

items.name: Opposed Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Ghost

entries: Hazlan is a domain drained of vitality, frayed by magical disasters, and overrun with abominations. Here the amoral, spellcasting elite have so exploited magic that it's turned erratic and dangerous to use.

Encouraging these magical abuses is {@creature Hazlik|RHW}, the domain's zulkir, a title meaning wizard-overlord. The Darklord postures as the domain's most powerful wizard and views all others as either apprentices or test subjects. All the while, Hazlik hides the fact that he's lost the ability to learn new magic. Paranoid and controlling, Hazlik constantly spies on those in his domain through the Eyes of Hazlik. These pervasive, eye-shaped symbols appear on structures, decorations, clothing, and individuals across the domain—and through these eyes, the Darklord watches. Hazlik surveils his domain endlessly, reveling in the fear of his people, encouraging rivalries between his apprentices, and searching for magical innovations he can claim as his own.

entries.name: Domain Features
entries: Hazlan is known for the following features:

items.name: Eye of Hazlik
items.entry: Inhabitants claim the eyelike design called the Eye of Hazlik bears Hazlik's blessing and wards off dangerous magic. In truth, the symbol is a sensor for Hazlik's magical surveillance.

items.name: Magocracy
items.entry: The wizard {@area Hazlik|19a|x} rules Hazlan. His apprentices have free rein to exploit the land and its people to further their magical experiments.

items.name: Shattered by Magic
items.entry: The effects of magical disasters disfigure the domain, poisoning rivers with alchemical runoff, scarring the land with magical blasts, and unleashing unpredictable hazards.

items.name: Wild Magic
items.entry: Magic is unreliable in Hazlan, resulting in dangerous side effects (see "{@area Hazlan Wild Magic|190|x}").

entries.name: Locations in Hazlan
entries: Magical disasters and supernatural environments make Hazlan's arid landscape even deadlier and its natural rock monuments even stranger. The settlements of Sly-Var and Toyalis are described below, and other notable locations are summarized on the {@book Hazlan Locations table|RHW|2|Hazlan Locations}.

entries.name: Sly-Var
entries: In Sly-Var, mages obsessed with earning the Darklord's favor raise architecturally discordant towers that defy physical laws. The residents magically create whatever they need, resulting in little need for trade. This leaves the labyrinthine streets and knotted bridges between intertwined compounds eerily empty. The few common folk who traverse these surreal streets often disappear—the victims of magical guardians or of wizards who see the magically deficient as stock for their experiments.

entries.name: Toyalis
entries: The largest town in Hazlan, Toyalis boasts a population of poor, hardworking commoners united by terror of the domain's magic-using elite. Colorful depictions of magical creatures, arcane symbols, and the Eye of Hazlik cover the town's rough wooden and adobe structures. Every few weeks, one of Hazlik's apprentices leads a procession of attendants and magical wonders to Toyalis in search of replacement servants, new apprentices, or stock for magical experiments. A carnival atmosphere ensues, with the apprentice displaying their magical accomplishments and setting up competitions to assess the locals' capabilities—magical or otherwise. When a mage's procession leaves, it takes townsfolk deemed valuable with it.

entries.caption: Hazlan Locations
rows: A plant at the center of the dense Balsam Wood can cure any illness. Unnervingly, the forest is alive, aware, and prone to agony, screaming if someone disturbs so much as a leaf.
Alchemical pollution from the village of Ramulai has poisoned the toxic marsh now known as the Brew. Creeks and bogs often erupt with violent fungal creatures and magical effects.
The Fallen Sisters are three mountains that have collapsed into triangular, bottomless pits. Vistani often camp here.
Plants and creatures in the Fleshless Forest were turned to stone. The condition is infectious.
The Lacuna is a region of knee-high mists that reach toward places of powerful magic, such as Hazlik's palace, Veneficus. Anyone who steps into these mists vanishes, and few reemerge.
The sigils of Hazlik's mightiest apprentices mark the land in giant, magic-enhancing geoglyphs. The Lacuna grows toward these Marks—and a fourth, mysterious glyph.
Meteors and otherworldly creatures crash into this wasteland during every new moon.
Earth from deep underground rose to form the Mound of the Worm mesa, unleashing dozens of {@creature Purple Worm|XMM|Purple Worms}. The aged, albino worm Gravedrinker dominates this region.
The Orbitoclasts are floating rock formations that resemble lobotomy tools. Their pointed ends align toward the nearest magic-user.
A cult of magic-warped beings worships an eight-story, citrine egg. The cult searches for a prophet who's never been touched by magic to release the antimagic entity within.
Former lakes, the Playas are now quicksand-riddled {@creature Purple Worm|XMM} feeding grounds.
Mines near the village of Ramulai yield rare magical elements. Refineries process these volatile resources, but in so doing, they magically sicken the people and the land.
The lifeless Seething region crumbles away into the Mists. A heavy footfall can break its surface.

entries.name: Hazlik
entries: {@i Darklord of Desperation and Magical Disaster}
You're new, I can tell. Impress me, and maybe I'll let you be my newest apprentice.

The paranoid, egomaniacal archmage Hazlik clings to his power despite his magical ambitions being supernaturally smothered. Since Hazlik entered Hazlan, his magic has become unstable. Spells he casts behave erratically and fade swiftly—most lasting no more than an hour—while his magical creations fall apart in about a day. The idea of others learning this terrifies the Darklord, which leads him to claim others' works as his own, spy on potential rivals, and order reckless research into the nature of magic and his domain.

Hazlik suffers vivid nightmares wherein past rivals taunt and torment him. The Darklord avoids sleeping, employing magical tinctures to stay awake, but this causes him to vacillate between bouts of exhaustion, jitters, and paranoia.

Hazlik knows that his domain is being ripped apart by magical disasters—many of his own creation. He openly denies the realm's devastation, though, his fear of cascading ruin clashing with his inability to admit he's wrong.

entries.name: Hazlik's Tale
entries: On the world of Toril, Hazlik rose among the ranks of the merciless Red Wizards of Thay. Arrogant and convinced mortality was a weakness, he sought hidden truths of magic, ways to visit realms within the psyche, and command of reality.

As Hazlik's power increased, he developed rivalries with other Red Wizards, many of which ended with his foes magically scarred. But one rival Hazlik couldn't defeat: the wizard Indreficus. Hazlik gradually came to obsess over Indreficus, viewing him as his only worthy opponent. Indreficus shared Hazlik's genius and arrogance, and they spent seasons as vicious adversaries and passionate lovers. They drove one another to feats of magical brilliance both revolutionary and unspeakable. This culminated when Hazlik discovered that Indreficus planned to betray Hazlik to garner the favor of Thay's ruling zulkirs. Heartbroken and enraged, Hazlik captured Indreficus and subjected him to nightmarish magical manipulations that permanently transformed him into a pain-wracked, living magical portal. Hazlik presented his creation to the zulkirs, hoping to gain their patronage

The zulkirs revealed that Indreficus hadn't betrayed Hazlik. Rather, the ruling mages had watched the pair's ascent and, wary of their ambitions, undermined them before they became threats. Moreover, they deemed Hazlik's transformation of Indreficus an abomination. As punishment, the zulkirs decreed that every mage Hazlik had scarred could do the same in return to him, marking him with their personal symbols. As the first brand-wielding member of the mob of Hazlik's rivals closed on the wizard, Hazlik fled through the groaning portal that was Indreficus.

Hazlik emerged amid the pain-wracked dreamscapes of Indreficus. Unwittingly, Hazlik had entered the Domain of Dread known as the {@area Nightmare Lands|2b1|x}, where Indreficus's shrieking and condemnation hounded him. After months of torment, Hazlik escaped into the Mists and eventually emerged into a realm that knew nothing of magic. His Thayan tattoos had turned gold and warped into symbols meaning "traitor." Undaunted, Hazlik used his magic to dominate the people of this new realm, claiming the title of zulkir and dubbing his realm Hazlan.

entries.name: Hazlik's Domain
entries: Hazlan is altered by Hazlik's presence, creating the following effects:

items.name: Closing the Borders
items.entry: While in his domain, Hazlik can close or open the borders to Hazlan at will. While the borders are closed, the Mists surrounding Hazlan are filled with monstrous magical experiments. A creature that enters the Mists for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take 5 ({@dice 1d10}) Force damage and have the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the start of its next turn. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

items.name: Frayed Magic
items.entry: Whenever a creature (excluding Hazlik and his allies) casts a spell using a level 5+ spell slot, it rolls on the {@table Wild Magic Surge|XPHB|Wild Magic Surge table} in the {@book Player's Handbook|XPHB} to create a magical effect.

items.name: Magic Nightmares
items.entry: While in his lair, Veneficus, Hazlik can cast {@spell Clairvoyance|XPHB}, requiring no spell components and using the same spellcasting ability as his Spellcasting action. When the spell is cast this way, its range is all of Hazlan, Hazlik can see but can't hear through the sensor, and the sensor can appear only on an Eye of Hazlik.

entries: If {@creature Hazlik|RHW} dies, these effects end immediately. The effects resume if Hazlik gains a new body (see his Darklord Restoration trait).

entries.name: Hazlik's Lair
entries: Hazlik's crimson-towered palace, Veneficus, stands atop the jagged mesa called the Red Rise. The Darklord is attended by a staff of magical creations and his most accomplished apprentices.

entries.name: Veneficus Features
entries: Veneficus is littered with frivolous magic effects and magic items that no longer keep Hazlik's attention. The site is depicted on {@book map 2.16|RHW|2|Map 2.16: Veneficus} and has the following features:

items.name: Halls of Triumph
items.entry: Multiple audience chambers, display galleries, and pleasure gardens rival one another in decadence, each exhibiting grotesque artwork and trophies of Hazlik's achievements.

items.name: House of Solitude
items.entry: The secluded House of Solitude is magically locked, and Hazlik forbids anyone from entering. At night, muffled screams and flickering lights emanate from within.

items.name: Red Tower
items.entry: Hazlik's personal chambers fill the Red Tower. Magical portals connect the untold numbers of demiplanar chambers that make up the tower. Somewhere within lies Hazlik's orrery, a multistory magical device that allows the Darklord to see through any Eye of Hazlik in the domain.

entries.name: Adventures in Hazlan
entries: Tales in Hazlan involve radical magical experiments, supernatural environments, rivalries between mages, and magical surveillance. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

entries.title: The Eye of Hazlik watches the city of Sly-Var and the approaching retinue of one of the Darklord's apprentices

entries.name: Horror in Hazlan
entries: Adventures set in Hazlan often include the following elements.

entries.name: Eyes of Hazlik
entries: Hazlik spies on his domain's inhabitants using the Eye of Hazlik. This eyelike sigil adorns buildings, clothing, talismans, and creatures across the domain. Locals claim the symbol protects them from dangerous magic and creatures. Hazlik uses the eyes to spy on useful or threatening mages and to reinforce his reputation as an ever-present overlord.

Hazlik often sends loyal apprentices or magical creatures to capture those whose behavior witnessed through the eyes offended him. He turns such individuals into fodder for experimentation in Sly-Var or Veneficus.

entries.name: Hazlan Wild Magic
entries: As a result of unchecked arcane experimentation, magic functions erratically in Hazlan. Whenever a creature in Hazlan expends a spell slot to cast a spell or activates a magic item, it rolls {@dice 1d10}. On a 1, it unleashes a random magical effect. Roll on the {@table Wild Magic Surge|XPHB|Wild Magic Surge table} in chapter 3 of the {@book Player's Handbook|XPHB} to determine the effect. Consider describing these results in unsettling ways that don't change their effects, as in these examples:

items: Summoned creatures use the proscribed stat blocks but are cosmetically warped to resemble undead or aberrant creatures.
Effects that teleport the caster relocate the caster into a misty expanse full of threatening shapes.
Effects on creatures cause unsettling sensations, such as the feeling of earwigs wriggling beneath one's skin or one's teeth struggling free.

entries: Anyone who openly wears an Eye of Hazlik can choose not to roll on the Wild Magic Surge table in this way once per day.

entries.name: Hazlik's Apprentices
entries: Dozens of mages serve as Hazlik's apprentices. Between infrequent, demeaning group lessons with the Darklord, these apprentices pit themselves against one another to impress Hazlik by pushing the boundaries of magical innovation. Those who gain Hazlik's favor are promised arcane secrets known only to the Darklord—secrets that might not exist. This creates a cadre of Hazlani mages who recklessly seek to impress Hazlik by the following means, among others:

items: Changing the nature of reality to suit their whims
Creating monuments in Hazlik's honor
Devising new magic items, spells, or supernatural materials
Forging unique magical servants for Hazlik
Stealing rare magic items
Unleashing dramatic displays of magical might

entries: Hazlik's current favorite apprentices include the masked wizard Yhal the Skygazer, the shadow-cloaked Ruzelo, and the daring genius Eleni.

entries.name: Hazlan Campaign Arc
entries: Campaigns set in Hazlan often involve mages abusing their power to garner Hazlik's fickle favor. A campaign here might follow this outline:

items.name: Levels 1–3
items.entry: The characters arrive in Toyalis, where servants of the mage Khrinitov abduct locals with supposed psychic potential. Eleni, an apprentice of Hazlik, believes Khrinitov's experiments are pointless. She hires the characters to rescue the villagers from Khrinitov's tower amid the Orbitoclasts. In doing so, the characters reveal Khrinitov to be an {@creature Intellect Devourer|XMM}, a {@creature Brain in a Jar|RHW} (see {@area chapter 5|399|x}), or another aberrant being.

items.name: Levels 4–8
items.entry: Eleni reveals that she's trying to undermine Hazlik and protect the people of Hazlan. She tells the characters of a magic mineral in the Mound of the Worm that {@creature Purple Worm|XMM|Purple Worms} pursue. She encourages the party to collect the mineral and use the purple worms to destroy locations like the Marks. As the characters do so, they face magical guardians and hazards. During their travel, they're exposed to strange magic and might gain Dark Gift feats (see {@area chapter 1|0a7|x}).

items.name: Levels 9–12
items.entry: Eleni asks the characters to come to Sly-Var to plan a raid on Hazlik's palace. There, she reveals that she's been exposing them to dangerous magic from across the domain. She entraps them with a portal that leads to arena-like laboratories connected to Veneficus, where {@creature Hazlik|RHW} pits them against terrors his other apprentices have created. The characters must escape, potentially facing Eleni and Hazlik along the way.

entries.name: Eyes of the Darklord
entries: {@i Adventure for Level 10 Characters}

items.name: Situation
items.entry: Subjects of magical experimentation seek to escape Hazlan, but every time they try, they're spotted through the Eyes of Hazlik, and the Darklord's apprentices hunt them down.

items.name: Hook
items.entry: Allies hire the characters to help them escape Hazlan by destroying Hazlik's orrery in Veneficus. They offer to pay the party 4,000 GP once they're guided to another domain.

entries.name: Encounters
entries: The adventure uses map 2.16 and consists of these encounters:

entries.name: Greeting at Veneficus
entries: The rooms within Veneficus (see map 2.16) and the walls outside all bear at least one Eye of Hazlik. If the characters are spotted, one of Hazlik's former apprentices (Medium, Chaotic Evil {@creature Archmage|XMM}) appears. This apprentice has been transformed to look and behave like the Darklord. The duplicate belittles the characters and fights to the death.

entries.name: Galleries of Suffering
entries: As the characters explore Veneficus, roll on the following table to determine what they find within.

rows: Another {@creature Archmage|XMM} that behaves like Hazlik (see above), or a similarly disguised {@creature Incubus|XMM}
Artwork that releases a {@creature Bone Devil|XMM} if touched
Three {@creature Mage|XMM|Mages} arguing about who's greatest
Three {@creature Waxwork|RHW|Waxworks} (see {@area chapter 5|3c8|x}) disguised as mages arguing about who's Hazlik's favorite
One {@creature Stone Golem|XMM} sculpted to look like Hazlik

entries.name: Flawed Magic
entries: Among mundane decorations, this room contains a random magic item. Roll on the {@table Arcana - Common|XDMG|Arcana—Common table} in chapter 7 of the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|XDMG}. The item functions once, causes a wild magic surge (see "{@area Hazlan Wild Magic|190|x}"), and then crumbles to dust.

entries.name: Red Tower
entries: Shifting portals connect the passages in the Red Tower. Every time the characters pass through a portal in the tower, roll {@dice 1d8}. If the number rolled is lower than the number of portals passed through, the characters arrive in the orrery chamber. On an 8, the characters appear at the entrance to Veneficus.

entries.name: Orrery Chamber
entries: This room is dominated by a magical orrery only Hazlik can use. It is defended by a {@creature Relentless Juggernaut|RHW}. The orrery has AC 19, HP 25, and Immunity to Psychic damage. Every time the orrery takes damage, roll on the {@table Wild Magic Surge|XPHB|Wild Magic Surge table} in chapter 3 of the {@book Player's Handbook|XPHB}. Negative effects affect the character who damaged the orrery, while beneficial effects affect the juggernaut.

entries.name: Hazlik
entries: After the orrery is destroyed, {@creature Hazlik|RHW} (see earlier in {@area this chapter|19a|x}) appears and offers a magic-using character power and influence as his apprentice. If the character accepts, he commands that character to murder the others. If the character declines, Hazlik mocks the party. The Darklord uses magic to flee if he takes any damage, swearing revenge. Characters who destroy the orrery and escape Veneficus earn a bonus 3,100 XP.

entries.name: Innsmouth
entries: {@i Domain of the Imprisoned Apocalyptic}

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: {@area Cthulhu|1a1|x}

items.name: Genre:
items.entry: {@area Cosmic horror|357|x}

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Cosmic horror creatures, invasive alien dreams, surreal locations, warring cults

items.name: Mist Talismans:
items.entry: Effigy of Great Cthulhu, starmetal rod or amulet with eldritch runes, sahuagin-tooth fishing lure

items.name: Aligned Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Bishop

items.name: Opposed Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Donjon

entries: Hidden amid the Domains of Dread lies a hideous realm defiant of reason and smothered by the Mists. From a violent, murky sea rises a crescent of shattered peaks, mangled spires broken like the jaws of some slain thalassic titan. From noxious fumaroles, miasmic pillars lurch from this accursed isle into a sky inconsistently lit by a distant, drowned light.

Strange ruins dot this island's rocky coasts, some suggestive of forgotten peoples, others alien in form and purpose. Massive statues of crimson-flecked bloodstone loom over sheer cliffs, each a towering effigy of some profane monstrosity or alien god. One shore bears the wreckage of a few dozen collapsing wooden structures. Little evidence remains of this settlement's residents, but at its edge lies a toppled sign bearing a word in Common that gives the island its name: Innsmouth.

Despite its forsaken appearance, the isle of Innsmouth isn't abandoned. Cults vie for control of the island, among them the servants of Great Cthulhu, a harbinger of world-rending aberrance. At the island's hidden heart, {@creature Cthulhu|RHW} lies dreaming, its alien mind reaching across the Domains of Dread and beyond, drawing to it those who might rouse its full might and unleash it from its prison in the Mists.

entries.name: Domain Features
entries: Innsmouth is known for the following features:

items.name: Cosmic Horror Visions
items.entry: Cthulhu's dreams draw aberrant beings and shape Innsmouth as an amalgamation of cosmic horrors, from disparate ruins on the surface to stranger realms in the depths.

items.name: Cult Haven
items.entry: Evil cults devoted to the entities Cthulhu, Dagon, Olhydra, and Zhudun struggle to control the domain. These cults view strangers as useful allies (see "{@area Cults of Innsmouth|1a6|x}" for details).

items.name: Dreams of Cthulhu
items.entry: {@area Cthulhu|1a1|x} seeks freedom. It manipulates the dreams of those on the island, sharing visions of their escape from the domain resulting from the same rituals that would free it.

entries.name: Locations in Innsmouth
entries: Innsmouth is an inhospitable expanse of alien mountains and ruin-littered coasts. The village of Innsmouth and the mazelike Vaults are described below, and other notable locations are summarized on the {@book Innsmouth Locations table|RHW|2|Innsmouth Locations}.

entries.name: Innsmouth
entries: The ruined coastal village of Innsmouth is the most obvious shelter from the storms, alien creatures, and strange events on the island. The Cult of the Chthonic Night knows this and realizes its imprisoned master, Great Cthulhu, calls to strangers to aid the cultists in releasing it. This makes the ruined village a shadowy war zone, where cultists and their servants hunt for fresh acolytes and sacrifices.

entries.name: The Vaults
entries: Beneath the mountains of Innsmouth winds a labyrinth of improbable passages. Dramatic caverns narrow into the corridors of buried palaces that transform into the wooden halls of forgotten farmhouses. Regularly, these underground paths open into vast cavities, many with their own strange environments, weather, and celestial bodies. What lies among the Vaults? Cultists speak of the realms of alien beings, ghoul empires, countrysides ravaged by sapient energies, the dwellings of reclusive witches, and a thin place in the walls of the prison where Great Cthulhu lies trapped.

entries.caption: Innsmouth Locations
rows: Within a ring of bismuth monoliths called the Blind Observatory, the clouds vanish, revealing whirling galaxies. The {@area Cult of the Heavenly Doom|1aa|x} searches this yawning infinity for meaning.
Vividly colored gases escape the mouth of the sea cave called the Cleft of Dreams, which extends into surreal realms within the Vaults.
Sahuagin dwell amid caves of sharp, crimson coral in the Devil Reef. They are violently divided between followers of the shark-god Sekolah and members of the {@area Cult of the Scaly Abyss|1ab|x}.
The head of a giant statue depicting a half-melted, eight-eyed humanoid rises just above the waterline. Its mouth—large enough to sail a small ship through—leads to the Vaults.
The swampy islands known as the Eyes of Olhydra almost disappear at high tide. At low tide, servants of Olhydra flock to a monument called the Fathomless Gate.

The domain's highest mount, Graven Peak, is carved into the shape of a terrifying octopoid fiend. Its brow is etched with a mysterious, branch-like rune. Deep below lies {@area Cthulhu's Vault|1a4|x}.
Hollows in a series of standing stones depict the progression of a crumbling constellation. Cultists believe that a force capable of moving the stars left this message.
A procession of thirty-foot-tall bloodstone statues of gaunt, distorted figures circles the ruins of a domed structure. Some of the statues are partially buried in the nearby mountain.
A tower blazing with a vaporous orange flame resembles a lighthouse, but the island's cultists say the Night Light is meant to draw something close rather than warn viewers away.
These physics-defying ruins change every time they're observed, reflecting wildly different ages and cultures. Sapient, hyperdimensional cats occasionally live here.
A ghoul-haunted hotel called the Wharf leans precariously toward the sea.
The wreck of the Shrike lies beneath the waves, inexplicably encased in black ice. Nearby, a duplicate of the ship shows evidence of a great fire, and another duplicate lies crushed.

entries.name: Cthulhu
entries: {@i Darklord What Eternal Lies}
Ph'nglui mglw'nafh Cthulhu R'lyeh wgah'nagl fhtagn.
entries.by: Cult of the Chthonic Night chant

entries: Cthulhu was—and inevitably will be once more—a threat to the entire multiverse. For untold ages, the Dark Powers have bound this harbinger of doom. Unlike the other prisoners of the Domains of Dread, Cthulhu isn't plagued by ironic torments or thwarted ambitions. Rather, this ageless terror remains physically bound and can exert only a fraction of its terrible might. Nevertheless, Cthulhu's dreams slip beyond its prison, manipulating minds and orchestrating events to ensure its release and a new epoch of multiversal doom.

entries.name: Cthulhu's Tale
entries: Spawned beyond the borders of the multiverse in realities where time and life have little worth, Cthulhu is a servant of elder evils and a harbinger of their inevitable ruin. For untold eons, Cthulhu's will influenced receptive minds across the multiverse, reaching out in dreams and using them to open gateways to the Far Realm. Through such portals, Cthulhu visited the Material Plane, corrupted and laid waste to civilizations, and erected city-size monuments to its mighty predecessors.

Such distortion and defilement didn't go unnoticed by the Dark Powers. Multiple times the Mists closed around dread Cthulhu, and multiple times the terror evaded them. It was drawn into the Shadowfell when an extinct cabal of mages summoned it onto a sepulchral world of the Material Plane during a once-in-an-era alignment of eclipses. With the magic of the Shadowfell enhanced, the Mists rose and drew in Cthulhu. Raging and grasping, Cthulhu dragged with it remnants of past conquests and violent minds linked to its own. These disparate nightmares became the foundation of its prison: a surreal island hidden amid the Domains of Dread.

entries.name: Cthulhu's Domain
entries: Innsmouth is altered by Cthulhu's presence, creating the following effects:

items.name: Choking Depths
items.entry: A creature can {@hazard Suffocation|XPHB|hold its breath} for only 30 seconds while in the domain.

items.name: Closing the Borders
items.entry: While in its domain, Cthulhu can close or open the borders of Innsmouth at will. While the borders are closed, those who enter the Mists surrounding Innsmouth become lost at sea amid inky fog for {@dice 3d4} days before arriving once more at Innsmouth.

items.name: Something in the Air
items.entry: Creatures other than Cthulhu and its allies have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

entries: If Cthulhu dies, these effects end immediately.

entries.name: Cthulhu's Lair
entries: Amid surreal expanses within Innmouth's depths lies the entrance to Cthulhu's Vault, a prison within the Mists where Cthulhu lies trapped. The entrance to Cthulhu's Vault is a ritual site etched with symbols of power from across time and hundreds of worlds. Those who perform the proper rituals, guided by visions from Cthulhu itself, might free themselves from the domain of Innsmouth, and, in so doing, unleash Cthulhu on the multiverse.

entries.name: Cthulhu's Vault Features
entries: Cthulhu's Vault is a hidden realm, but the entrance to it lies amid the Vaults beneath the island. The vault is depicted on {@book map 2.18|RHW|2|Map 2.18: Cthulhu's Vault} and has the following features:

items.name: Noisome Brine
items.entry: A dark, frothy waterway winds through the Vaults, ultimately emerging from either the Cleft of Dreams or the mouth of the Drowned Colossus.

items.name: Ritual Site
items.entry: A partially toppled monument bearing a tentacled visage marks a weak point in Cthulhu's prison. Before it stands an altar etched with bizarre runes. These symbols are of otherworldly design, but each cult of Innsmouth knows the meaning of a few. Only cultists imbued with the might of all four cults can perform the ritual these runes describe and force open Cthulhu's prison.

items.name: The Perceptive
items.entry: A small ship called the Perceptive lies beached here, a remnant of a group of cultists' past attempt to access Cthulhu's Vault. The bowsprit is broken, ending in a jagged point.

items.name: The Providence
items.entry: The shattered remains of a ship called the Providence serves as the shelter of a mysterious seer. It's said this former cultist knows more of Cthulhu's Vault than any other creature.

entries.name: Cults of Innsmouth
entries: Four cults operate in Innsmouth, each with its own vile ends. While these groups are ruinously evil, characters might temporarily ally with one or more to oppose another foe or to escape the domain.

entries.name: Cults and Cultists
entries: The following groups vie for power on and under Innsmouth. Following each cult's name are the ritual words cult members use in their blasphemous practices, along with other details.

entries.name: Cult of the Chthonic Night
entries: {@i Hail, Hail, Cthulhu Awaits}

items.name: Worships:
items.entry: Cthulhu

items.name: Leader:
items.entry: King-Priest Eeyog-Yah

items.name: Goal:
items.entry: Free Cthulhu from imprisonment

items.name: Lair:
items.entry: The village of Innsmouth and the Vaults

entries: Initiates of the Cult of the Chthonic Night open their minds to the whispers of Great Cthulhu and seek to free the imprisoned terror from the Domains of Dread. Predominantly composed of {@creature Aberrant Cultist|XMM|Aberrant Cultists} and {@creature Kuo-toa|XMM} that dwell amid waterlogged ruins deep below the Graven Peak, the cult interprets visions from Cthulhu as omens, calls for sacrifice, and signs of their god's coming.

King-Priest Eeyog-Yah, an ageless and desiccated {@creature Kuo-toa Archpriest|XMM}, leads the cult. Eeyog-Yah occupies a state of perpetual somnambulism and narrates its visions. The priest's attendants decipher their leader's rambling as directives from their god.
Those who join the cult and participate in its induction rituals hear the voice of Cthulhu more clearly than others do (see "{@area Cult Dark Gifts|1ae|x}").

entries.title: Symbol of the Cult of the Chthonic Night Symbol of the Cult of the Drowning Wave Symbol of the Cult of the Heavenly Doom Symbol of the Cult of the Scaly Abyss

entries.name: Cult of the Drowning Wave
entries: {@i Make Clean for the Aeon Wave}

items.name: Worships:
items.entry: Olhydra

items.name: Leader:
items.entry: Draa Shoom

items.name: Goal:
items.entry: Sink Innsmouth below the waves

items.name: Lair:
items.entry: Eyes of Olhydra

entries: Those who await the Drowning Wave seek to make Innsmouth and all its inhabitants part of Olhydra's realm on the Elemental Plane of Water. Olhydra, the Prince of Evil Water, then could use the might of Cthulhu and the mass sacrifice of the island to dominate the Elemental Planes and flood the Material Plane.

Draa Shoom, the cult's {@creature Aboleth|XMM} leader, believes Olhydra will return the cult to a place of dominance over countless worlds in return for its sacrifice. To effect this, Draa Shoom coaxes {@creature Elemental Cultist|XMM|Elemental Cultists} and shipwrecked individuals to the Eyes of Olhydra. There, amid boggy ruins and the lairs of murky elemental creatures, rises the Fathomless Gate. This ancient stone arch, thought to connect to the Elemental Plane of Water, is covered by bars of starmetal, which prevent passage by solid creatures. The cult seeks the proper rituals to open the gate, allowing Olhydra's scouring waters to flood the domain.
Those who participate in the cult's induction rituals are infused with a draught of Olhydra's sapient form (see "{@area Cult Dark Gifts|1ae|x}").

entries.name: Cult of the Heavenly Doom
entries: {@i Empty Yourself to Gorge on the Stars}

items.name: Worships:
items.entry: Zhudun

items.name: Leader:
items.entry: Celesta Grayl

items.name: Goal:
items.entry: Open gateways to join Zhudun

items.name: Lair:
items.entry: Blind Observatory and the Map of the Eclipse

entries: Among the broken heights of the domain, the obsessed astronomers of the Cult of the Heavenly Doom seek signs in the heavens and omens from Zhudun, the Corpse Star, an Elder Evil that wanders space and knows the secrets of extinction. These cultists hope to climb into the heavens and join Zhudun.

The cult's leader, Celesta Grayl, has sought Zhudun for centuries. While documenting her observations, Grayl discovered a path to immortality and became a {@creature Vampire Umbral Lord|XMM}. {@creature Death Cultist|XMM|Death Cultists}, along with desperate and deluded souls, have flocked to the ancient stargazer, forming the cult around Grayl and her discoveries despite their leader's protestations and predations. The vampire suffers the cultists only so long as they help her decipher secrets from ruins around the island and retrieve knowledge from the grip of other cults. She believes their discoveries will help her reveal when the stars are right to join Zhudun in the dark places between the stars.
Those who join the cult and participate in its induction rituals release a portion of their life to the stars (see "{@area Cult Dark Gifts|1ae|x}").

entries.name: Cult of the Scaly Abyss
entries: {@i The Prince Awaits—Become the Way}

items.name: Worships:
items.entry: Dagon

items.name: Leader:
items.entry: Xr'gh

items.name: Goal:
items.entry: Summon Dagon

items.name: Lair:
items.entry: Dagon's Claws

entries: The followers of Dagon, Demon Prince of the Darkened Depths, lurk among and beneath the waves of Innsmouth. Ancient even among inhabitants of the Abyss, Dagon seeks to drag demon lords and their Abyssal realms into its demesne of Shadowsea. Once sated on the flesh of its foes, Dagon aspires to unleash its might across the multiverse in a sanity-shattering wave.

Xr'gh, a {@creature Sahuagin Baron|XMM} fanatically devoted to Dagon, seeks to turn all who dwell beneath the waves to the demon lord's worship. Xr'gh seeks enough sacrifices to turn the sea red with blood, an offering great enough to attract Dagon's notice and draw the demon lord to Innsmouth. The cult leader expects Dagon then to feed on Cthulhu and begin its conquest of the Abyss. Ravenous {@creature Sahuagin Warrior|XMM|Sahuagin} follow Xr'gh, as do {@creature Fiend Cultist|XMM|Fiend Cultists} and murderers desirous of the eerie gold treasures the sahuagin offer from their submerged vaults. The Cult of the Scaly Abyss hunts the domain's ruined villages, abducting anyone who seeks shelter amid the wreckage. Those they find, they drag through secret paths beneath the waves to their ritual site under the island called Dagon's Claw.
Those who join the cult and participate in its induction rituals develop shark-like organs (see "{@area Cult Dark Gifts|1ae|x}").

entries.name: Cult Rites
entries: The cults of Innsmouth seek initiates to aid in their unfathomable works. Those who prove themselves reliable devotees gain access to the transformative power of strange gods.

entries.name: Cult Renown
entries: The cults of Innsmouth reward those who honor their obscene patrons. Use the {@book guidance|XDMG|2|Renown} in the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|XDMG} to track characters' individual standing within Innsmouth's cults. Characters can gain renown with a cult by performing deeds such as those in the Innsmouth Cults Renown table.

entries.caption: Innsmouth Cults Renown
rows: Ritual sacrifice of an enemy or magic item
Slaying the leader of an enemy cult
footnotes: *Renown for this deed can be gained only once per week.

entries.name: Cult Dark Gifts
entries: Characters who have a Renown Score of 10+ with an Innsmouth cult are honored as the chosen of the cult's patron. They are urged to take part in a ritual that inducts them into the cult and bestows on them a supernatural boon. A character who participates in such a ritual gains the Dark Gift (see {@area chapter 1|0a7|x}) noted on the Innsmouth Cults Dark Gifts table.

These Dark Gifts include magical transformations associated with the cult's patron and its members. This magic can make it easier to navigate parts of the domain and bypass seals made by cult magic. This includes the binding sigils that mark Cthulhu's Vault. A character with one of these Dark Gifts can't gain another. A character with one of these gifts who leaves Innsmouth can choose to lose the Dark Gift or keep it.

entries.name: Adventures in Innsmouth
entries: Adventures in Innsmouth involve cults, degenerate rituals, nightmarish visions, and aberrant transformations. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

entries.title: Nightmarish monuments and reality-defying ruins cover the island called Innsmouth

entries.name: Corruptive Horror
entries: The cults of Innsmouth are monstrous and dangerous, but not unwelcoming. Each seeks to bring a different terrifying cataclysm to Innsmouth. As characters interact with these cults and learn more of the island's secrets, bizarre dreams encourage the characters to accept the Dark Gifts the cults offer and balance rivalries to gain the might of each. Groups that engage with each cult might have strange adventures across Innsmouth and undergo bizarre transformations. Those that do will find themselves with the power to free Cthulhu from the island—and maybe themselves as well.

entries.name: Dreams of Innsmouth
entries: Cthulhu sends dreams to those in Innsmouth—and in realms beyond—that might help it escape its prison. These dreams include strange images spurring the dreamers to seek the cults of Innsmouth, gain their powers, and then dispel the sigils in Cthulhu's Vault. Dreams sent by Cthulhu might focus on the following topics and imagery:

items.name: Cult of the Chthonic Night:
items.entry: The village of Innsmouth, the Graven Peak, desiccated fish, tentacles

items.name: Cult of the Drowning Wave:
items.entry: The Eyes of Olhydra, shipwrecks, tidal waves, three eyes underwater

items.name: Cult of the Heavenly Doom:
items.entry: The peaks of Innsmouth, corpses in space, monoliths, whirling stars

items.name: Cult of the Scaly Abyss:
items.entry: Dagon's Claws, deep-sea predators, sharks eating sharks, undersea ruins

items.name: Cthulhu's Vault:
items.entry: The Cleft of Dreams, the Drowned Colossus, dark passages, strange sigils

entries: Any of these dreams might end with a vision of the Mists parting and the characters' home or a similarly tantalizing location appearing beyond.

entries.name: Old One's Bargain
entries: Cthulhu's dream-messages tempt characters to release the entity to win their own escape from its domain. Whether Cthulhu can or will fulfill this offer is up to you, but the characters might not wish to jeopardize reality to escape. In the course of exploring Innsmouth, they can find the writings of obsessed wizards and cult heresies that suggest the following secrets:

items.name: Cthulhu's Disruption
items.entry: Cthulhu's power is such that, should someone release it, the Mists around Innsmouth would open and those in the domain could escape.

items.name: Cthulhu's Might
items.entry: Cthulhu wouldn't emerge from its prison at full strength. For approximately one hour after its release, heroes might force Cthulhu back into its prison, should they subject the terror to potent magic such as {@spell Imprisonment|XPHB} or {@spell Wish|XPHB}, or a blow powerful enough to disrupt its physical form, like a powerful lightning strike, a massive explosion, or a strike from a large sailing ship.

entries: Elaborate on these possibilities as characters explore Innsmouth, and encourage them to believe they might be able to outwit a Great Old One.

entries.name: Otherworldly Relics
entries: The monsters noted in the "{@area Cosmic Horror|357|x}" section of {@book chapter 4|RHW|4} make appropriate inhabitants of the ruins and vaults of Innsmouth. These beings often possess evidence of alien technologies and cultures. Innsmouth's cults covet such relics. Roll on or choose a result from the Alien Relics table to inspire what otherworldly beings might possess.

entries.caption: Alien Relics
rows: A crystalline wand with a monstrous face.
An jade amulet shaped like a sphinx.
A metallic cluster of globes and half globes.
A preserved brain in a glass cylinder.

entries.name: Innsmouth Campaign Arc
entries: Campaigns set in Innsmouth involve warring cults, unfathomable beings, and bizarre transformations. A campaign here might follow this outline:

items.name: Levels 1–2
items.entry: The characters wash up on Innsmouth island and shelter in the village. They experience strange dreams. Soon after, they encounter cultists who seek to initiate them and others who seek to sacrifice them. The characters gain allies among one of the island's cults.

items.name: Levels 3–5
items.entry: The allied cult sends the characters to disrupt other cults and retrieve relics from the ruins. In return, the cult offers insights into the characters' dreams and suggests ways to escape the island. After proving their worth, a character receives the cult's Dark Gift (see "{@area Cult Rites|1ac|x}").

items.name: Levels 6–16
items.entry: Led by their dreams and duplicitous cult members, the characters move from cult to cult, gaining influence by delving into ruins and strange vaults. In doing so, they collect Dark Gifts, as well as hints about Cthulhu's Vault and ways to undermine the Great Old One (see "{@area Old One's Bargain|1b2|x}").

items.name: Levels 17–20
items.entry: After collecting a Dark Gift from each cult, the characters follow a treacherous path through the Vaults to Cthulhu's Vault. There, they can risk releasing {@creature Cthulhu|RHW} to escape the island.

entries.name: Vault of the Elder
entries: {@i Adventure for Level 11 Characters}

items.name: Situation
items.entry: Cult lore tells of a vault that is home to a wise elder who knows the secrets of escaping the island.

items.name: Hook
items.entry: A cult leader shares directions to the vault but wants one of the strange relics the elder is said to hoard.

entries.name: Encounters
entries: The adventure uses map 2.18 and consists of these encounters:

entries.name: Vault Ambush
entries: On the way to the vault, two {@creature Cultist Fanatic|XMM|Cult Fanatics} and a {@creature Fiend Cultist|XMM} (or another enemy cultist selected from those mentioned in "{@area Cults of Innsmouth|1a6|x}") ambush the characters.

entries.name: Frigid Spires
entries: The Vault of the Elder lies in a realm of icy mountains gripped by extreme cold (see "{@book Environmental Effects|XDMG|2|Environmental Effects}" in the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|XDMG}). The towers of a gravity-defying stone city protrude from one mountain.

entries.name: Hollow City
entries: The characters find the city filled with narrow ramps and abandoned, tubelike structures etched with eerie hieroglyphs. Of particular note is an out-of-place collection of tents and a templelike building.

entries.name: Encampment
entries: Brown mold covers the tents (see {@hazard Brown Mold|XDMG|chapter 3} of the Dungeon Master's Guide). If the characters remove the mold, they find five cultists frozen inside the tents, wrapped in blankets. One wears a {@item Ring of Mind Shielding|XDMG}.

entries.name: Elder Temple
entries: The templelike building bears carvings depicting five-winged beings fighting giant, fungus-like insects. Through the temple lies a path leading deeper into the mountain—and defended by a {@creature Shoggoth|RHW} (see {@area chapter 5|3b6|x}).

entries.name: Frozen Warning
entries: Ice encrusts the walls of the deeper temple, covering more strange carvings. Etched into the ice over a depiction of another five-winged creature is a warning in Common: "Beware the Elder."

entries.name: Elder's Reliquary
entries: The passage ends in a chamber with four pillars of ice. Perched on one is a {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} {@creature Elder Thing|RHW} (see {@area chapter 5|3a2|x}).

entries.name: Ice Tombs
entries: The pillars of ice each hold a shadowy shape. These pillars have AC 14, HP 20, Immunity to Poison and Psychic damage, and Vulnerability to Fire damage. The pillars contain the following:

items.name: Alien Relic
items.entry: The first pillar contains a strange relic. Roll on the "{@book Alien Relics|RHW|2|Alien Relics}" table earlier in this chapter to determine what it is.

items.name: Mi-Go
items.entry: Two pillars contain {@creature Mi-Go|RHW} (see {@area chapter 5|3aa|x}), which awake 1 round after being freed.

items.name: Vault Map
items.entry: A hieroglyph-etched slab in the final pillar charts a route from Innsmouth's surface to Cthulhu's Vault. It also provides information from the "{@area Old One's Bargain|1b2|x}" section. However, only someone with the {@book Dark Gift|RHW|2|Innsmouth Cults Dark Gifts} of the {@area Cult of Heavenly Doom|1aa|x} can decipher its alien language.

entries.name: Kalakeri
entries: {@i Domain of Betrayal and Revenge}

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: {@area Ramya Vasavadan|1c4|x}

items.name: Adventure Genres:
items.entry: {@area Gothic horror|37b|x} and {@area dark fantasy|360|x}

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Monstrous leaders, family intrigue, war-torn nation

items.name: Mist Talismans:
items.entry: Rusted tulwar, shield with a wyvern-and-lotus emblem, a polished skull

items.name: Aligned Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Avenger

items.name: Opposed Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Artifact

entries: Deceptively beautiful, Kalakeri is a land of verdant rainforests and a web of rivers and lakes known as the Backwaters. At the height of its prosperity, Kalakeri was a robust center of art, commerce, and religion, with merchants spreading wild tales of the land, using fanciful names such as Sri Raji and the Steaming Lands. Now Kalakeri is a conflict-torn shadow of its past glory. The remnants of the Vasavadan dynasty—three heirs transformed into monsters by their depravity and hatred—trap the land in an endless cycle of war.

The people of Kalakeri live in fear of {@creature Ramya Vasavadan|RHW|Maharani Ramya Vasavadan}, the domain's Darklord, as well as her treacherous siblings, Arijani and Reeva. The Darklord's curse manifests both in her deathless desire to rule Kalakeri and in her siblings' endless rebellions. Both sides escalate their atrocities against one another, drawing the powerful and the innocent alike into their squabbles. As the maharani's siblings court allies, the Darklord's forces hunt and execute renegades, adding skulls to the grim Tower of Traitors.

entries.name: Domain Features
entries: Kalakeri is known for the following features:

items.name: Factions in Conflict
items.entry: Two factions claim Kalakeri's Sapphire Throne: the soldiers and loyalists of {@area Maharani Ramya Vasavadan|1c4|x}, and the nobles and merchants who support the younger royal siblings, {@area Arijani and Reeva Vasavadan|1cb|x}.

items.name: Jadurai, a City at War
items.entry: The vast city of Jadurai is Kalakeri's capital, the site of the royal palace, and a battlefield where factions struggle to claim or hold it as a symbol of rule.

items.name: The Backwaters
items.entry: Rare creatures and extraordinary magical plants flourish in the Backwaters, the collective name for the wilderness away from Kalakeri's cities. Scattered refugee groups hide in the wilds and organize dangerous, desperate attacks on the Vasavadan factions.

entries.name: Locations in Kalakeri
entries: Tropical rainforests isolate Kalakeri's communities. While the domain's residents are adept at navigating these wildernesses, strangers often fall prey to the domain's myriad hazards and predators.
The capital of Jadurai is detailed below, and other notable locations are summarized on the {@book Kalakeri Locations table|RHW|2|Kalakeri Locations}.

entries.name: Jadurai
entries: Ancient structures adorned with the artistry of past dynasties stand alongside modern buildings in Kalakeri's capital, Jadurai. The city retains its splendor, but the conflict between the Vasavadan siblings takes an enormous toll as they struggle over the Cerulean Citadel, the nation's capitol, as well as the Sapphire Throne within. Sections of the city lie in ruins.

On the outskirts of Jadurai rises the Tower of Traitors. Rows of skulls taken from the Darklord's enemies fill depressions in this stone tower. Two skulls hold places of special prominence: the original human skulls of Arijani and Reeva Vasavadan. The Darklord's skeletal soldiers build the tower as part of her plan to rid Kalakeri of evil and receive the gods' blessings.

entries.caption: Kalakeri Locations
rows: The forbidden ruins of the accursed city of Bahru lie hidden somewhere in the Ashwagangha Mountains. A powerful evil force lies imprisoned there, attended by monstrous cultists.
The villages of Kattachira and Grantha flank the Bay of Lost Lights, which is named for the {@creature Will-o'-Wisp|XMM|will-o'-wisps} that bob in and above it.
The sea boils around an ancient, submerged ruin called the Drowned Altar. With Kalakeri's upheaval, no one has performed the rites to placate what dwells in the deep.

Statues of helpless animals and terrified refugees alike decorate the quiet, {@creature basilisk|XMM}-infested Forest of the Still. Some of the statues are said to be the remains of heroes from past ages.
The villages of Banyan and Veena are renowned for their remarkable herbalists, but their secrets rely on plants guarded by the titanic crocodiles of the Gharial Rivers.

The eternal {@creature Guardian Naga|XMM|guardian nagas} of the Isle of the Naga have a perfect memory of Kalakeri's lost secrets. Suspicious of strangers, the nagas test the worthiness of any who seek their knowledge.
The {@creature stone giant|XMM} guru Jalendu lives in his sanctuary and claims to know a path to religious enlightenment. Those who fail to prove their devotion to his teachings turn up petrified.
The shadows of people of Meenakara are disappearing, and the shadow-bereft soon sicken and die. Meenakara's people believe the cure lies in the Ashram of Niranjan.
Bandits overtook the village of Neelakurinji, claiming Arijani Vasavadan as their leader. In truth, they serve no one, opportunistically robbing those who fear disobeying the Vasavadan factions.
The giants who occupy the islands known as the Silent Sitars were cursed for offending the gods. Sound is magically suppressed on these isles, stifling the truths within the giants' music.
Temples crown the Vochalam mountains. In one, a deity placed a treasure that grants wishes. In the other is a treasure that turns people into wyverns loyal to Kalakeri's ruler.

entries.name: Ramya Vasavadan
entries: {@i Darklord Perpetually Denied Control}
Are you loyal to your maharani? Or are you yet more rebel skulls to add to the Tower of Traitors?

Ramya Vasavadan desperately fights to claim and hold power over Kalakeri in a brutal cycle of victory and loss with her equally uncompromising siblings. Unlike most Darklords, Ramya struggles to control her domain, leading her to seek allies in opposing her siblings and their treacherous allies.

In her true form, Ramya is an undead horror, but illusions mask her monstrous appearance. Her true nature is revealed by her chilled skin and her reflection, which always appears withered and terrifying.

Although Ramya's siblings, Arijani and Reeva, aren't Darklords of Kalakeri, they motivate the rebellions in Kalakeri and Ramya's torments. This monstrous pair is detailed in "{@area Arijani and Reeva|1cb|x}."

entries.name: Ramya's Tale
entries: The Vasavadan dynasty has ruled Kalakeri since ancient times. Ramya Vasavadan was handpicked by her father to succeed him, but on the bleak day of his death, Ramya's brother, Arijani, declared himself the new maharaja.

Ramya refused to yield to her brother, and with her allies, she captured and prepared to execute him. But Ramya's sister, Reeva, counseled mercy. Instead, Ramya imprisoned Arijani and ascended as Kalakeri's maharani, Queen of Kings.
In the years that followed, Ramya ruled justly. But in the shadows, Reeva plotted with rebels, who eventually freed Arijani and spurred nobles across the nation to revolt.

Regretting her mercy, Ramya sent her army to crush the rebels and mercilessly execute the treacherous. Reeva and Arijani combated Ramya's superior forces with ambushes and deceptions. Once again exploiting Ramya's sympathies, the pair tricked Ramya, invaded the Cerulean Citadel, and executed their sister by strangling. With her final gasps, Ramya cursed her brother and sister, calling them bloodthirsty beasts. Soon after, a terrible monsoon struck Kalakeri, bringing with it the Mists.

The storm lashed Kalakeri for weeks. At its height, Ramya returned from the dead and called on those who had fallen in her service. Loyal in death, a corpse legion joined the revitalized maharani, besieged the Cerulean Citadel, and slaughtered Arijani and Reeva. Ramya then stole their skulls and built them into the foundations of what would become the Tower of Traitors.
Arijani and Reeva's plots didn't end with their lives. They too were reborn, transformed into monstrous reflections of their ravenous desires.
Now in the Land of the Mists, the conflict gripping Kalakeri continues, trapping the nation in an endless war and forcing its people to swear loyalty to monsters.

entries.name: Ramya's Domain
entries: While Ramya is in control of the Sapphire Throne, Kalakeri is altered by Ramya's presence, creating the following effects:

items.name: Closing the Borders
items.entry: While in her domain, Ramya can close or open the borders to Kalakeri at will. While the borders are closed, the Mists surrounding Kalakeri sap the life of those within them. A creature that enters the Mists for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there and isn't a Construct or an Undead must succeed on a {@dc 20} Constitution saving throw or gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

items.name: Everlasting Army
items.entry: A Medium or Small Humanoid slain in combat rises 24 hours later as a {@creature Wight|XMM} under Ramya's control, unless the Humanoid is restored to life or its body is destroyed.

entries: If Ramya dies or if her siblings control the Sapphire Throne, these effects end immediately.

entries.name: Ramya's Lair
entries: The Cerulean Citadel is Kalakeri's royal palace. It derives its name from the blue sandstone used in its construction. Control of the citadel and the Sapphire Throne within regularly passes between Ramya and her siblings.

entries.name: Cerulean Citadel
entries: Bas-reliefs adorn the palace, depicting the history, heroes, and legends of the kingdom. The site is depicted on {@book map 2.20|RHW|2|Map 2.20: Cerulean Citadel} and has the following features:

items.name: Domed Court
items.entry: The central domed court houses the Sapphire Throne. It hosts guardians and treasures gathered by the paranoid Vasavadans. Towers guard the surrounding walls, which are accessible via bridges from the court's upper floors.

items.name: War-Torn Courtyards
items.entry: Within the palace walls lie marble pavilions arranged among baths, ponds, and gardens. Some are intact, but sieges and repeated conflicts have ravaged others.

entries.name: Adventures in Kalakeri
entries: Adventures set in Kalakeri involve suspicion and power struggles. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

entries.title: Evidence of the endless conflict between the Vasavadan siblings lies rotting before the Cerulean Citadel

entries.name: Horror in Kalakeri
entries: Adventures in Kalakeri often include the following elements.

entries.name: Arijani and Reeva
entries: Ramya's rule is jeopardized by her siblings' pursuit of power. Arijani and Reeva Vasavadan promise their followers wealth and freedom, but in truth, their agendas serve only them. Ramya still loves her siblings, making her vulnerable to their promises and lies. So long as Ramya exists, the Dark Powers resurrect Arijani and Reeva as if they had {@creature Ramya Vasavadan|RHW|Ramya's} Darklord Restoration trait.

entries.name: Arijani
entries: Arijani is the charming face of resistance for all who oppose Ramya. Now a {@creature Rakshasa|XMM}, gregarious Arijani has decadent tastes. When in control of the Cerulean Citadel, he hosts parties for Kalakeri's elite that conceal private feasts featuring obscene delicacies and mortal flesh.

entries.name: Reeva
entries: Reeva's adoration of Ramya gave way to jealousy when Ramya eclipsed her in their family's esteem. Now an {@creature Arcanaloth|XMM}, Reeve uses insidious magic from beneath the Cerulean Citadel to summon fiends and manipulate foes from afar. Behind her back, many mockingly call Reeva "Inajira," her charismatic Arijani's opposite.

entries.name: Renown in Kalakeri
entries: You can track the characters' influence with Ramya's loyalist faction and her siblings' rebel faction, using the {@book renown rules|XDMG|2|Renown} from the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|XDMG} to track the characters' standing with these groups. Characters might gain renown with either faction by performing missions such as these:

items: Capturing or killing a rival agent
Planting evidence that a rival committed a crime
Conscripting a powerful ally for the faction
Revealing the location of a rival's hold
Revealing traitors within the faction

entries: Anytime the characters complete a mission for either group, their Renown Score with that group increases by 1, but their Renown Score with the other group decreases by 1 (to a minimum of 0). After the first time characters gain renown with either faction, any faction the characters have a Renown Score of 0 with treat the characters as follows:

items.name: Loyalist Faction
items.entry: Members of this faction are {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} toward the characters. The faction sends troops of {@creature Wight|XMM|Wights} or other Undead to capture the characters weekly.

items.name: Rebel Faction
items.entry: Members of this faction are Hostile toward the characters. The faction sends {@creature Assassin|XMM|Assassins} or {@creature Wraith|XMM|Wraiths} under Reeva's command to kill the characters weekly.

entries: Characters can gain renown with a faction they have a Renown Score of 0 with by presenting proof of an action that undermines the other group. Even if betrayed, the factions seek the party's aid, as they view the characters as useful pawns.

If the characters have a Renown Score of 5+ with either group, their deeds might benefit the faction enough for it to take control of Jadurai and claim the Sapphire Throne. The longer either faction controls the capital, the more extreme its reign becomes, its atrocities escalating week after week. The characters must balance aiding and opposing both factions to maintain equilibrium.

entries.name: Kalakeri Campaign Arc
entries: Campaigns set in Kalakeri typically involve balancing the horrors committed by the Vasavadan factions. A campaign here might follow this outline:

items.name: Levels 1–4
items.entry: Agents from the rebel faction contact the characters, presenting Arijani and Reeva Vasavadan as misunderstood heroes seeking to overthrow a tyrant. They entreat the characters to save the heir of an allied noble family from a compound controlled by Ramya's {@creature Wight|XMM} soldiers and return him to a hidden safe house.

items.name: Levels 5–8
items.entry: The characters meet Arijani and Reeva in their disguises as charming human royals. They thank the characters for their aid and ask them to reclaim the shield of an ancient hero petrified in the Forest of the Still, hoping the shield will be a symbol of their right to rule. As the characters travel to the forest, a messenger delivers a request from {@creature Ramya Vasavadan|RHW|Ramya} to do the same. The characters must face {@creature Basilisk|XMM|Basilisks} and the {@creature Wraith|XMM|Wraiths} of the petrified dead to recover the shield and then decide who to return it to.

items.name: Levels 9–12
items.entry: Arijani and Reeva plot to attack Jadurai to claim the Sapphire Throne and request the characters' help. Ramya's spies have informed her of this plot, and she also seeks the characters' aid. In the ensuing conflict, the characters might strike at any of the Vasavadan siblings.

entries.name: Tower of Traitors
entries: {@i Adventure for Level 11 Characters}

items.name: Situation
items.entry: Arijani and Reeva Vasavadan seek to recover the skulls of their mortal bodies from the Tower of Traitors. They believe the skulls will grant them greater power.

items.name: Hook
items.entry: Arijani or Reeva order the characters to recover two skulls from the Tower of Traitors but don't say who the skulls belong to. They offer to pay the party with 10,000 GP in gemstones and a {@item Candle of Invocation|XDMG}.

entries.name: Encounters
entries: This adventure assumes you are tracking the characters' renown with Kalakeri's loyalist and rebel factions (see "{@area Renown in Kalakeri|1ce|x}" earlier in this section). It includes these encounters.

entries.name: Stolen Skulls
entries: The skulls Arijani and Reeva seek are from their own corpses, which were defiled by Ramya. The skulls are among hundreds in the Tower of Traitors. The pair don't reveal who the skulls belong to but insist they're vital to the rebel faction's allies. To aid in the search, the siblings give the characters two {@item Spell Scroll (Level 2)|XDMG|Spell Scrolls} of {@spell Locate Object|XPHB}. However, they don't provide the characters with enough details for the spell to pinpoint the skulls.

entries.name: Learning About the Skulls
entries: The characters must learn three details about the skulls for them to be familiar enough to the characters that a spell like Locate Object can locate them. Characters can learn details in three ways:

items.name: Research
items.entry: Characters who spend a day searching for rumors or investigating records in one of Kalakeri's settlements learn a detail by succeeding on a {@dc 16} Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check or Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check respectively.

items.name: Renown
items.entry: A character can spend a day questioning non-{@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} members of a faction. After doing so, the characters learn a detail, but their Renown Score with that faction is reduced by 1. The group can do this only once.

items.name: Magic
items.entry: The {@spell Legend Lore|XPHB} spell reveals one detail; each character can use this spell once.

entries: Each time the party learns a detail about the skulls, reveal a portion of the story from the "{@area Ramya's Tale|1c5|x}" section, obscuring the names of the maharani and the traitors until the end.

entries.name: Tower of Traitors
entries: The Tower of Traitors is depicted on {@book map 2.20|RHW|2|Map 2.20: Tower of Traitors} and is guarded by patrols of two {@creature Revenant|XMM|Revenants} and four {@creature Wight|XMM|Wights}. If the characters have a Renown Score of 3+ with the loyalist faction, the undead are {@variantrule Indifferent [Attitude]|XPHB|Indifferent} and tell the party to move along—they fight only if characters linger for more than 5 minutes or remove a skull from the tower. If the characters have less renown with the loyalists, the undead are Hostile. A new patrol appears {@dice 2d6} rounds after a patrol is defeated.

entries.name: Searching the Tower
entries: The tower is composed of thousands of skulls, and the ones the rebels want can be found via magic such as the {@spell Locate Object|XPHB} spell. Once discovered, a skull can be removed in 1 minute using a sharp implement. When both skulls are freed, the hatred lurking in the skulls manifests as two Hostile {@creature Gallows Speaker|RHW|Gallows Speakers} (see {@area chapter 5|3a4|x}).

entries.name: Loyalty or Treachery
entries: The next time the characters rest, Arijani ({@creature Rakshasa|XMM}) appears and demands the skulls. If the characters give him the skulls, he compensates them and the characters' Renown Score with the rebel faction increases by 4. If the characters refuse to give him the skulls, Arijani fights them. The party's Renown Score with the rebels decreases by 4 if Arijani is slain. Soon after, Ramya invites the characters to discuss future plans.
Regardless of how the adventure concludes, each character earns a bonus 4,100 XP.

entries.name: Kartakass
entries: {@i Domain of Tarnished Dreams}

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: {@area Harkon Lukas|1dd|x}

items.name: Adventure Genres:
items.entry: {@area Dark fantasy|360|x} and {@area gothic horror|37b|x}

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Hidden identities, dangerous performances, exploitative ambitions, werewolves

items.name: Mist Talismans:
items.entry: Handbill advertising remarkable performances, rustic woodcut of hunting wolves, wolf's tooth necklace

items.name: Aligned Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Swashbuckler

items.name: Opposed Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Beast

entries: Life in Kartakass feels staged and surreal, as every plant, beast, and person strives to prove their greatness. Trees and flowers erupt into bloom; songbirds sing themselves hoarse; and every local knows that dreams, fame, and immortal adulation are theirs should they prove worthy. And where talent and expertise fail, obsession and duplicity reign, leading to cycles of triumph, betrayal, and despair. Predators flourish in this land of unrealistic hopes, consuming passions, and vicious secrets.

With each full moon, the truth of Kartakass is exposed, as the domain's many werewolves and other lycanthropes indulge their hunger for dominance and blood. After the full moon passes, the idyllic facade returns. Locals don't speak of why most bar their doors and shutter their windows on the nights of the full moon, but they acknowledge that the path to greatness is messy.

entries.name: Domain Features
entries: Kartakass is known for the following features:

items.name: False Idylls
items.entry: Kartakass is supernaturally picturesque and outwardly serene. Every season is strikingly beautiful, communities appear idyllic, and many animals are affected as if by the {@spell Awaken|XPHB} spell. This makes deceptions and violence all the more stark.

items.name: Meistersingers
items.entry: Kartakass has no unified government. Each settlement is governed by a meistersinger, the acknowledged mayor and embodiment of local art. Meistersingers maintain creative rivalries with one another largely for show.

items.name: Theatricality
items.entry: Settlements pride themselves on performative traditions, such as distinctive dress, traditional dances, or song stylings.

items.name: Wolves and Werewolves
items.entry: Wolves roam the land freely, and {@creature Werewolf|XMM|Werewolves} lurk in the domain's settlements. Residents seldom venture outside during the full moon, fearing the lycanthropes' hunger.

entries.name: Locations in Kartakass
entries: Kartakass is a land of rolling hills, light forests, clear lakes, and pastoral farmlands. Communities are welcoming and host frequent festivals. The settlements of Harmonia and Skald are detailed below, and other notable locations are summarized on the {@book Kartakass Locations table|RHW|2|Kartakass Locations}.

entries.name: Harmonia
entries: Artists vie to perform at the town of Harmonia's popular Crystal Club, a members-only venue inside a cave that features a stage constructed from a massive geode.
