The club features dramatic decor and discerning clientele. Rumors say that performers who fail to impress are exiled. The bloodier truth is that the club restricts membership to lycanthropes. Talented artists gain the curse and join their ranks, while the mediocre get devoured.

entries.name: Skald
entries: The largest community in Kartakass, Skald is a bustling hub of commerce and creativity. The lure of overnight celebrity and fawning crowds attracts the ambitious. Yet behind the facade of creativity and freethinking, business owners and celebrities prey on creativity, while performers struggle viciously over paltry opportunities. Vice, criminality, dark bargains, and supernatural predators flourish amid Skald's many stages and frequent festivals. Among Skald's most popular venues is the Old Kartakan Inn and Taverna, owned by the performer and Darklord Harkon Lukas.

entries.caption: Kartakass Locations
rows: Performers flock to a bardic college atop the Argent Falls. Caverns behind the falls bear rich silver deposits but are haunted by {@creature Banshee|XMM|Banshees}—the souls of failed performers.
Violent goblins lurk amid mazelike canyons called the Catacombs. Here, the goblin gladiator Nym Pymplee hosts fighting tournaments, with victors winning treasures from distant domains.
The Cavea Plains' acoustics carry voices over great distances. Any speech above a whisper might attract the attention of {@creature Wolf|XMM|wolves}.
On the night of a full moon, a tarokka-reading wolf appears atop Cesca's Vantage. She produces crimson cards from any corpse she is offered, and her readings are never wrong.
A plague has taken root in Cortton. The town's residents cake heavy makeup and garish blush on their faces, desperate to convince themselves and others that they're uninfected.
Every "resident" of Emherst is an in-character improvisational actor. They live in the Understage, an underground network of backstages, and they never interrupt performances.
Undead goblinoids often emerge from the Goblyn Graves, the former lair of Radaga, an undead witch said to possess powerful magic treasures.
This fun house of visual illusions is run by Jack Bequick: a vampire cursed with eternal invisibility and a twisted sense of humor.
Several dramatic-effects artists call the fishing village of Medria home. Colorful explosions and wandering puppets are commonplace.
The Mists often surge over Lake Variatio to deposit newcomers at the rickety quay known as Point Hallucination, where traveling merchants and performers eagerly greet them.
At twilight, a sourceless chorus drifts through these woods. Those who don't sing along risk losing their lives, while those who discover the last verse discover a hidden sanctuary.

entries.name: Harkon Lukas
entries: {@i Darklord Who Preys on Talent}
I must say, your skill is unlike any I've ever seen. The name's Harkon. I'm sure you've heard of me.

The charming werewolf Harkon Lukas manipulates those around him to satisfy his narcissistic needs for adulation and control. Endlessly seeking fame, Harkon travels widely, earning applause and accolades. Yet, whenever he returns to a town he's performed in, he finds he's been forgotten. Lukas believes he needs just the right song or inspiration to make himself unforgettable. To that end, he searches for talented protégés, cultivating their skills, then stealing their work. He ensures their loyalty by gifting them copies of his cursed wolf-tooth necklace, {@item Harkon's Bite|RHW}, or by transforming them into werewolves. He then manipulates these victims into believing only he will accept them.

Harkon wears a signature wide-brimmed hat and wolf-tooth necklace, and he carries a violin he calls Bleeding Heart. He uses flattery to charm those he takes an interest in. A skilled artist who declines the offer to become his protégé often earns his obsession. The Darklord might go to any extreme to claim such performers' talents as his own.

entries.name: Harkon's Tale
entries: Harkon Lukas weaves numerous self-aggrandizing tales of his origins. Among them, Harkon tells of being spurned for his ambitions by the werewolves that raised him. Through talent and sacrifice, he became a legendary performer, and people flocked to bask in his remarkable presence.

Eventually, the rulers of Harkon's homeland realized the threat posed by Harkon's growing fame. Officials tried to imprison him for invented crimes, but Harkon's fans defended him. When royal agents came for him, Harkon feigned his death, and his followers revolted. When outraged masses bearing Harkon's coffin besieged the royal palace, Harkon ended his ploy. He burst from his crimson coffin in wolf form and devoured the land's monarch. Drenched in blood, the "resurrected" Harkon returned to his human form and donned the monarch's crown amid the cheers of his deluded followers. Yet before Harkon's reign could begin, the Mists rose around him. When they cleared, Harkon found himself in an unfamiliar land called Kartakass.

entries.name: Harkon's Domain
entries: Kartakass is altered by Harkon's presence, creating the following effects:

items.name: Beast Spies
items.entry: From dawn until dusk, Tiny Beasts in the domain magically gain the ability to understand Common and can communicate telepathically with other Beasts and Monstrosities.

items.name: Children of the Night
items.entry: Harkon exerts influence over the animals of his domain. From dusk until dawn, Medium or smaller Beasts within 1 miles of Harkon have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition. If Harkon pleases, these animals become {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to creatures other than Harkon and his allies.

items.name: Closing the Borders
items.entry: While in his domain, Harkon can close or open the borders to Kartakass at will. While the borders are closed, a strange melody fills the Mists surrounding Kartakass. A creature that enters the Mists for the first time on a turn or starts its turn in them must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom saving throw, or it has the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition for {@dice 1d6} hours, then awakes within sight of the town of Harmonia in Kartakass. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

entries: If Harkon dies, these effects end immediately.

entries.name: Harkon's Lair
entries: The Darklord owns a tavern in Skald called the Old Kartakan Inn and Taverna. The inn occupies a small island on the Sing Song River. Nightly, Harkon Lukas or artists under his tutelage perform there.

entries.name: Old Kartakan Inn and Taverna
entries: Welcoming and rustic, the Old Kartakan features subtle wolf-themed decor. The site is depicted on {@book map 2.22|RHW|2|Map 2.22: Old Kartakan Inn and Taverna} and has the following features:

items.name: Bedrooms
items.entry: The inn's rooms are insulated to block the sounds of performances and maulings alike.

items.name: Harkon Lukas's Room
items.entry: Several secret doors and a trapdoor lead to Harkon's tower bedroom, where he hides evidence of his own uninspired music and trophies from past victims.

items.name: Secret Doors and Chambers
items.entry: The inn is riddled with secret spaces to spy into other chambers, hide bodies, or indulge in bestial meals.

items.name: Taproom
items.entry: Among the drinks the taverna offers is Meekulbrau, a blood-red berry wine that soothes the throat and improves vocal performances.

entries.name: Adventures in Kartakass
entries: Adventures set in Kartakass involve art, werewolves, and sinister ambitions. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

entries.title: Locals celebrate during a festival in Skald, unaware of the predators lurking among them

entries.name: Horror in Kartakass
entries: Kartakan adventures often include the following elements.

entries.name: Harkon's Curse
entries: Harkon Lukas can create a copy of his signature necklace once per month on the night of the full moon. This cursed copy is called {@i Harkon's Bite}.

entries.name: Harkon's Bite
entries: Wondrous Item, Uncommon (Requires Attunement by a Humanoid)
A dire wolf's tooth dangles from this simple cord necklace. You gain a +1 bonus to ability checks and saving throws while you wear this necklace.

entries.name: Curse
entries: This necklace is cursed. Attuning to the necklace curses you; this curse can't be removed until {@creature Harkon Lukas|RHW} dies. As long as you remain cursed, you become a {@creature Werewolf|XMM} serving Harkon Lukas under the DM's control during the night of a full moon.

entries.name: Harkon's Manipulations
entries: Harkon Lukas seeks to win the attention and trust of artistic and charismatic individuals. This might include characters—especially bards—or their allies. Harkon might do this to further a grand plot, but he also does so to assuage his perpetually bruised ego and satisfy his need for control. Harkon also seeks supernatural forces he can exploit, including magic items or beings with performative powers—such as an {@item Instrument of the Bards|XDMG}, {@creature Harpy|XMM|harpies}, or {@creature Satyr|XMM|satyrs}.
Harkon presents himself as a charming admirer or mentor seeking to aid others. He tries to manipulate those he pursues and earn their trust by encouraging them to do the following:

items: Collaborate in creating a new song or show
Compete in performances against the domain's meistersingers
Join him for tutoring or training exercises
Protect him from a jealous former student
Find a performer who stole one of his songs
Travel with him on a tour of other towns

entries: Harkon's requests eventually turn criminal or violent. He might then blackmail those who aided him or threaten their reputations, or he might isolate them by gifting them {@item Harkon's Bite|RHW}. Harkon continues such manipulations until it bores him, at which point he consumes his prey.

entries.name: The Lukas Pack
entries: An ever-changing entourage of followers and victims surrounds Harkon Lukas, but three individuals are constants in the Darklord's life:

items.name: Akriel Lukas
items.entry: Harkon's eldest daughter, Akriel (Medium, Chaotic Evil {@creature Performer Legend|XMM}), idolizes Harkon and pursues her own performing career. The Dark Powers don't torment Akriel as they do her father, and her reputation steadily grows—much to her father's resentment. Akriel is a human and isn't a lycanthrope.

items.name: Casimir Lukas
items.entry: Harkon's eldest son, Casimir (Medium, Chaotic Neutral {@creature Spy Master|XMM}), is seeking his place in the world. He wants nothing to do with Harkon, preferring to travel, even to other domains. Whether at the whims of his father or the Mists, Casimir always finds his way back to Skald. Casimir is a human and isn't a lycanthrope.

items.name: Haldrake Moonbaun
items.entry: Harkon's oldest ally, Haldrake (Medium {@creature Werewolf|XMM}) appears as a heavily tattooed human. He serves as the Old Kartakan Inn and Taverna mixologist and head bouncer. When Harkon isn't in Skald, he ensures things at the taverna run smoothly. Haldrake aids Harkon in his plots and knows many of the Darklord's secrets, so Harkon compensates him well.

entries.name: Traces of Terror
entries: Kartakass's charm masks its horrors. Roll on or choose a result from the Kartakass Sights table to inspire unsettling scenes in the domain.

entries.caption: Kartakass Sights
rows: Awakened sheep plotting against a shepherd.
A cheery home that shows signs of slaughter.
Folk carvings of youths dragged off by wolves.
Lupine paw prints in a tavern or theater.
A songbird singing until its heart stops.

entries.name: Kartakass Campaign Arc
entries: A campaign in Kartakass might follow this outline:

items.name: Levels 1–2
items.entry: The characters meet Maria, a human woman whose idealistic son, Frantosh, ran away to Harmonia to become a performer. She gives the characters an Uncommon {@item Instrument of the Bards|XDMG} and will allow them to keep it if they return Frantosh home safely. Along the way to Harmonia, the characters are plagued by {@creature Wolf|XMM|Wolves} and people-eating {@filter Goblins|bestiary|search=goblin}.

items.name: Levels 3–6
items.entry: In Harmonia, the characters find Frantosh has begun performing with a skilled bard named {@creature Harkon Lukas|RHW}. Harkon encourages the characters to help Frantosh follow his dreams. To help Frantosh, Harkon asks the characters to fetch voice-enchanting waters from a mystical silver spring near Argent Falls Conservatory. The spring is guarded by dead performers risen as {@creature Banshee|XMM|Banshees} and a covetous {@creature Young Copper Dragon|XMM}.

items.name: Levels 7–8
items.entry: On returning to Harmonia, the characters find Frantosh went to Skald with Harkon. There, Frantosh is performing nightly with Harkon at the Old Kartakan Inn and Taverna. Frantosh—now wearing a wolf-tooth necklace Harkon gave him—refuses to leave with the characters until after his first solo performance. The "Hungry for More" adventure below details this performance. After this Harkon might resent the characters or start obsessing over one of them.

entries.name: Hungry for More
entries: {@i Adventure for Level 7 Characters}

items.name: Situation
items.entry: The singer Frantosh has fallen under the sway of Harkon Lukas. He refuses to return to his family until after he performs at the Old Kartakan Inn and Taverna on the night of the full moon.

items.name: Hook
items.entry: Frantosh's mother, Maria, offers the party an Uncommon {@item Instrument of the Bards|XDMG} if they return her son.

images.title: Map 2.22: Old Kartakan Inn and Taverna

entries.name: Encounters
entries: This adventure uses map 2.22 and includes these encounters.

entries.name: Frantosh's Debut
entries: Frantosh (Medium {@creature Performer|XMM}) is performing on his own for the first time tonight. If the characters seek him out and mention his mother, he agrees to leave with them after his performance—it ends around midnight. If the characters try to abduct Frantosh, three {@creature Tough Boss|XMM|Tough Bosses} (or more) in Harkon Lukas's service intervene.

entries.name: Full-Moon
entries: Mention that the performance is on first night of the full moon only if a character asks.

entries.name: The Old Kartakan
entries: The taverna is a popular spot in Skald. Crowds of {@creature Commoner|XMM|Commoners} fill the first floor of the Old Kartakan. {@creature Harkon Lukas|RHW} (detailed earlier in {@area this section|1dd|x}) is here, but he avoids combat unless otherwise mentioned.

entries.name: The Show
entries: During his 1-hour performance, Frantosh makes a {@dc 15} Charisma ({@skill Performance|XPHB}) check every 15 minutes. Characters can aid him with the {@action Help|XPHB} action or in other ways. Track how many of these checks Frantosh succeeds on. During the performance, these events occur:

items.name: Heckler
items.entry: A drunk {@b Commoner} heckles Frantosh. If he's not silenced within 3 rounds, Frantosh has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on his next ability check.

items.name: Brawl
items.entry: A fight breaks out between three {@creature Bandit|XMM|Bandits}. After the first round of combat, a {@b Bandit} with bad aim targets Frantosh with its Light Crossbow action each round. If Frantosh is struck, he automatically fails his next ability check.

items.name: Howl
items.entry: A wolf howls outside, distracting the audience. The characters have 1 round to direct attention back to Frantosh, perhaps with flashy magic or by making a {@dc 18} Charisma ({@skill Performance|XPHB}) check. If they don't, Frantosh has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on his next ability check.

entries.name: After the Show
entries: After the show, the characters see Frantosh vanish through a secret door. The next events take place in the inn's hidden areas.

If Frantosh succeeded on three or more checks during the show, Harkon Lukas seeks to keep Frantosh. He won't fight the characters, but he uses the necklace Frantosh wears to transform the singer into a {@creature Werewolf|XMM}. Frantosh and four more {@b Werewolf} servants of Harkon assault the characters.

If Frantosh didn't succeed on three or more checks during the show, Harkon has grown bored with Frantosh. The characters find Frantosh {@status Bloodied|XPHB} and being attacked by four {@b Werewolves}.

entries.name: Conclusion
entries: If the characters escape the Old Kartakan with Frantosh (potentially now a {@b Werewolf}), each earns a bonus 1,700 XP. Even if forced into battle, Harkon Lukas won't kill the characters—they've intrigued the Darklord, and he might appear to toy with them later.

entries.name: Lamordia
entries: {@i Domain of Snow and Stitched Flesh}

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: {@area Viktra Mordenheim|1f9|x}

items.name: Adventure Genres:
items.entry: {@area Body horror|34e|x} and {@area gothic horror|37b|x}

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Amoral science, bizarre constructs, frigid wilderness, mutagenic radiation

items.name: Mist Talismans:
items.entry: Animated digits, glowing minerals, preserved humanoid limb

items.name: Aligned Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Transmuter

items.name: Opposed Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Marionette

entries: A domain of amoral science, Lamordia shudders amid the icy environment and terror of its residents' creations. Revolutions of steam and steel spur Lamordia's people into frenzies of amoral invention. From industrialized communities and the iron walls of Ludendorf, the domain's largest city, flow streams of scrap and chemical poisons that taint the land and transform creatures into horrors. Yet, the domain's reckless scientists don't pursue just mechanized inventions—the greatest goal is reinvention of life itself, transmuting people into ideals and unlocking generations of potential. In the name of such ambitions, the depraved reshape life and unleash abominations. And no aspirations have unleashed greater evils than those of the domain's Darklord, {@creature Viktra Mordenheim|RHW|Dr. Viktra Mordenheim}.

entries.name: Domain Features
entries: Lamordia is known for the following features:

items.name: Frigid Environs
items.entry: Lamordia is a frigid land of bleak mountains, frozen swaps, and icy seas. Few voluntarily trek into its wilderness, fearing freak blizzards and starving beasts.

items.name: Mutated Creatures
items.entry: Creatures warped by alchemy roam Lamordia. The most infamous are the monstrous "whales" of the Sea of Secrets—violent, unnatural leviathans, no two of which are alike.

items.name: Rational Culture
items.entry: The people of Lamordia are direct and skeptical of superstition. They revere scientific innovation—as it's all that stands between them and a life of frigid starvation. Magic is acknowledged but widely considered backward.

items.name: Steam Technology
items.entry: Steam power driven by boiling sewers fuels clockwork inventions and massive cranes in the city of Ludendorf.

entries.name: Locations in Lamordia
entries: Lamordia is a frigid land. In the north, the Sea of Secrets is choked with ice, while the mountains known as the Sleeping Beast are never without snow. The cities of Ludendorf and Neufurchtenburg are detailed below, and other notable locations are summarized on the {@book Lamordia Locations table|RHW|2|Lamordia Locations}.

entries.name: Ludendorf
entries: The city of Ludendorf is a morass of steam-powered factories operating at the behest of morally bankrupt barons. Boiling sewers and steam tunnels thread the city's foundations, heating cramped tenements and powering cranes serving the city's fleet of ironclad ships. These ice-breaking, whaling vessels dare the Sea of Secrets to bring back massive beasts that provide vital food and fuel for the city.

At the city's heart, the unscrupulous Ludendorf University conducts risky investigations into such fields as alchemical combustion, automated ingenuity, chemical sentience, and speculative anatomy. The university continually needs new subjects for experiments and hires volunteers or pays individuals for "flesh rights"—ownership of their cadavers once they die. A secret society within Ludendorf University idolizes Viktra and follows the reckless paths laid by her work.

entries.name: Neufurchtenburg
entries: The smoking refineries of Neufurchtenburg process minerals harvested from the Sleeping Beast. These rare, little-understood elements are then shipped to Ludendorf's factories. Radiation-warped monsters from the nearby bogs frequently attack, disrupting operations, but the town's people use hulking clockwork machines, cutting-edge firearms, and destructive alchemy to fend off incursions.

entries.caption: Lamordia Locations
rows: Lamordia's ancient druidic peoples buried their dead in the Chokingmere. Prospectors seeking pockets of rare gas in this swamp disturb forgotten ritual sites and bog mummy burial sites.
Ludendorf dumps its waste and scrap metal in the Forest of Rust. Creatures of living iron, {@creature Rust Monster|XMM|Rust Monsters}, and hungry beasts prowl the wooded junk heaps.
Lamordians know the Isles of Agony as freezing death traps. At the hidden sanctuary of Hope's Heart, Mordenheim's runaway creation, {@area Elise|200|x}, shelters all manner of outcasts.
The waters of the Boiling River are poisoned by mining. Those who listen at Lorelei Falls claim to hear screaming, as though the water were burning someone behind the falls.

Baron Rudolph von Aubrecker, the reclusive ruler of Lamordia, sends proclamations to his people from the remote castle Schloss Aubrecker to hide the fact that he's a {@creature Brain in a Jar|RHW} (see {@area chapter 5|399|x}).
A former academic, Shoshan Cutter isolates her family at her ancestral home, Schloss Cutter. She supports any who fight against greed and amorality cloaked as innovation.
Dr. Viktra Mordenheim's home, Schloss Mordenheim, is an imposing castle that hosts the doctor's laboratory and servitor creations. She frequently needs new lab assistants.
Radioactive minerals vein this jagged mountain range. Legends claim the range is a single titanic, sleeping monster and that its blood and bones are the source of rare minerals.
Tallman's Wood is the storied home of Tallman, a being that "wears" a too-tall, humanlike body. Tallman kidnaps those who sleep in its wood but also knows much of other lands.
A theater overlooks the fishing village of Whale End. Concrete encloses every entrance to the theater, and the sealed main entry bears a message: "Forgive me, all those I love."
Tales say the sea answers questions posed to it through a fissure in the ice called Winter's Mouth. Those who fall into the waters here return plagued by visions of the drowned.

entries.name: Viktra Mordenheim
entries: {@i Darklord Who Pursues Perfection}
Ah. You're conscious. Good. Test your limbs, and get accustomed to your new body.

From her laboratory at Schloss Mordenheim, Dr. Viktra Mordenheim uses her scientific genius to create horrors and wonders. All manner of undead and constructs march from her laboratory, but such flawed creations don't satisfy Mordenheim. Rather, she seeks to re-create or reclaim her greatest creation, the Unbreakable Heart, a miraculous device that grants life to Mordenheim's elusive reanimated victim, Elise.

entries.name: Viktra's Tale
entries: From an early age, Viktra Mordenheim sought to understand the complexities of anatomy. She pursued medicine not to aid patients but to reveal the secrets of life. Eventually, her research bent toward reanimating dead flesh and improving humanoid bodies. When Viktra began employing thieves to procure specimens for her tests, she met Elise, a beautiful resurrectionist whose spontaneity entranced the methodical surgeon. Viktra was smitten, and with Elise she discovered there might be more to life than chemical reactions.

When Elise started exhibiting signs of an incurable disease, Viktra hastened her experiments. On the doctor's operating table, victims were killed, returned to life, and died again as Viktra sought a breakthrough. When finally Elise passed away, Viktra refused to accept failure. The doctor worked feverishly crafting her masterpiece, an artificial organ she called the Unbreakable Heart. But as she fitted the device into Elise's corpse, constables burst into the lab and accused the doctor of numerous murders. As Viktra struggled against arrest, smoking chemicals, arcing electricity, and a strange mist filled the laboratory.

When the chaos cleared, Viktra awoke in Lamordia, an unfamiliar land where her genius was celebrated. Elise and the Unbreakable Heart were nowhere to be found, but the doctor soon heard rumors of a wondrous and terrifying figure who wandered the icy realm and whose chest glowed as though her heart were the sun. Ever since, Viktra has continued her experiments, striving to re-create her successes but failing every time. Between disappointments she searches for whatever Elise has become—and with her, the miracle of the Unbreakable Heart.

entries.name: Viktra's Domain
entries: Lamordia is altered by Viktra's presence, creating the following effects:

items.name: Air of Obsession
items.entry: When a creature fails a Constitution saving throw to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}, that creature takes 3 ({@dice 1d6}) Psychic damage.

items.name: Closing the Borders
items.entry: While in her domain, Viktra can close or open the borders to Lamordia. While the borders are closed, lightning storms roll through the Mists surrounding Lamordia. A creature that enters the Mists for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there makes a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 4d6} Lightning damage on a failed save and half as much on a successful one. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

entries: If Viktra dies, these effects end immediately.

entries.name: Viktra's Lair
entries: Schloss Mordenheim is a castle that stands amid dramatic cliffs overlooking the sea. Travelers from Ludendorf reach Schloss Mordenheim by crossing the ice and following a treacherous road.

entries.name: Schloss Mordenheim
entries: Schloss Mordenheim holds dozens of rooms, but most are empty or have furnishings pushed aside to make room for scientific supplies. Prominent locations from this site are depicted on map 2.24 and have the following features:

items.name: Auxiliary Workshops
items.entry: Viktra's assistants toil in workshops where they craft tools and devices to the doctor's exacting specifications.

items.name: Disused Chambers
items.entry: Rooms originally constructed for relaxation serve as storage rooms, act as extra laboratories, or sit eerily empty. A dining hall half filled with crates is set for a single diner, but it's rarely used.

items.name: Gate and Entry
items.entry: A stone bridge crosses a chasm leading to the castle's keep. Stairs flanked by grim statuary lead to the keep's entrance.

items.name: Laboratory Tower
items.entry: A heavily renovated turret holds Viktra's laboratory. Mechanisms allow the tower's roof to part and its operating table to rise 30 feet, exposing subjects to lightning during the island's frequent storms.

entries.name: Adventures in Lamordia
entries: Lamordia provides a stage for adventures featuring amoral science and altered or resurrected beings. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

entries.title: Schloss Mordenheim hosts the laboratories and obscene experiments of Viktra Mordenheim

entries.name: Horror in Lamordia
entries: Adventures in Lamordia often include the following elements.

entries.name: Elise von Brandthofen
entries: Elise knows she died yet finds herself alive again. She is now a {@creature Mordenheim's Monster|RHW} (see {@area chapter 5|3ad|x}), and her life is sustained by the Unbreakable Heart (see the following section). Elise is learning to accept her new life but avoids Lamordia's people, who label her as an outcast and a monster. She also knows that Viktra Mordenheim seeks to recover the Unbreakable Heart. The doctor's actions—as well as the mercenaries she sends to abduct Elise—have shattered the pair's bond. Now Elise hides at a secret sanctuary in the {@area Isles of Agony|205|x} called Hope's Heart. Here, she shelters other victims and creations of Viktra, showing them the esteem their creator denies them. Those in Elise's care have taken to calling themselves the Children (detailed below).

entries.name: Mordenheim's Masterpiece
entries: The Unbreakable Heart is a nonmagical scientific wonder. Despite being the creator of the Unbreakable Heart, Viktra can't re-create it and is obsessed with reclaiming it and rediscovering its secrets.

entries.name: Unbreakable Heart
entries: This heart-shaped, clockwork device installs itself, fastening into place if positioned where a creature's heart used to be. A creature that contains the device is affected as if by the {@spell Raise Dead|XPHB} spell. After this, the creature is immune to disease, ceases to age, and doesn't die of old age. The Unbreakable Heart glows, shedding {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 10 feet. While inside a creature, the device causes the creature to shed Dim Light in a 10-foot radius.

entries.name: The Children
entries: The Children are discarded creations of Dr. Viktra Mordenheim who have found shelter with Elise. Composed a few dozen Constructs and chemically animated Undead, the Children follow Elise's lead and seek to live peacefully away from those who would misunderstand and harm them.

The exception to this is Adam, a {@creature Mordenheim's Monster|RHW} who recently joined the Children. Adam was created by Viktra to capture Elise, but Elise convinced him to join her instead. Unlike Elise, Adam was created from disparate parts and knows no previous life. Although Elise seeks to teach Adam humanity, he shows little respect for the lives of more fragile beings. Moreover, Adam is fascinated by Mordenheim's work and quietly seeks ways to create life that will serve him.

entries.title: A desperate crew of Ludendorf whalers runs afoul of Rahab, a fleshless leviathan that hunts the Sea of Secrets

entries.name: Lamordia Campaign Arc
entries: Campaigns set in Lamordia often make the characters subjects or accomplices to Viktra's schemes. A campaign here might follow this outline:

items.name: Levels 1–2
items.entry: A dean of Ludendorf University hires the characters to escort the coach of students traveling to an assistantship at Schloss Mordenheim. Along the way, they are plagued by mismatched {@creature Homunculus|XMM|Homunculi} that sabotage their travels. These are members of the Children, seeking to stymie folk they take for Mordenheim's allies.

items.name: Levels 3–6
items.entry: At Schloss Mordenheim, Viktra Mordenheim hires the character to recover her invention, the Unbreakable Heart. She explains it was stolen by a monster that dwells on the Isles of Agony. Viktra wishes the characters to bring back the heart and the monster. The characters face dangers in the wilds, but the quest culminates in the "Isles of Agony" adventure (see below).

entries: The conclusion of the campaign depends on whether the characters aid Viktra or Elise. When they return to Schloss Mordenheim, events might play out in the following ways:

items.name: Levels 7–9 (Aiding Viktra)
items.entry: If the characters aid Dr. Mordenheim, the doctor seeks to remove the Unbreakable Heart from Elise or test it by reanimating a new being. This results in a dramatic operation where an enraged {@creature Mordenheim's Monster|RHW} (see {@area chapter 5|3ad|x}) breaks free and rampages through the castle.

items.name: Levels 7–9 (Aiding Elise)
items.entry: If the characters aid Elise, the characters must free innocent victims of the doctor from the castle and prevent Dr. Mordenheim from animating new terrors by destroying her laboratory. Ultimately the characters must face {@creature Viktra Mordenheim|RHW} (detailed earlier in {@area this section|1f9|x}) and two {@creature Flesh Golem|XMM|Flesh Golems} made from the assistants the characters brought to the castle.

entries: The campaign might continue with the characters continuing to aid or being targeted by the Children.

entries.name: Isles of Agony
entries: {@i Adventure for Level 5 Characters}

items.name: Situation
items.entry: The characters must recover the Unbreakable Heart (detailed earlier in {@area this section|202|x}). They don't know it's inside the outcast Elise.

items.name: Hook
items.entry: Viktra hires the characters to recover the Unbreakable Heart, which was stolen by a monster. They are offered 2,500 GP, plus a 1,000 GP bonus if they capture the monster.

entries.name: Encounters
entries: The adventure begins when the characters reach the Isles of Agony and consists of these encounters.

entries.name: Shipwreck
entries: Soon after reaching the icy isles, the characters find six human survivors of a wrecked whaling ship. These sailors ({@creature Pirate|XMM|Pirates}) are led by Mate Herzogin, and their captain was carried off by a half-human, half-machine monster. The survivors seek help returning home but want to recover their captain's corpse (and their pay, which he carried).

entries.name: The Monster
entries: The monster the survivors fear is one of the Children, a {@creature Flesh Golem|XMM} named Kartad. He's peaceful, but the survivors' captain, Hans Giesbrecht (Medium {@creature Pirate Captain|XMM}), attacked him on sight. Kartad knocked out Hans, panicked, picked him up, and fled to Hope's Heart.

entries.name: Track of the Monster
entries: Characters who succeed on a {@dc 12} Wisdom ({@skill Survival|XPHB}) check find and can follow Kartad's tracks to Hope's Heart. If the characters fail, a survivor points out the path. Roll on or choose results from the following table for three encounters that happen on the way (rerolling duplicates).

rows: Six {@creature Dire Wolf|XMM|Dire Wolves} pursue a {@creature Homunculus|XMM}. The homunculus flees as soon as possible.
{@dice 1d8} {@creature Zombie|XMM|Zombies} animated by mechanical apparatuses stare into the sky. They are Constructs and don't react, even if harmed.
A {@creature Primeval Owlbear|XMM} made of mismatched parts forages for food; it is a Construct. It is {@variantrule Indifferent [Attitude]|XPHB|Indifferent} to the characters.
A {@creature Troll|XMM} hunts alongside a {@creature Winter Wolf|XMM}.

entries.name: Hope's Heart
entries: Hope's Heart is a hidden canyon home to numerous harmless Constructs that is accessed via a passage in an abandoned cabin. When the characters approach, Elise appears on the cliffs 20 feet above and demands to know their business. Elise is a {@creature Mordenheim's Monster|RHW} (see {@area chapter 5|3ad|x}). The Unbreakable Heart glows from her chest.

entries.name: Fighting Elise
entries: Elise attacks to prevent entry to the cabin and to defend herself. She avoids killing and won't pursue those who flee. If the characters attack, they'll likely have to make multiple attempts to wear down Elise or use trickery to defeat her.

entries.name: Negotiating with Elise
entries: Elise is {@variantrule Indifferent [Attitude]|XPHB|Indifferent} to the characters or {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} if they harmed any Constructs on the isle. She's wary of those who working for Viktra and can share the details described in "{@area Viktra's Tale|1fa|x}." If a character explains they're seeking the missing captain, she brings forth Hans and Kartad. Kartad has nursed Hans back to health; he apologizes and releases the bewildered captain.

entries.name: The Crew
entries: The six survivors arrive to see the characters speaking with Kartad. They overreact on seeing the "monster" and initiate combat. Kartad panics and defends himself. Hans has 10 Hit Points but is too weak to command his crew or fight.

entries.name: Aftermath
entries: If the characters fight alongside the crew, Kartad and Elise defend themselves but flee after 3 rounds or if anyone is killed. Elise leads her followers to hide elsewhere on the island. The sailors thank the characters and offer to help them rally aid in Ludendorf to return and hunt the monsters.

If the characters deescalate the conflict or defend Kartad, Elise's attitude toward them improves. She won't give them the Unbreakable Heart or return to Schloss Mordenheim. Rather, she tries to convince the characters to see Viktra for the villain she is. She entreats them to return to the castle, save captive members of the Children, and thwart the doctor, as detailed in "{@area Lamordia Campaign Arc|204|x}." In return, Elise offers a trove of 4,000 GP and an {@item Immovable Rod|XDMG}.
Characters who defeat or choose to aid Elise earn a bonus 1,100 XP.

entries.name: Mordent
entries: {@i Domain of the Haunted}

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: {@area Wilfred Godefroy|214|x}

items.name: Adventure Genre:
items.entry: {@area Ghost stories|372|x}

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Ancestral curses, haunted mansions, mist-shrouded moors, vengeful spirits

items.name: Mist Talismans:
items.entry: Broken jewelry, death mask, faded love letter, family portrait

items.name: Aligned Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Miser

items.name: Opposed Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Horseman

entries: In Mordent, the dead don't rest. Here, farmers and fishers toil in scattered villages, cleaving to stoic, hardworking traditions. In the manors of landed, aristocratic families, the bearers of noble names view themselves as conservators of the land and of stifling reputations. Tradition, memory, and expectation haunt the lives of Mordent's people, and when they die, nothing changes.

Little known to the living, the dead in Mordent rise as spirits, and for most—knowing no other ways—their work continues. Abandoned estates hide spectral generations of frustrated nobles and their downtrodden servants. While some spirits drift idly or deteriorate to mere impressions of their past selves, others greedily cling to their former possessions and ambitions. Some pernicious spirits become infamous hauntings, while others—such as the domain's Darklord, the ghostly {@creature Wilfred Godefroy|RHW|Lord Wilfred Godefroy}—seek to dominate the living and dead.

entries.name: Domain Features
entries: Mordent is known for the following features:

items.name: Lingering Spirits
items.entry: All manner of spectral undead and other hauntings lurk in Mordent. Ghost stories and rumors of haunted sites are common.

items.name: Local Nobility
items.entry: Mordent is governed by town mayors and local lords. Few nobles remain, and some have only decrepit manors left to their names.

items.name: Old Faiths
items.entry: People casually worship vague deities in Mordent, but evidence of forgotten druidic traditions lies scattered around the wilds.

items.name: Spiritualism
items.entry: Attempting to communicate with the dead is a popular pursuit for investigators, bored nobles, and charlatans.

items.name: Tradition and Shame
items.entry: The people of Mordent follow traditions that prioritize hard work, self-sufficiency, and avoiding public embarrassment. Locals overlook blatant strangeness so not to shame their neighbors, and every family has secrets.

entries.name: Locations in Mordent
entries: Mordent is a foggy land of forests, fields, and moors that endures frequent rainstorms. The land is dotted with tiny farming hamlets and the rotting homes of forgotten nobles.
The town of Mordentshire is detailed below, and other notable locations are summarized on the {@book Mordent Locations table|RHW|2|Mordent Locations}.

entries.name: Mordentshire
entries: Mordentshire is the largest community in Mordent. Sturdy fisherfolk work the town docks and, after dusk, fill crowded tavern common rooms. Cold winds frequently blow in from Arden Bay. When they stop, they're replaced by a shroud of fog the locals call "the breath of the dead."

To the chagrin of the town's no-nonsense populace, Mordentshire is home to a small community of scholars and adventurers. Saulbridge Sanitarium provides a refuge for the ill while also welcoming researchers from other domains. Across town, an unassuming herbalist shop is run by {@creature Rudolph van Richten|RHW} (see {@area chapter 5|3b5|x}), an investigator of all things monstrous and mysterious.

The lands around Mordentshire host two prominent estates. Heather House is the home of the Weathermay family, regional lords who count numerous heroes among their number. To the east stands Gryphon Manor, the notorious House on Gryphon Hill. The mansion has a history of violence, tragedy, and hauntings and is the home of the domain's Darklord, Lord Wilfred Godefroy.

entries.caption: Mordent Locations
rows: A group of silent Ezra worshippers maintain a sanctuary at Abbey Point. Maps there claim that only the sea lies north of the abbey, making the nearby wooded coast a mystery.
Little more than a dock, market, and tavern, Blairsbay serves a line of coastal noble estates. Many of these great homes are abandoned, including Lumley House and Preston Hill.
The hamlet of Candle Cross spills from the remains of a ruined castle, the drawbridge of which extends over the Arden River. The bridge rises of its own accord to bar evil from entering.
Near the village of Crawford, countless of ravens roost at Crawford Manor. A carving in the manor reads: "The Death of Ravens Shalt Thou Abhor, Lest the Crawford Line Be Nevermore."
The residents of the village of Glaston cleave to druidic traditions. Rumors say ancient druids forged magical passages between the nearby Sigil Lakes and similar sites in realms beyond.
Years ago, the sand cliff that bore half the village of Glenwich—including its ancient church—collapsed into the bay. Locals avoid the tide pools mingling amid dilapidation and the dead.
Haworth Hall numbers among the domain's dozens of lonely, decaying manors. Something inhabits the estate, but whatever it is terrifies Mordent's ghost and they avoid it at all costs.
Miniature constructs made of stitched-together body parts run amok in this abandoned hamlet's Punchinel Manor, once home to an eccentric inventor.
Locustbranch was once a renowned hospital, but it was abandoned after a series of mysterious deaths. Now, ghostly patients pack its wards, crying out for cures to eternal ills.
The hamlet of Waterford stands at a confluence of rivers and roads. Here, the Globe inn quietly keeps a back entrance and rooms to accommodate silent, ghostly travelers.
The last of the noble Esker family recently died, bequeathing Whelk Spring House to distant relatives. At low tide, the waters of Bent Key Causeway reveal a muddy path to the estate.

entries.name: Wilfred Godefroy
entries: {@i Darklord Tormented by Ghostly Undeath}
Speak your piece, then hold your tongue. No fools are more insufferable than the living.

Lord Wilfred Godefroy rules the afterlife of all who die in Mordent. Arrogant, impatient, and vengeful, the Darklord seeks any opportunity to exert his control and reinforce his undying noble standing.

Lord Godefroy haunts the House on Gryphon Hill, along with countless other spirits. Wilfred can send mobs of obedient spirits to do his will anywhere in the domain. He uses his control over the dead to influence the living, threatening to harm his victims' dead loved ones if they don't obey his fickle commands.

A generation ago, the creators of the mist-piercing device called the {@area Apparatus|21b|x} vanished from Mordent. Lord Godefroy has discovered the hidden machine, but, to his frustration, lacks a way to use it.

entries.name: Wilfred's Tale
entries: In life, Lord Wilfred Godefroy was an unremarkable minor aristocrat. He inherited the estate of Gryphon Hill near Mordentshire centuries ago by murdering his father and framing it as an accident. During a bloody night years later, the entire Godefroy family—Wilfred; his wife, Estelle; and their daughter, Penelope—died violently. But instead of passing on to the afterlife, the Godefroys' souls lingered at Gryphon Hill. In death, Wilfred is haunted by the ghosts of his family, recent and the past generations, who blame him for their deaths and the family's disgrace.

A generation ago, a mysterious alchemist created a device in Mordent called the {@area Apparatus|21b|x}. This magical machine had many fantastic powers, including the ability to open passages between the planes and separate a creature's good and evil natures into separate beings. The Apparatus drew multiple powerful beings to Mordent and eventually attracted the Mists themselves when the device malfunctioned, causing a deadly disaster. The Mists collected Mordent into the Domains of Dread, and the mysterious alchemist vanished. With the departure of mightier beings, Lord Godefroy remained as the land's most fearful villain. He inherited the mantle of Darklord of the new domain of Mordent.

Since then, Wilfred has claimed lordship of all who die in Mordent. He knows the location of the Apparatus and seeks to master its powers, but how to do so and the location of a vital piece of the machine—the Rod of Rastinon—both elude him. He seeks to blackmail anyone who might be able to reveal the device's secrets or find the missing rod. Wilfred forces Alice Weathermay, mayor of Mordentshire, to keep him appraised of useful visitors to the domain, by holding her dead husband, Daniel Foxgrove, hostage.

entries.name: Wilfred's Domain
entries: Mordent is altered by Lord Godefroy's presence, creating the following effects:

items.name: Closing the Borders
items.entry: While in his domain, Lord Godefroy can close or open the borders to Mordent. While the borders are closed, the groans and whispers of the dead fill the Mists surrounding Mordent. Creatures in the Mists have the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition.

items.name: Ears Everywhere
items.entry: While in his domain, Lord Godefroy can cast {@spell Sending|XPHB}, requiring no Material components and using Charisma as the spellcasting ability. When casting the spell in this way, Lord Godefroy can target any Undead creature in Mordent, in addition to the usual targets.

items.name: Spectral Serfs
items.entry: A Humanoid that dies in Mordent rises 7 days later as a {@creature Specter|XMM} under Lord Godefroy's control, unless the Humanoid is restored to life.

entries: If Wilfred dies, these effects end immediately.

entries.name: Wilfred's Lair
entries: The House on Gryphon Hill lies just outside Mordentshire and is home to Lord Wilfred Godefroy. Hundreds of spirits remain bound to the house and the surrounding grounds, and all serve the lord of the house and struggle to stay in his good graces. Spirits that rebel are punished—either forced to face Wilfred's kennel of spectral hounds or dragged into the deepest recesses of the house, which even the eldest spirits fear.

entries.name: House on Gryphon Hill
entries: Cobwebs and dust coat the House on Gryphon Hill. Lurking spirits remain out of sight until trespassers pose a threat or Lord Godefroy orders them to attack. The house has the following features:

items.name: Basement
items.entry: Lord Godefroy orders ghostly teams to endlessly dig deeper and wider basements beneath the house. As if creating his own underworld, Wilfred imprisons spirits who displease him in the expanding haunted dungeons beneath the house. Somewhere amid these dungeons, a passage connects to the site where the {@area Apparatus|21b|x} is hidden.

items.name: Haunted Rooms
items.entry: Estelle and Penelope Godefroy haunt the house's attic, avoiding Wilfred and doing all they can to frustrate the Darklord and stymie his ambitions. The house's other spirits obey Estelle and Penelope unless their commands contradict an order from Wilfred.

items.name: Withered Estate
items.entry: Wilfred's ghostly hounds patrol the house's grounds, while spectral groundskeepers tend to dead topiaries and hedges as if they still thrived.

entries.name: Adventures in Mordent
entries: Mordent is a realm of ghost stories (see {@area chapter 4|372|x}). Moldering manors, lonely moors, neglected graveyards, and similar gothic sites provide natural settings for encounters with spirits and the tragic dead. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

entries.title: Fog locally known as the breath of the dead obscures mysterious shapes on the streets of Mordentshire

entries.name: Horror in Mordent
entries: Adventures in Mordent often include the following elements.

entries.name: The Apparatus
entries: Wilfred is obsessed with control over the dead and the living. He knows where the magical device known as the Apparatus is hidden but has limited understanding of how to use it. Nevertheless, he knows it is powerful and can produce devastating effects. He cares nothing for the machine's remarkable abilities and seeks to use it as a weapon to force all in Mordent to obey him.

To fulfill his goals, Wilfred seeks individuals with magical and alchemical insights, as well as rumors of the Apparatus's missing piece, the Rod of Rastinon. He might threaten or blackmail the living into aiding him. Should he suspect the Rod of Rastinon or other vital information lies beyond Mordent, he might send his agents to other domains.
The Apparatus can exchange souls, open passages through the Mists, and create more dangerous effects, but the magical device might have any properties an adventure demands.

entries.name: Bastion of Heroes
entries: Despite the horrors lurking in Mordent, the domain hosts several quiet, relatively peaceful communities. Characters from Mordent might have had relatively peaceful, mundane lives before mysteries or tragedies led them to adventure. The abandoned estates of Mordent also make fine places to use the rules for Haunted Bastions, detailed in {@area chapter 3|30e|x}.

entries.name: Ghosts of Mordent
entries: Ghost stories often involve spirits that linger as the result of some tragedy or unfinished work. In Mordent, spirits linger and might request help from the living. Roll on or choose a result from the Mordent Hauntings table to inspire adventures featuring the lingering dead.

entries.caption: Mordent Hauntings
rows: Freeing a living loved one from possession by another ghost.
Hiding a secret that could embarrass or undermine a living loved one.
Liberating another spirit from the dungeons beneath the House on Gryphon Hill.
Locating a spirit that wanders a broad region or who appears only during the new moon.
Restoring the memory of an ancient spirit that has almost lost its sense of self.
Searching a distant haunted site for a spirit it has been separated from.
Sealing the site of an ancient evil or the lair of a creature that torments spirits.
Smuggling a living relative out of Mordent, away from Wilfred's clutches.

entries: Even though spirits are trapped in Mordent, many find secluded places where they reflect on nature, their lives, and eternity. Over generations, such spirits often become little more than vague shapes or disembodied voices or emotions before fading away entirely.

entries.name: Van Richten's Herbalist Shop
entries: {@creature Rudolph van Richten|RHW} (see {@area chapter 5|3b5|x}) is the foremost scholar of monsters and strange occurrences in the Domains of Dread. He lives and works from the Mordentshire herbalist shop (see {@book map 2.27|RHW|2|Map 2.27: Van Richten's Herbalist Shop}). Beyond the storefront, Rudolph's studies and prepares methods of combating evils plaguing the Land of the Mists. Adventurers might find lore and tools to combat all manner of monsters here.

When absent from his shop, Rudolph leaves it in the care of his protégés, {@creature Gennifer Weathermay-Foxgrove|RHW|Gennifer} and {@creature Laurie Weathermay-Foxgrove|RHW} (detailed in {@area chapter 5|3c9|x}) and Beatrice Polk, an elderly local gardener and accomplished detective.

entries.name: Mordent Campaign Arc
entries: Mordent campaigns often involve ghostly mysteries. A campaign here might follow this outline:

items.name: Levels 1–2
items.entry: Caarey Gelthik, a local ne'er-do-well in Mordent, has become a {@creature Ghast|XMM} and lurks beneath the town docks. By night, he robs graves to consume the bodies within. The characters take note when silent spirits—perhaps of allies—begin wandering town, each missing limbs, tongues, and other soft features. Only finding and defeating Gelthik causes the dismember spirits to depart.

items.name: Levels 3–4
items.entry: Beatrice Polk, caretaker at the Mordentshire herbalist shop, hires the characters to end a haunting afflicting the shop. See the adventure "{@area The Ghost Hunter's Haunting|220|x}."

items.name: Levels 5–10
items.entry: Alice Weathermay, mayor of Mordentshire, hires the characters to find a lost piece of local history: the Rod of Rastinon. She shares leads involving various strange sites, but the rod doesn't appear. Eventually, {@creature Wilfred Godefroy|RHW|Lord Godefroy}—who has been working through Alice Weathermay—grows impatient. He threatens the party's dead loved ones, insisting the characters find the rod swiftly and help him use the Apparatus (see {@book chapter 3|RHW|3}).

entries.name: The Ghost Hunter's Haunting
entries: {@i Adventure for Level 3 Characters}

items.name: Situation
items.entry: An accident at {@area Rudolph van Richten's|3b5|x} herbalist shop broke a strange relic and unleashed a deadly feline spirit from another domain.

items.name: Hook
items.entry: Beatrice Polk, the shop's caretaker, offers the characters 400 GP if they end the haunting.

entries.name: Encounters
entries: This adventure uses map 2.27 and consists of these encounters.

entries.name: Beatrice Polk's Aid
entries: Beyond her folksy politeness and dislike of curse words, Beatrice Polk takes her stewardship of the herbalist shop seriously but doesn't enter the shop during the haunting. She can provide the characters with a number of {@item Potion of Healing|XDMG|Potions of Healing} equal to the party's members. She scuttles plans that involve harming or stealing Rudolph's possessions.

entries.name: Ghost Cat
entries: A {@creature Wraith|XMM} that appears as a black cat the size of a panther haunts the shop. Whenever the characters enter a room without monsters present, roll a die. If the number is odd, the cat is in the room. If the cat has more than half its Hit Points, it fights for a round, then flees to another room or floor; otherwise, it flees without attacking. If destroyed, the wraith reappears 24 hours later with full Hit Points. Its threat ends only if the ritual in the cellar is completed.

entries.name: Laboratory
entries: Two {@creature Poltergeist|XMM|Poltergeists} haunt this room.

entries.name: Stairs
entries: An unnatural wind blows against anyone who tries to ascend either stairway to the second floor. A character must succeed on a {@dc 16} Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check to climb the stairs.

entries.name: Dumbwaiter
entries: A dumbwaiter connects the kitchen and the upstairs hall. It can hold one Medium or smaller creature. A creature outside the dumbwaiter can use an action to raise or lower the dumbwaiter and its contents.

entries.name: Upstairs Hall
entries: Broken on the floor is a strange relic: a mummied cat. Shelves nearby hold accounts of Rudolph's travels in a land called {@book Har'Akir|RHW|2}.

entries.name: Library
entries: Rudolph's library is filled with occult texts. Any character who spends a minute investigating the library finds a copy of Van Richten's Guide to Ghosts. Those who spend at least 10 minutes reading this book and have seen both the relic in the upstairs hall and the ritual room in the cellar understand how to use the cellar room to exorcise the ghostly cat. Additionally, a character who succeeds on a {@dc 12} Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check finds a {@item Spell Scroll (Level 1)|XDMG|Spell Scroll} of {@spell Protection from Evil and Good|XPHB}.

entries.name: Guest Room
entries: Standing facing a corner of this room is {@creature Gennifer Weathermay-Foxgrove|RHW}. She is possessed by a {@creature Ghost|XMM}, which holds her in a catatonic state. Gennifer is freed of her possession if she's affected by a spell like Protection from Evil and Good or if she's removed from the shop. The feline-featured wraith attacks the party if its possession ends or if Gennifer is moved from the room. If freed from the ghost's control, Gennifer is aware of the ghostly cat, as she accidentally broke the relic in the hall. She knows how to exorcise the spirit using the wards in the cellar.

entries.name: Cellar
entries: A room in the cellar is prepared for magic rituals. A character who succeeds on a {@dc 12} Intelligence ({@skill Arcana|XPHB}) check realizes this space is warded to contain or dispel spirits. If the characters place the relic from the upstairs hall within the space, the ghostly cat appears 1 minute later and fights until destroyed. If the cat is defeated here, the relic repairs itself and the haunting ends. Each character gains a bonus 400 XP for ending the haunting.

entries.name: Shadowlands
entries: {@i Domain of Faded Glory and False Virtue}

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: {@area Ebonbane|24a|x}

items.name: Genre:
items.entry: {@area Gothic horror|37b|x}

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Cursed magic items, doomed quests, falls from grace, knightly lineages, obsessive zeal

items.name: Mist Talismans:
items.entry: Broken holy symbol, religious relic, symbol of the Circle

items.name: Aligned Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Torturer

items.name: Opposed Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Broken One

entries: For the knights of the Shadowlands, the struggle with evil isn't a philosophical trial—it is an active war. East of the rugged Theospine Mountains spreads the peaceful realm of Nidala, where the virtuous knights of the Circle guard their lands against wickedness beyond and within. To the mountains' west lies the ruined land of Avonleigh, the birthplace of the knightly Circle; this realm fell to evil long ago and has become overgrown by the spirit-stalked Phantasmal Forest. Many knights of the Circle view themselves as inheritors of a legacy of shame. They aspire to the heroism of their storied ancestors and battle to absolve themselves of imagined failures. They throw themselves into crusades against foes, real and imagined, sacrificing their lives—and those of their people—for redemption and glory.

For all the Circle's good intentions, these knights are cursed. Monsters and villains they've defeated reappear in the Shadowlands seeking vengeance. Most infamously, deep in the Phantasmal Forest lingers the evil that caused Avonleigh's downfall: {@creature Ebonbane|RHW}, Darklord of the Shadowlands and the sapient sword of the Circle's founder, Kateri Shadowborn.

entries.name: Domain Features
entries: The Shadowlands is known for the following features:

items.name: Bastions on the Borderlands
items.entry: The Circle seeks to reclaim a foothold in Avonleigh in a region called the Borderlands. They often reward allies with Bastions here (see {@area chapter 3|30e|x} and the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|XDMG} for details on {@book Bastions|XDMG|10}).

items.name: The Circle
items.entry: The knights of the Circle (described in {@area chapter 3|2fc|x}) make their home in this domain.

items.name: Nidala's Ruler
items.entry: Elena Faith-hold rules Nidala. But this knight is rarely present in the land, as she leads deadly quests beyond the domain's borders (see "{@area Faith-hold's Crusades|23f|x}").

entries.name: Locations in the Shadowlands
entries: The Shadowlands is a dualistic domain: Avonleigh remains an overtly haunted wilderness home to lurking evils, while Nidala touts itself as the virtuous opposite. But the land's harmony is artificial, its people forced to smile while trapped in the service of Elena Faith-hold and her sycophants. Accordingly, the domain's conflict isn't one of good versus evil, but rather of overt and covert evils.
The Phantasmal Forest is detailed below, and other notable locations are summarized on the {@book Shadowlands Locations table|RHW|2|Shadowlands Locations}.

entries.name: Phantasmal Forest
entries: The Phantasmal Forest dominates the haunted land of Avonleigh, a shifting, surreal woodland cloaked by perpetual night. A dreamlike place controlled by {@i Ebonbane}, the forest changes constantly, and locations within drift like flotsam on a dark sea. The River Avalon and the King's Highway—an overgrown route of misaligned stones—enter the forest, but within the woods they circle endlessly and never leave. Only those descended from the Circle's founding members can navigate the Phantasmal Forest without becoming lost. All others wander at {@i Ebonbane}'s whim, becoming prey for the wolves, corrupted fey, cursed knights, or wicked souls that haunt the forest.

entries.caption: Shadowlands Locations
rows: After being slain by Elena Faith-hold, the dragon Banemaw returned as a {@creature dracolich|XMM}. Elena keeps this a secret and sacrifices a dozen villagers to the creature every three years.
Several abandoned keeps stand within sight of the Phantasmal Forest. The Circle grants these holdings to members and allies who swear to restore them as bastions of light.
From her castle, Elena readies knights for bold quests, makes her "Decrees of Faith," and employs harsh inquisitors in hidden torture chambers and reeducation cells.
Icy Lake Amenta is said to hide a golden realm beneath its waters. Only Elena remembers the city of "heretical" dwarf artisans she drowned in a flood here long ago.
Levity is forbidden in the lumbering community of Proll. People are wary of smiling and report those suspected of secretly being bards. Those who transgress are sent to the Faith Hold.
The peasants of Eckham rebelled against their harsh treatment. Elena claimed the people succumbed to demon worship and had her followers slay the locals and burn the town.
Ludyard met a fate similar to Eckham. Freedom fighters hide out here and plan Elena's undoing, but they endlessly squabble over how best to rule after her downfall.
Ebonbane tempts virtuous souls to Shadowborn Manor, crushes their faith, and takes control of their bodies. The manor is still haunted by the spirit of Kateri Shadowborn.
The Circle was founded here long ago. Amid rune-carved dolmens is a circular slab marked with the impression of thirteen distinct swords. Ebonbane sees nothing that happens here.
Long ago, the wizard Morgoroth betrayed the Circle but was trapped in a mirror in Tergeron Manor. Within the manor lies a crystal coffin that holds the hero Aurora Shadowborn in stasis.
The gleaming, white-walled city of Touraine is peaceful and safe. The people are wary of strangers and the evils they bring. A grand temple of Ezra serves as a meeting place for the Circle.
Wynderton is a farming community protected by a family of knights of the same name. To prove their piety, the family insists the villagers attend religious services three times a day.

entries.name: Ebonbane
entries: {@i Darklord of Endless Vengeance}
Questing knights? Champions of the realm? Look upon thy sterling souls. Tell me, heroes, by what fair deeds art thou known in this rotted world?

{@item Ebonbane|RHW} is an evil, sapient blade that seeks to annihilate the legacy of the Shadowlands' greatest hero and her allies. This includes all with the Shadowborn name and every member of the Circle.

{@i Ebonbane} is a magic weapon (see "{@area Ebonbane as a Magic Item|232|x}" later in this section), but within Shadowborn Manor, it becomes a creature that can move on its own (see the {@b Ebonbane} stat block). Beyond the manor, it doesn't use its stat block—it requires a wielder to work its evil in the world.

Many who attempt to attune to the weapon are slain outright by its evil. Those who do manage to wield {@i Ebonbane} must endure the Darklord's constant demands and risk becoming its pawn (see "{@book Sentient Magic Items|XDMG|6|Sentient Magic Items}" in the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|XDMG}). If unattended outside Shadowborn Manor, {@i Ebonbane} eventually finds its way back there. No one can carry {@i Ebonbane} out of the Shadowlands. If someone tries, the sword vanishes and reappears in Shadowborn Manor.

{@i Ebonbane} has a flamboyant personality and a penchant for the dramatic and sadistic. It has no regard for life and delights in animating the corpses of foes, then destroying them in front of their allies. When it speaks, it uses language that mocks the elevated speech of knights.

entries.name: Ebonbane's Tale
entries: Kateri Shadowborn was a great hero who, alongside her order of valorous knights called the Circle, spent her life in service of good. Among her greatest deeds was leading an army against a nation whose king had been possessed by a fiend. She defeated the fiend, though doing so caused her magic sword to shatter.

After a life of great deeds, Kateri Shadowborn retired to live in peace with her family at Shadowborn Manor. But the hero had made many enemies. Thinking her vulnerable, wicked cultists recovered the pieces of Kateri's broken sword and conjured the lingering malice of the evils she had thwarted. These the cultists forged into a terrible weapon, {@i Ebonbane}—and they became its first victims.

Animated by hatred for Kateri and her allies, {@i Ebonbane} took control of its creators and became a curse on the world. For years it worked to destroy what Kateri had created, ruin what she loved, and slaughter those who aided her. Many times the evil sword's wielder was captured, but none ever suspected that the weapon itself was behind the grisly crimes.

Finally, in the hands of an unassuming monk, {@i Ebonbane} found its way to Shadowborn Manor, where grim news had isolated Kateri and worn her down. Aged but still a mighty knight, Kateri recognized {@i Ebonbane}'s threat, and a great battle ensued. During the clash, Kateri kept {@i Ebonbane}'s attention and ensured her family and friends escaped the manor. When only the hero and the wicked sword remained, Kateri summoned her remaining might into a blow to destroy {@i Ebonbane}. But the wicked sword proved too powerful. Kateri's blade shattered, and {@i Ebonbane} struck down the legendary knight. Exalting in its victory, the evil weapon possessed Kateri's body and pursued her fleeing family. But as it did so, it witnessed its quarry fading into the rising Mists.

When the Mists receded, {@i Ebonbane} found itself in a realm where the residents regarded the ancestors of Kateri Shadowborn and her fellow knights as heroes. Thrilled to continue its spree, {@i Ebonbane} sought to leave Shadowborn Manor, but it had no one to carry it beyond the grounds. Now, the evil blade seethes in frustration, waiting for a worthy bearer to carry it into the world so it might destroy all the good that came from Kateri's work.

entries.name: Ebonbane as a Magic Item
entries: {@i Ebonbane}'s magic weapon form is described below.

entries.name: Ebonbane
entries: Weapon (Longsword), Artifact (Requires Attunement)
The Darklord of the Domain of Dread known as the Shadowlands is {@i Ebonbane}, a sapient sword. {@creature Ebonbane|RHW} uses its monster stat block while in Shadowborn Manor.

entries.name: Bound to the Shadowlands
entries: When you enter a space outside of the Shadowlands, your Attunement immediately ends and Ebonbane teleports to somewhere in Shadowborn Manor.

entries.name: Insatiable Rage
entries: The sword demands destruction. If the sword hasn't slain a Celestial or a Humanoid for 3 days, you make a {@dc 17} Charisma saving throw at the next dawn. On a successful save, you take {@damage 8d8} Force damage. On a failed save, you are dominated by the sword, as if by the {@spell Dominate Monster|XPHB} spell, and the sword demands the blood of a Celestial or a Humanoid. The spell effect ends when the sword's demand is met.

entries.name: Magic Weapon
entries: You gain a +3 bonus to attack rolls and damage rolls made with this magic weapon. When you hit a Celestial or a Humanoid with it, that attack also deals {@damage 3d6} Necrotic damage.
While you hold this weapon, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

entries.name: Sentience
entries: Ebonbane is a sentient Chaotic Evil weapon with an Intelligence of 17, a Wisdom of 14, and a Charisma of 19. It has hearing and {@sense Truesight|XPHB} out to 120 feet. The weapon speaks Common and can communicate with its wielder telepathically. While you are attuned to it, Ebonbane also understands every language you know.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Ebonbane was created to kill the paladin Kateri Shadowborn, and it still possesses a burning hatred for goodly knights. Ebonbane revels in seeing good defeated and hope vanquished. The sword bonds with any wielder that assists in its goals but always views it wielder as a lackey.

entries.name: Destroying Ebonbane
entries: The only way to destroy Ebonbane is to forge a weapon from the {@area Four Keys|240|x} and reduce Ebonbane to 0 Hit Points with it (see the {@b Ebonbane} stat block).

entries.name: Ebonbane's Domain
entries: The Shadowlands is altered by {@i Ebonbane}'s presence, creating the following effects:

items.name: Closing the Borders
items.entry: While in its domain, Ebonbane can close or open the borders of the Shadowlands at will. While the borders are closed, whirling blades of magic fly through the Mists surrounding the Shadowlands. A creature that enters the Mists for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a {@dc 15} Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 3d10} Force damage. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

items.name: Haunted Woods
items.entry: The forest within 1 mile of Ebonbane's lair is wreathed in a powerful illusion. Whenever a creature other than Ebonbane or the sword's allies finishes a Short or Long Rest in that area, it must succeed on a {@dc 15} Intelligence saving throw, or it is lost in the forest, always finding itself at the location it started in until it finishes a Short or Long Rest.

items.name: Turning Weapons
items.entry: While in its lair, Ebonbane can transform nonmagical {@item Greatsword|XPHB|Greatswords} and {@item Longsword|XPHB|Longswords} within 1 mile of the lair into {@creature Animated Flying Sword|XMM|Animated Flying Swords} by completing a 24-hour ritual.

entries: If {@i Ebonbane} is destroyed, these effects end immediately.

entries.name: Ebonbane's Lair
entries: {@i Ebonbane} remains imprisoned in Shadowborn Manor, a fortified estate hidden in the Phantasmal Forest. The grounds are strewn with the bodies of those who sought the legendary blade and failed.

entries.name: Shadowborn Manor
entries: Though abandoned, Shadowborn Manor remains in decent repair. The estate is depicted on {@book map 2.29|RHW|2|Map 2.29: Shadowborn Manor} and has the following features:

items.name: Dungeon
items.entry: The Circle understood that some evils needed to be contained. A secret stairway in the east wing leads to a dungeon that has remained hidden from Ebonbane.

items.name: Great Hall
items.entry: This hall hosts the round table where members of the Circle once met. Ebonbane poses the bodies of knights here to possess as it pleases.

items.name: Lady's Chambers
items.entry: Kateri Shadowborn's armored corpse lies in the largest bedroom on the second floor. It is Ebonbane's favorite body to reanimate, and if destroyed, it reappears in this chamber.

entries.name: Adventures in the Shadowlands
entries: Adventures set in the Shadowlands feature individuals striving to prove their worth or superiority. Characters might be welcome to join the knights of the Circle, or perhaps they belong to families with heroic histories. Such ties could connect characters to the domain's history and future quests. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

entries.title: The remains of failed quests and fallen knights lie near Shadowborn Manor in the Phantasmal Forest

entries.name: Horror in the Shadowlands
entries: Adventures in the Shadowlands often include the following elements.

entries.name: Faith-hold's Crusades
entries: Elena Faith-hold rules Nidala, crushing its people under the weight of her intolerance and need to maintain her virtuous reputation. While she doesn't lead the Circle, she is its most respected member.

Elena leads forays of knights into the Mists on lengthy quests to defeat wicked foes in mysterious lands. An invitation to join one of Elena's crusades is a great honor and a death sentence. Whenever Elena returns from the Mists, she always does so alone, claiming to be the sole survivor of a great victory.
Elena Faith-hold isn't a Darklord, but should someone ever destroy {@i Ebonbane}, she might ascend to that position.

entries.name: Quest of the Four Keys
entries: One can destroy {@i Ebonbane} permanently using the sword of Kateri Shadowborn. However, the Dark Powers shattered her sword and scattered its pieces. These fragments are known as the Four Keys—emblems of Kateri's valor and faith. Each embodies a different virtue and one of the four elements.

entries.name: The Keys' Locations
entries: The Four Keys might appear anywhere in the Shadowlands or other domains. The ghost of Kateri Shadowborn or lore hidden amid tragic sites might point toward the keys.

entries.name: Using the Keys
entries: Should the characters collect the Four Keys, they can reforge them into a weapon that—if used to reduce {@creature Ebonbane|RHW} to 0 Hit Points—negates its Darklord Restoration trait. It's up to you whether this ends the Darklord's threat or unleashes a greater evil bound within.

entries.name: Shadowlands Campaign Arc
entries: Campaigns in the Shadowlands often involve quests that reveal evils steeped in tragedies and abuses of power. A campaign here might follow this outline:

items.name: Levels 1–4
items.entry: The characters aid members of the Circle in defeating a dangerous {@creature Basilisk|XMM} in Nidala. Impressed, the knights charge the characters with reclaiming a fort in the Borderlands from a wicked knight. The knight—a Circle member who died trying to recover Kateri Shadowborn's sword—is now a {@creature Revenant|XMM}. Once the knight is defeated, the Circle gives the characters its fort as a Bastion ({@book detailed|XDMG|10} in the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|XDMG}).

items.name: Level 5–8
items.entry: In their Bastion, the characters find a piece of a broken sword. The spirit of Kateri Shadowborn appears to the most virtuous character and asks for their help freeing her spirit and defeating Ebonbane. She shares the details in the "{@area Quest of the Four Keys|240|x}" section. The characters begin a journey across the Shadowlands and beyond to recover the other three sword fragments.

items.name: Levels 9–11
items.entry: Once the characters have the Four Keys, Kateri's spirit instructs them to travel to the Stone Circle in the Phantasmal Forest, where the sword can be magically reforged as a {@item Holy Avenger|XDMG}. With this weapon, the characters journey to Shadowborn Manor and face {@creature Ebonbane|RHW}.

entries.name: The Shadowborn Sword
entries: {@i Adventure for Level 10 Characters}

items.name: Situation
items.entry: A knight seeks to prove herself by finding a hero's lost sword.

items.name: Hook
items.entry: The human knight Lucia Lambert (Medium, Lawful Good {@creature Questing Knight|XMM}) invites the characters to join her mission to recover the lost sword of the hero Kateri Shadowborn. In return, she'll reward them with 4,000 GP—or double that amount in additions to the characters' Bastions.

entries.name: Encounters
entries: The adventure starts at the edge of the Phantasmal Forest and consists of these encounters.

entries.name: Phantasmal Forest
entries: The journey to Shadowborn Manor is 18 miles, with the forest's density and perpetual {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} limiting characters to a {@book Slow pace|XPHB|1|TravelPace}. As Lucia's family members have belonged to the Circle since its founding, she can navigate the forest at a Normal pace, so the journey takes only 8 hours.

entries.name: Fallen Knight
entries: Outside the manor, a {@creature Death Knight Aspirant|XMM} wearing the armor of a Circle knight bars the path and warns characters not to enter. A Good character who succeeds on a {@dc 16} Charisma ({@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check can convince the corpse to let them pass; otherwise, it attacks those who try to enter.

entries.name: Shadowborn Manor
entries: Armored corpses lie about Shadowborn Manor (use map 2.29). If the characters disturb them or enter the great hall, five rise as {@creature Strahd Skeleton|RHW|Strahd Skeletons} (see {@area chapter 5|3bb|x}).

entries.name: Shadowborn's Ghost
entries: The Lawful Good {@creature Ghost|XMM} of Kateri Shadowborn haunts the manor. If the characters seem near defeat, she appears and can distract Ebonbane or lead them to the hidden dungeon.

entries.name: Ebonbane
entries: {@creature Ebonbane|RHW} (see earlier in {@area this chapter|24a|x}) lies in the great hall. If with the characters, Lucia claims the sword and is stricken dead by Ebonbane, who animates her body. Otherwise, Ebonbane animates another corpse. The Darklord tries to convince the characters to take it from the manor so it can slay their foes. If they reject it, Ebonbane fights.

entries.name: Conclusion
entries: Should the characters escape Shadowborn Manor, each of them earns a bonus 3,100 XP. If they don't take Ebonbane, Kateri's ghost appears to them the following night and explains that Ebonbane's evil isn't ended. She goes explains the Darklord's history and how to destroy it (see "{@area Quest of the Four Keys|240|x}"). The Circle rewards the characters in Lucia's stead if she is slain.

entries.name: Sithicus
entries: {@i Domain of the Soul's Shadow}

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: {@area Lord Loren Soth|252|x}

items.name: Genre:
items.entry: {@area Dark fantasy|360|x}

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Cataclysmic fire, malicious shadows, tormented memories, twisted fantasy

items.name: Mist Talismans:
items.entry: Black rose, burnt armor, scrap of black fabric, three thorns

items.name: Aligned Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Paladin

items.name: Opposed Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Innocent

entries: Whatever Sithicus was or could have been, it will never be. The dramatic ruins of a fabulous elven realm cover a land realm forested by gigantic trees and burning with the flames of a forgotten magical disaster. Scattered communities appear among the dilapidation, the domain's predominantly elf population living like strangers amid structures to which they have no connection. At dusk, people withdraw, hiding from the domain's true residents: the shadows.

By night, the shadows of Sithicus reenact forgotten lives or mimic the deeds of questing knights, devoted lovers, and lurking monsters. Most of these shadows are harmless, if unnerving, and seem not to notice other beings. Some, however, resent the domain's other residents and torment the living.

Though few in the domain realize it, Sithicus has ties to the world of Krynn on the Material Plane. Ruins suggest the work of elves from that world, while the night sky blazes with the five-headed dragon constellation of the deity Takhisis. As on Krynn, the black moon Nuitari is visible only to evil beings. But the domain's clearest tie to Krynn is its Darklord, the accursed knight {@creature Lord Soth of Sithicus|RHW|Lord Soth}.

entries.name: Domain Features
entries: Sithicus is known for the following features:

items.name: Bound to Krynn
