items.entry: Sithicus is a warped reflection of the lands of Krynn. This world and its people, such as {@race kender|DSotDQ}, are described in {@adventure Dragonlance: Shadow of the Dragon Queen|DSotDQ}.

items.name: Cataclysmic Fire
items.entry: Accursed flames blaze amid prominent ruins and turn shadows into monsters drawn from Lord Soth's past.

items.name: Living Shadows
items.entry: Shadows in Sithicus move on their own. Most are harmless, but some are deadly creatures such a {@creature Shadow|XMM|Shadows} or {@creature Shadow Demon|XMM|Shadow Demons}.

items.name: Soth's Traitor
items.entry: Lord Soth's undead soldiers hunt for the ghostly knight Caradoc, whom Soth blames for his imprisonment in Sithicus.

entries.name: Locations in Sithicus
entries: Two significant features of Sithicus are detailed below, and other notable locations are summarized on the {@book Sithicus Locations table|RHW|2|Sithicus Locations}.

entries.name: Endless Ruins
entries: Sithicus lies blanketed in endless ruins. Ancient forests rise from them, rivers flow in their broken avenues, and whole mountain ranges are sculpted with crumbled towers. These structures suggest the work of a vast, magical elven nation, but all evidence of these people has been reduced to enigmatic scraps of art and lore. At the heights of crumbling towers or in empty crypts blaze violet, cataclysmic fire (see "{@area Cataclysmic Flames|25a|x}"). In rare cases, these phantasmal flames reveal glimpses of Lord Soth's past and details that the clever might use to win the Darklord's favor.

entries.name: Great Chasm
entries: The Great Chasm gapes in Sithicus like a wound. None can say how deep it goes, as light fails to pierce more than a few dozen feet into the ravine. Those who descend deeper vanish into the shadows and rarely return. Those who do tell of a malicious darkness beneath the domain, a cacophony of hateful voices, and shadows that hunger.

Hidden amid ruins spilling into the chasm is the home of Inza Kulchevich, a hateful witch who can speak to shadows and believes all virtue is a lie. Despite her cynicism, she might perform prophetic fortune-telling or share strange lore in return for her visitors' shadows.

entries.caption: Sithicus Locations
rows: Throughout the Giant's Cloak forest are petrified trees carved with strange runes and weeping tar-like sap. Stone giant hermits tend these groves and set deadly traps against trespassers.
Har-Thelen is largely abandoned. Its gates stand open, and only a few hundred people reside at its center. By night, stray shadows work and live among the abandoned structures.
The decrepit town of Hroth is built amid giant swamp trees, many of which lean at precarious angles. The bogs nearby are said to be magic and can return to life those buried within.
The Iron Warden Inn is closed by day, but at night, those with enough wealth to buy entry gamble at games of cards and death's-head dice. Sometimes the dead come to play.
A hidden village of faded tree houses, huts, and burrows, Kendralind is home to a few dozen kender vampires. The residents are equally delighted by games, whimsical tales, and bloodshed.
The Lake of Glass moves like water but is as solid as glass. At night, the lake reflects the hidden moon, Nuitari, and magic cast within its reflected light is empowered but cursed.
Forgotten scholars built Mal-Erek amid giant trees. Now only squatters and obsessed scholars reside here, with few noticing the decay of the vaults and archives around them.
The Merchant's Slash trail is littered with the wreckage of merchant and traveler caravans. Some are still occupied by eccentric traders selling eerie wares from strange lands.
The towering fortress of Lord Soth is strangled by black thorns and blazes with cataclysmic flame. Within, undead knights and spectral elves reenact and recite Soth's great failures.
A hidden prison for the living guarded by the dead, Sorrow accepts mysterious transfers from beyond the Mists. Many thought dead in other domains are imprisoned here.
A dismal work camp stands at the entrance to a maze of unfathomably deep salt mines. Caustic minerals burn explorers who seek the source of whispers from the depths.

entries.name: Lord Soth
entries: {@i Darklord Cursed by Memories}
Explain why you have interrupted my reverie, or I will turn your waking hours into nightmares.

Lord Loren Soth seethes within a realm that mocks and frustrates him. Having known the rigid standards of knighthood and warfare, Soth finds Sithicus empty and chaotic, his people apathetic and undisciplined. The elven beauty that once captivated him lies ruined, the land burns with the flames of Krynn's Cataclysm, and the realm's shadows remind Soth of his past failures. Worse, no worthy foes exist to challenge him. So Soth broods on his throne in Nedragaard Keep, haunted by banshees that sing the tale of his disgrace and by rotted, undead copies of the knights who once served him.

Lord Soth cares nothing for his domain. But should noteworthy threats or remarkable individuals appear, he seeks to crush or enlist them. The only exception is the spirit of his treacherous former ally, Caradoc. Soth's undead servants and his werebadger seneschal, Azrael Dax, scour the land for the elusive ghost.

Soth shares his imprisonment in Sithicus with all in the domain by keeping its borders perpetually closed. Only those who rouse him to activity or invade Nedragaard Keep might convince him to let them escape. This risks provoking the mighty death knight's wrath, but Soth still thinks of himself as a true knight—he might assist those who present themselves as honorable warriors or show knowledge of his home (see "{@area Influencing Lord Soth|25c|x}").

entries.name: Villain of Two Worlds
entries: Lord Soth features in adventures in both the Dragonlance and Ravenloft campaign settings. If you want to give Soth a unique relationship to the Domains of Dread, consider these possibilities:

items: The nature of Soth's curse creates aspects of the death knight in both Krynn and Sithicus. When Soth broods in his fortress in Krynn, he remains active in Sithicus. When the evil god, Takhisis, calls for his service on Krynn, Soth enters a magical torpor in his fortress in Sithicus.
Soth traveled from Krynn to Sithicus and is destined to return. If he leaves Sithicus, the domain might be destroyed, or a villain like Inza Kulchevich might take over.
Soth was trapped in Sithicus, escaped, and was trapped again, perpetuating a cycle of despair.

entries: Soth's story and where characters encounter him are for you to detail in your adventures.

entries.name: Lord Soth's Tale
entries: Lord Soth was given a quest to save the people of his world, Krynn, from the wrath of the gods. Possessed by baseless paranoia and possessiveness of his elf wife, Soth forsook that quest, and the world was devastated in a great Cataclysm. In punishment, Soth was resurrected as a death knight and, for centuries, brooded on his disgrace in the charred ruins of his fortress, Dargaard Keep.

When the evil god Takhisis whispered to Soth, promising to restore his glory and avenge him against the heroic knights who banished him long ago, Soth answered and served Takhisis. During this time, he called on his undead servants, including the ghost of the knight Caradoc. Seeking to forge a new knighthood of loyal dead, Soth became obsessed with Dragon Highlord Kitiara Uth Matar and charged Caradoc with claiming her soul. When Kitiara died in battle, Caradoc refused to capture her spirit unless Soth bargained with Takhisis to restore Caradoc to life. Enraged, Soth tried to destroy Caradoc as the Mists rose around them.

When the Mists faded, Soth found himself in the domain of {@book Barovia|RHW|2}. For a time, he found an ally in {@creature Strahd von Zarovich|RHW|Count Strahd von Zarovich}, but the two villains couldn't abide one another for long. Soth eventually pursued rumors of his treacherous servant, Caradoc, into the Mists. When the Mists parted again, the domain of Sithicus spread before him.

entries.name: Lord Soth's Domain
entries: Sithicus is altered by Lord Soth's presence, creating the following effects:

items.name: Closing the Borders
items.entry: While in his domain, Lord Soth can close or open the borders to Sithicus. While the borders are closed, purple flames fill the Mists surrounding Sithicus. A creature that enters the Mists for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there takes 5 ({@dice 1d10}) Fire damage plus 5 ({@dice 1d10}) Necrotic damage.

items.name: Fearless Soldiers
items.entry: Undead allies of Lord Soth have Immunity to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

items.name: Guilt of Sithicus
items.entry: Few sleep soundly in Sithicus. Whenever a creature other than Lord Soth and his allies finishes a Long Rest in the domain, the creature must succeed on a {@dc 10} Constitution saving throw or gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.

entries: If Lord Soth dies, these effects end immediately.

entries.name: Lord Soth's Lair

entries.title: Nedragaard Keep

entries: Lord Soth rules from Nedragaard Keep, a twisted, copy of his accursed fortress on Krynn, Dargaard Keep. Nedragaard Keep grows and withers over time like a living thing. Undead soldiers patrolling the keep regularly forget their duties or collapse into lifelessness. The disorder within the rigid death knight's fortress endlessly frustrates Lord Soth.

entries.name: Nedragaard Keep
entries: Nedragaard Keep is a towerlike fortress of crumbling, flame-scarred stone. Prominent locations in the keep are depicted on {@book map 2.31|RHW|2|Map 2.31: Nedragaard Keep} and have the following features:

items.name: Gatehouse
items.entry: Undead soldiers guard the gates to Nedragaard Keep, demanding to know trespassers' identities. Those who identify themselves with the proper passphrase, such as "a seeker of the rose" or "a servant of Nightlund's lord," are admitted. Stairs beyond the gate climb several floors to the top of the keep's walls.

items.name: Garden
items.entry: Gardens on the keep's second floor are overgrown with thorny plants—use the rules for {@hazard razorvine|XDMG} from the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|XDMG}. From here spread massive vines that bloom with black roses and strangle the entire castle.

items.name: Throne Room
items.entry: High in the keep is Soth's throne room. Here Lord Soth spends much of his time dwelling on failures and twisted fantasies. Thirteen {@creature Banshee|XMM|Banshees} haunt the keep, and at any time, at least three linger here, singing of Soth's disgrace.

entries.name: Adventures in Sithicus
entries: Adventures set in Sithicus feature mysterious shadows, grim ruins, and strange foes from the warped memories of Lord Soth. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

entries.name: Horror in Sithicus
entries: Adventures in Sithicus often include the following elements.

entries.name: Cataclysmic Flames
entries: Illusory violet flames burn across Sithicus—fires from the Cataclysm that devastated Krynn. Powerful shadows guard prominent, bonfire-like pillars of these flames. These shadows might use the stat blocks of any monster, but they resemble dragons, knights, and figures from Soth's past.

Whenever someone defeats one of these guardians, the flame it protected reveals an illusory scene from the Darklord's past before vanishing. Any character who witnesses such a scene learns something of Soth's history, the world he comes from, or the knighthood he once belonged to. One of the viewers then gains the {@reward Charm of the Black Rose|RHW} (see "{@book Supernatural Gifts|XDMG|2|Supernatural Gifts}" in the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|XDMG} for additional details).

entries.name: Influencing Lord Soth
entries: Lord Soth is typically {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to strangers, but characters can adjust his attitude to {@variantrule Indifferent [Attitude]|XPHB|Indifferent} by succeeding on a {@dc 20} Charisma ({@skill Deception|XPHB} or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check. Soth might employ characters he deems worthy on quests, such as the hunt for the ghost Caradoc. Making a second successful {@dc 20} Charisma ({@skill Deception|XPHB} or {@skill Persuasion|XPHB}) check while offering Soth something he covets can improve Soth's attitude to {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} for a short time, during which he might let others leave his domain.

entries.name: Living Shadows
entries: Shadows in Sithicus often act with a life of their own. Roll on or choose a result from the Shadows of Sithicus table to inspire these shapes' behavior.

entries.caption: Shadows of Sithicus
rows: An elf desperately offering a swaddled bundle.
Glimpses of skeletal warriors or horses.
Knights in battle or in the midst of a siege.
Peasants being consumed by shadowy flames.
Solemn elf priests being ambushed by ogres.

entries.name: Sithicus Campaign Arc
entries: Campaigns set in Sithicus often involve trying to gain Lord Soth's favor and win escape from the domain. A campaign here might follow this outline:

items.name: Levels 1–4
items.entry: Azrael Dax, Lord Soth's werebadger seneschal, brusquely recruits the characters to hunt down deadly shadows. These shadows take the form of and behave as noble knights.

items.name: Level 5–9
items.entry: Impressed by the characters, Azrael sends them to Inza Kulchevich to ask the witch to use her powers to find Lord Soth's elusive former servant, Caradoc. Inza can do this, but first she requires the characters to collect the cataclysmic flame from three ruins across the domain in a magical iron pendant. Each flame is guarded by a shadow creature that resembles some foe from Soth's twisted memories—like a lance-wielding {@creature Warrior Commander|XMM} or a pompous {@creature Archpriest|XMM}.

items.name: Levels 10–14
items.entry: Inza uses the pendant to reveal that Caradoc's soul is trapped in a black sapphire that Azrael already possesses. The characters must recover the gem. They might then infiltrate Nedragaard Keep to offer it to {@creature Lord Soth of Sithicus|RHW|Lord Soth}. Soth is fickle though, and this approach might end in a confrontation with the death knight.

entries.name: Shadow of the Black Rose
entries: {@i Adventure for Level 14 Characters}

items.name: Situation
items.entry: The characters seek to escape Sithicus or the Domains of Dread.

items.name: Hook
items.entry: Inza Kulchevich (Medium, Neutral Evil {@creature Death Cultist|XMM}) offers to tell the characters how to escape Sithicus if they recover a magical gem from Lord Soth's seneschal, Azrael Dax.

entries.name: Encounters
entries: The adventure consists of these encounters.

entries.name: Inza's Quest
entries: The witch Inza loathes Lord Soth. She knows he has tasked Azrael with recovering a magical gem that holds the spirit of the Darklord's treacherous former ally, the knight Caradoc. Inza gives the characters an iron pendant bearing an image of a rose that's missing its central gem. She asks them to follow Azrael to a ruin in the Misttop Mountains, where he hunts the missing gem, and recover it before him.

entries.name: Inza's Secret
entries: Inza doesn't share that she seeks to release Caradoc to provoke Soth into a conflict with the characters.

entries.name: Searching the Ruins
entries: The ruins where Azrael hunts for the gem appear on map 2.31. For every 10 minutes the characters are exploring, roll {@dice 1d6} on the table below for an encounter. For each previous encounter rolled on this table, add 1 to the result.

entries.caption: Encounters in the Ruins
rows: {@creature Death Knight Aspirant|XMM} and two {@creature Banshee|XMM|Banshees}
A {@creature Gulthias Blight|XMM} covered in black roses

entries.name: Kender Nosferatu
entries: A morbidly whimsical pair of {@creature Vampire Nosferatu|RHW} pesters the characters, hoping to be entertained. If the characters don't keep them amused, the nosferatu attack. Because these monsters were kender in life—mischievous folk from the world of Krynn—they are Small.

entries.name: Shadow Highlord
entries: A dragon rider made of shadows guards a pillar of illusory violet flame. It reflects one of Lord Soth's warped memories and uses the stat blocks of a {@creature Dullahan|RHW} (see {@area chapter 5|3a1|x}) riding a {@creature Young Blue Dragon|XMM}, followed by a withered {@creature Death's Head|RHW}. The monsters are {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile}. If they're defeated, the nearby pillar of flames shows a scene of Lord Soth being knighted and grants a character the benefits noted in "{@area Cataclysmic Flames|25a|x}." If you roll this encounter a second time, treat it as a meeting with Azrael Dax.

entries.name: Azrael Dax
entries: Azrael Dax is Lord Soth's dwarf seneschal and a murderous werebadger (use the {@creature Werebear|XMM} stat block). Azrael and the six {@creature Strahd Skeleton|RHW|Strahd Skeletons} (see {@area chapter 5|3bb|x}) attending him are Hostile. If reduced to 50 Hit Points or fewer, Azrael bargains for his life and offers the characters the black sapphire they're looking for, which he's been hiding from Soth for moons. After this, don't roll for more encounters in the ruins.

entries.name: Repairing the Pendant
entries: Place the black gem in Inza's iron amulet releases Caradoc (Neutral Evil {@creature Ghost|XMM}). The arrogant creature is {@variantrule Indifferent [Attitude]|XPHB|Indifferent} to the characters. If they don't free Caradoc, Inza does when the group returns the gem and pendant to her.

entries.name: Soth Appears
entries: Three rounds after Caradoc is freed, {@creature Lord Soth of Sithicus|RHW} (see earlier in {@area this chapter|252|x}) appears from the shadows. Soth fights Caradoc and mistakes the characters for the ghost's allies. A character might convince him otherwise—see "{@area Influencing Lord Soth|25c|x}."

entries.name: Escape
entries: Characters who flee or defeat Lord Soth gain an additional 6,200 XP. Those who change the Darklord's attitude to {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} or help him defeat Caradoc can convince him to let them leave Sithicus. Defeating Soth also causes the domain's borders to open. If the characters confront Inza about her method for leaving Sithicus, she cryptically explains that the only route to do so leads through Soth, encouraging them to seek the Darklord's aid or confront the death knight again.

entries.name: Tepest
entries: {@i Domain of Nature's Cruel Secrets}

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: {@area Mother Lorinda|270|x}

items.name: Adventure Genre:
items.entry: {@area Folk horror|369|x}

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Fey bargains, rural cults, secret sacrifices, sinister festivals

items.name: Mist Talismans:
items.entry: Bloodstained farm implement, dried crown of white camellias, straw doll

items.name: Aligned Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Druid

items.name: Opposed Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Executioner

entries: All is well in Tepest. Fields shimmer with bountiful harvests, and horned village children recite sing-song rhymes. But those who linger among this land's pastures and colorful cottages can't escape the feeling of being watched or the impression that strange forms move amid the fields. Locals dismiss such worries as the work of fey tricksters, but their smiles fail to mask the desperation in their eyes.

Brutality wears a welcoming face in Tepest, a truth embodied by the ancient hag Lorinda, who betrayed her coven in pursuit of adoration. Taking the guise of a deity called Mother, {@creature Mother Lorinda|RHW|Lorinda} adopted the village of Viktal, protecting its people from nature's whims so they can worship her even as they sacrifice themselves to feed her monstrous offspring. In the wilderness beyond, resentful fey watch and plot, offering cruel bargains to those who stray beyond Mother's sight. The people of Viktal do what they must to survive, using tradition to cloak their fear and complicity in a cycle of murder. Strangers become symbols of hope for Viktal's citizens—welcome alternatives to slaughtering their families in Mother's name.

entries.name: Domain Features
entries: Tepest is known for the following features:

items.name: Children of the Fey
items.entry: Few children are born in Viktal. Would-be parents must bargain with Mother or the fey to bring them a child.

items.name: Fey Threats
items.entry: Caves dot the land, connecting to depths where dangerous fey dwell. The people of Viktal fear them and blame them for every ill.

items.name: The Last Village
items.entry: Viktal is the only community in Tepest. The others displeased Mother and suffered terrible fates.

items.name: Mother
items.entry: Viktal's residents devoutly follow the optimistic faith of the nature deity called Mother.

items.name: Mother's Minders
items.entry: Mother's most zealous faithful are called Mother's Minders. These devotees sacrifice their left eyes to Mother to show their faith.

items.name: The Tithe
items.entry: Viktal's festivals include a fertility celebration called the Tithe that occurs once a season.

entries.name: Locations in Tepest
entries: The valley of Tepest is rocky, ill-suited to farming, and plagued by dangerous fey. Nevertheless, those who worship Mother are protected from the worst of the land's dangers. Mother's Sight and Viktal are detailed below, and other notable locations are summarized on the {@book Tepest Locations table|RHW|2|Tepest Locations}.

entries.name: Mother's Sight
entries: A mile outside Viktal, in a field apart from the surrounding farms, stands Mother's Sight: an array of rune-etched standing stones. These stones rise as tall as thirty feet, and several have pieces that defy gravity. Within the central and largest stone floats a disk reminiscent of a great eye.

Here the people come to worship, celebrate, and sacrifice. Colorful crafts adorn long tables, where the villagers play games and feast in celebration of Mother's bounty. Meals here are tasty, and guests are encouraged to eat and drink beyond their fill.
During rituals here, Viktal's community leaders give thanks to Mother before a dramatic fire surrounded by an eight-foot scaffold. (See "{@area The Tithe|27a|x}" adventure for additional details.)

entries.name: Viktal
entries: Mother ensures peace in Viktal. The village consists of a few dozen cottages, a market square, and an inn on the banks of Lake Kronov. Wicker scarecrows guard the rich fields blessed by Mother, and friendly people go about their daily lives. Cheery villagers welcome visitors, show them hospitality, and demonstrate interest in their stories.

entries.caption: Tepest Locations
rows: Mysterious valleys hide amid the forested Arkenheights mountains. Each is home to territorial fey who know the strange histories and mystical properties that linger in each.
A gigantic evil {@creature Treant|XMM} called Blackroot roots amid the stone circles of an ancient order of druids. Blackroot hoards the druids' magic and protects its glade with its deadly saplings.
This ruined village of Briggdarrow is overgrown by a toxic, vision-inducing fungus. Eerie fungal beings emerge from the ruins by night to spread the infestation beyond the village.
A ruined fortress covers most of Cas Island. Within it lies a mysterious magical relic, but the waters around the island are guarded by a notorious, crocodilian lake monster, the Avanc.
A fey witch drowned in this pool long ago. Now, any corpse bathed in its magical waters returns to life—but the spirit inhabiting the body never matches the original owner's.
People-eating goblins lurk in the Goblinwood. They attack trespassers, but a curse affecting the goblins prevents them from seeing anyone wearing a cap of a certain color.
The walled village of Kellee once worshipped Mother, but something went wrong. Now, Lorinda's forsaken followers haunt the place, and neither she nor her current worshippers dare approach.
By day, this idyllic valley is serene and filled with friendly animals. At night, the ground turns to mud and brambles, from which rise the transformed victims of fey curses.
Beneath Mount Arak lies a hidden realm of the fey. The goodly Queen Maeve and wicked Prince Loht jointly rule their people. The fey resent the surface folk for ancient slights.
Fey creatures avoid this sturdy, welcoming Nobody's Inn. Few travel so far from Viktal, but those who do are welcomed by Bryonna, the inn's gregarious but unnerving skeleton proprietor.
The mountain lake called Seer's Glass has prophetic powers. Those who gaze on their reflection in it see their past by day and their future by night.

entries.name: Mother Lorinda
entries: {@i Darklord Who Demands Adoration}
What precocious moppets you are! Little ones who show Mother love get rewarded, but bad children get punished. Which sorts of tots are you?

Mother Lorinda desires a family over all else, not realizing she's destroyed every family she's ever had. A creator of monstrosities, this ancient green hag obsesses over unnatural maternalistic pursuits.

Over generations, Tepest's villagers have come to view Lorinda not as a powerful witch, but as a god of the land and fertility called Mother. Lorinda doesn't dissuade her fragile mortal followers from their baseless cult practices. The villagers provide willing sacrifices for the hag's flawed magical attempts to create life, and their adoration gradually soothes the violent, magical outbursts that follow her failures.

Lorinda's walking tower of bones, the Gurgyl, ranges across Tepest. Those who seek her here find a distracted but deceptively cunning hag clinging to grotesque dolls of her family. She laments the loss of her sisters, Laveeda and Leticia, never acknowledging that they remain alive as her prisoners. She often speaks of her daughter, Laoirse (pronounced "LUR-shuh")—the name she uses over and over again for each of her monstrous creations.

When Lorinda visits Viktal, she appears in her illusory guise as Mother, an ancient, powerful woman wearing a mothlike cloak and bearing a branch with burning limbs. She calls on the people to prove their love for her through sacrifice, whether of food and crafts, their left eyes, or their lives. Sometimes her arrival is sudden and terrifying, such as when she magically appears to crawl from one of her followers' empty eye sockets.
Lorinda seeks new ways to create a permanent family. This might involve great sacrifices, stolen fey magic, strange adoptions, or all manner of supernatural bargains.

entries.name: Mother Lorinda's Tale
entries: The sisters Lorinda, Laveeda, and Leticia Mindefisk lived desperate lives at the wooded edge of a terrible land. Their brutish family claimed the girls were unnatural fey in disguise and forced them to toil constantly. After years of harsh treatment from their cruel father and brothers, the hopeless sisters snapped and killed an abusive traveler during a chance encounter. They butchered the body and served the remains to their unwitting family. Thus began a cycle of murder and cannibalism that persisted for years.

The Mindefisk sisters' crimes were finally revealed when they all fell in love with a charming stranger. They squabbled, and unwilling to relent or share, they murdered the stranger in full view of their horrified family. With nothing left to lose, the sisters killed the rest of their family as the Mists rose around them.

The horror of the Mindefisk sisters only grew in the Domains of Dread. Finding themselves in Tepest, the sisters discovered their new nature as hags. Over generations, mortals and fey alike came to know them as fickle miracle workers. Despairing families came to them often, willing to trade much for {@race hexblood|RHW} children born from the hags' magic.

Eventually, Lorinda grew fascinated by others' pursuit of family. She entreated her sisters to help her create a child, but Laveeda and Leticia refused, loathing the idea of any one of them having greater happiness than the others.

Defying her sisters, Lorinda assembled a monstrosity of animal parts and foul magic in secret. With her new daughter's help, the hag trapped her sisters inside their magic cauldron and stole their powers. Yet Lorinda's joy with her creation was short—the monster's magic quickly waned, and it rotted away. Ever since, Lorinda has sought to create a family of her own, producing countless monstrosities in experiments and approaching the villagers of Viktal in her guise as Mother. Over generations, this has given rise to the cult of the Mother in Viktal, but it has brought Lorinda no closer to attaining the lasting family connection she covets.

entries.name: Mother Lorinda's Domain
entries: Tepest is altered by Lorinda's presence, creating the following effects:

items.name: Closing the Borders
items.entry: While in her domain, Mother Lorinda can close or open the borders to Tepest at will. While the borders are closed, a violent storm fills the Mists surrounding Tepest. A creature that enters the Mists for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must succeed on a {@dc 15} Strength saving throw or take {@damage 2d8} Lightning damage and be pushed up to 30 feet straight toward Tepest. A creature makes this save only once per turn.

items.name: Mother's Watchers
items.entry: Creatures that have sacrificed an eye to Mother can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and pray to her. When they do so, Lorinda can perceive through the creature's senses as well as her own until the end of the creature's next turn. When perceiving through a creature's senses, Lorinda benefits from any special senses it has.

items.name: Sun and Storms
items.entry: While in Tepest, Lorinda can cast {@spell Control Weather|XPHB}, requiring no Material components and using the same spellcasting ability as Spellcasting. When casting the spell this way, Lorinda can control the weather within 5 miles of herself, regardless of whether she is inside or outside.

entries: If Lorinda dies, these effects end immediately.

entries.name: Mother Lorinda's Lair
entries: Ranging across the domain is Mother Lorinda's home, the Gurgyl. This towerlike structure of thorns, wicker, and giants' bones walks on three skeletal dragon legs.

entries.name: The Gurgyl
entries: The villagers of Viktal know the Gurgyl serves their god in some way, but sighting it portends bad luck. The site has the following features:

items.name: Kitchen-Laboratory
items.entry: The Darklord concocts foul creation magic in this central chamber.

items.name: Nursery-Oubliette
items.entry: This room is home to whichever monster Lorinda calls her daughter, Laoirse.

items.name: Old Cauldron
items.entry: Lorinda's hag sisters languish in this plugged, oversize pot.

entries.name: Adventures in Tepest
entries: Adventures set in Tepest feature rural cults, deceptive fey, and dread of nature's mysteries. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

entries.title: Celebrants parade from Viktal to Mother's Sight to honor their god on the day of the Tithe

entries.name: Horror in Tepest
entries: Adventures in Tepest often include the following elements.

entries.name: Strangeness in Viktal
entries: The locals seem welcoming and vigorous in their worship of the nature deity, Mother. The village leader, Wyan, guides them in their faith, assisted by elders called Mother's Minders—all of whom are missing their left eye.

Residents invite visitors to stay at the village's only inn, the Fisher's Rest, free of charge for as long as they please. They encourage new arrivals to linger and participate in their upcoming festival, called the Tithe (detailed later in {@area this section|27a|x}).

entries.name: Mother's Children
entries: Multiple times a year, Lorinda goes through a cycle of trying to magically create a new child. She calls this monstrous being Laoirse and dotes on it for a time. She believes that sacrifices to Laoirse during the Tithe can extend her child's life, but inevitably, the monster's magic fades, and it becomes mud and bones once more. For weeks afterward, Lorinda remains inconsolable, and violent storms and strange disasters plague Tepest.

Lorinda's children take varied forms. To create such a monster, choose a stat block appropriate to challenge your characters, then consider how that creature might appear if it were made from different parts. For example, a {@creature Minotaur of Baphomet|XMM} or {@creature Werewolf|XMM} might be a bipedal mixture of ram, stag, and wolf parts, while a {@creature Shambling Mound|XMM} might be a person made of wicker. These descriptions should seem outlandish but reflective of the creature's traits and actions. And no matter how bizarre her children are, Lorinda loves them all the same.

entries.name: Tepest Campaign Arc
entries: Campaigns set in Tepest often involve Viktal's cult and the dangerous spirits of the wilds. A campaign here might follow this outline:

items.name: Levels 1–2
items.entry: Characters find themselves welcomed in Viktal and encouraged to take part in the Tithe (see the adventure below). At the rite's conclusion, the villagers try to sacrifice one or more characters to one of Mother's children. The characters defeat the monster, earning the enmity of both the villagers and Mother Lorinda.

items.name: Level 3–5
items.entry: Fey creatures or druids who have been victims of Lorinda aid the characters; they reveal that she is no god, but a powerful hag who wanders the land in a walking tower called the Gurgyl. They send the group on a quest to learn from a mystical site or elder forest spirit how to infiltrate the hag's lair.

items.name: Levels 6–8
items.entry: After learning how to infiltrate the Gurgyl, the characters locate and break into Lorinda's lair, finding some of the hag's bizarre creations and her diminished, imprisoned sisters. When they emerge from the mobile fortress, they find themselves near Viktal, where {@creature Mother Lorinda|RHW|Lorinda} confronts them, backed by her fanatical followers.

items.name: Levels 9+
items.entry: If the group defeats Lorinda, the land spirals into chaos as fey from Mount Arak run wild. The characters might become involved in a power struggle between the good and evil fey of Arak. Lorinda's return might drive the fey back—but the Darklord will help only if someone becomes her new child.

entries.name: The Tithe
entries: {@i Adventure for Level 2 Characters}

items.name: Situation
items.entry: The characters participate in a religious celebration, at the end of which they are poised to become sacrifices.

items.name: Hook
items.entry: The human leader of Viktal, Wyan (Medium, Neutral Evil {@creature Priest|XMM}) invites the characters to participate in the Tithe, a rite honoring their god, Mother.

entries.name: Encounters
entries: This adventure begins the day of the Tithe, after {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly} villagers from Viktal ({@creature Commoner|XMM|Commoners}) lead the characters to Mother's Sight. It uses map 2.33 and consists of these encounters.

entries.name: Tithe Competitions
entries: Wyan directs the assemblage in a prayer to Mother, and the day of games begins. Characters each can play three games, participating in each selected game once. During each game, a character makes the ability check listed on the table below three times. Those who succeed on the check two or more times win the game; if they fail, a villager wins.

rows: Crooked Joust. Participants use stilts strapped to their arms and legs to topple one another.
Powrie Chase. Pursuers play tag in while wearing tattered red cloaks.
Lost Siblings. Blindfolded participants identify other players by touching their hair.
Gossamer Glutton. Whoever eats the most live moths wins.
Never Naughty. Participants flatter elderly villagers, who decide who wins and who gets paddled.

entries: Characters who don't play a game might socialize and see some benign oddity extrapolated from the details presented earlier in this section.

entries.name: Competition Winners
entries: The day's games draw to a close at dusk. Wyan names a character who won two or more games as Mother's Favorite and crowns them with a garland of white camellias. If no character won this many games, a human villager named Lorelei (Medium {@creature Commoner|XMM}) wins the crown and invites them to sit with her at the head table during the feast.

entries.name: Feast
entries: Wyan begins the feast with another prayer as the Mother's Minders light a grand bonfire within a tall scaffold. During the feast, villagers encourage the characters eat and drink to excess. A character who does so must succeed on a {@dc 10} Constitution saving throw or gain the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for the next 6 hours.

entries.name: The Sacrifice
entries: At midnight, standing before the scaffold (see {@book map 2.33|RHW|2|Map 2.33: Mother's Sight}), Wyan announces, "Now we prove our undying love for Mother and beg her for another season of bounty. Bring forth our sacrifice!" Mother's Favorite receives a bowl of food and tiny crafts and is instructed to take it to the bonfire.

If Lorelei is Mother's Favorite, she obeys and approaches the bonfire. If a character is Mother's Favorite, they choose whether to approach. Regardless, 2 rounds later, a biped with wolflike features and stag's horns (use the {@creature Minotaur of Baphomet|XMM} stat block) emerges from the field nearest Mother's Favorite. This is one of Lorinda's monstrous children. It fights Mother's Favorite (and anyone else who damages it) to the death. During combat, the villagers raise their voices in a hymn but stay out of the conflict. Lorelei smiles and doesn't defend herself if attacked.

entries.name: Conclusion
entries: If the monster dies, Wyan and the villagers are horrified that the Tithe went wrong. They turn {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile}, blaming the characters and demanding they leave. Mother Lorinda learns of this and seeks revenge.

If the monster wasn't killed, the villagers remain {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Friendly}, but their true nature becomes clear. Mother Lorinda also takes an interest in the characters, potentially seeking to adopt them as her new children.
Regardless of how the Tithe concludes, the characters gain 200 XP.

entries.name: Valachan
entries: {@i Domain of the Hunter}

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: {@area Chakuna|287|x}

items.name: Genres:
items.entry: {@area Gothic horror|37b|x} and {@area slasher horror|38a|x}

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Deadly predators, death traps, hostile wilderness, survival games

items.name: Mist Talismans:
items.entry: Displacer beast skin, poisonous flower blossom, rusty foot trap

items.name: Aligned Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Shepherd

items.name: Opposed Tarokka Card:
items.entry: Darklord

entries: In the wild domain of Valachan, only the deadliest predators survive. Bestial hunters, carnivorous plants, and ravenous monsters stalk the dense rainforests, but the land's greatest threat is its Darklord: the devious, undying hunter {@creature Chakuna|RHW}.

Chakuna stalks the jungle, testing herself against the domain's deadliest beasts. Yet little delights her more than discovering strangers to her realm—visitors who soon realize they've become the Darklord's newest quarry. Chakuna forces her victims to participate in the Trial of Hearts, a race through the wilderness to remote sanctuaries. Participants face deadly traps and all the dangers of the wilds, including Chakuna herself. Those who reach the goal at the end of the trial are permitted to leave Valachan. Those who don't reach it die, either due to the deadly wilderness or at the hands of Chakuna herself.

entries.name: Domain Features
entries: Valachan is known for the following features:

items.name: Displacer Beast Packs
items.entry: Packs of {@creature Displacer Beast|XMM|Displacer Beasts} roam the jungle, led by Yana, a preternaturally large and cunning displacer beast that serves Chakuna.

items.name: Natural Hunters
items.entry: Wild plants and creatures in Valachan usually seem hostile toward strangers. Most creatures here are carnivorous even if elsewhere they aren't.

items.name: Trial of Hearts
items.entry: The deadly {@area Trial of Hearts|28e|x} is a battle royale that starts on the night of the full moon. Chakuna chooses participants.

items.name: Wilderness Domain
items.entry: Valachan is covered by a fantastically dangerous rainforest. Visitors face constant perils, but the domain's residents flourish.

entries.name: Locations in Valachan
entries: Valachan is lush and wild. Rare creatures and plants reign here, as do villages of people who understand and thrive in the rainforest. No roads pass through the land, but ships from other domains sometimes wreck on these shores. The settlement of Oselo and the Pantara Lodge are described below, and other notable locations are summarized on the {@book Valachan Locations table|RHW|2|Valachan Locations}.

entries.name: Oselo
entries: The former home of Darklord Chakuna, Oselo is a prosperous village that welcomes visitors. When night falls, the residents change, shifting from people into panthers, ocelots, tigers, and other hunting cats. These shape-shifters hunt animals for food and do battle with other peoples only to defend their home. Most fear Chakuna and want no involvement with the Trial of Hearts, fearing the Darklord's wrath. However, in rare instances, Oselo's shape-shifters might be convinced to guide, hide, or otherwise aid participants in the Trial. This displeases Chakuna, but she respects the decisions of her people's elders. The villagers might adopt strangers deemed worthy, but outsiders first must prove themselves in an initiation ceremony focused on surviving unarmed in the forest for a week.

entries.name: Pantara Lodge
entries: The Pantara Lodge is a series of thatched structures strung together high in the forest canopy, suspended above the overgrown ruins of a burned castle. This is Chakuna's home. Its interconnected treehouses include a great hall, the Darklord's personal quarters, an armory, an infirmary, and several guest lodges. The Darklord's {@creature Displacer Beast|XMM} and {@creature Panther|XMM} allies prowl the trees amid the lodge's structures and can enter them freely.

entries.caption: Valachan Locations
rows: Within this cavern, a sapient {@creature Giant Spider|XMM} presides over representatives of countless insect species. Those seeking insect allies might entreat these councilors for aid.
Dinosaurs and megafauna seem common in the Forest of Fossils. The region's most notorious predator is Mother Heartless, a heavily scarred {@creature Tyrannosaurus Rex|XMM}.

The steep walls of the forested ravine called the Gasping Way keep the forest below in near-constant shadow. {@hazard Quicksand Pit|XDMG|Quicksand}, common along this route, acts with deadly swiftness.
People say the lake called Poison's Taste is filled with the venom of a giant, sleeping snake. Most who drink from the lake die—but those who survive become immune to nearly all poisons.

Sharks, {@creature Plesiosaurus|XMM|Plesiosaurs}, giant aquatic snakes, and other waterborne menaces battle each other constantly in Sangui Cove. Deep below lie the wrecks of ships, some laden with fantastic wealth.

The human warriors of the village of Shuaran value honor above all. They avoid Chakuna and might help her foes or those who offer aid against the deadly howling {@creature Ape|XMM|Apes} that dwell nearby.

Noisy animals and pests are common in the Sleepless Forest. Anything that dies here reanimates as a {@creature Zombie|XMM} the following night, shambles away, and collapses in a quieter resting place.
Skeleton-covered ruins jut from the center of the Starving Water. The deadly waters frequently release gases in limnic eruptions capable of suffocating nearby creatures.

An {@creature Aboleth|XMM} named Apez-Zos lurks within bizarre ruins beneath the lake called the Throne of Eels. It has enthralled nearby lizardfolk communities into serving it and seeks to supplant Chakuna.
The predators in the Weeping Rainforest have a sadistic streak, grievously wounding their prey, then leaving it to struggle in high trees. The cries of the wounded ring through the canopy.
Within the canyon of Yaguara's Heart lies a temple, beneath which sprawl blood-painted caverns. Hidden inside is Chakuna's beating heart, which binds her to the land.

entries.name: Chakuna
entries: {@i Darklord Who Hunts to Survive}
You seem capable, but do you have the heart of a hunter?
Chakuna is Valachan's deadliest hunter, a youthful werepanther with the intensity and calm of a veteran executioner. Few can elude her cold cunning, deadly traps, and packs of feline allies.

Chakuna is no mere murderer. She hunts because she must to protect the people of Oselo from a more terrible fate. To that end, she prefers to target strangers in her land, forcing them to participate in the Trial of Hearts. She relishes the chance to hunt the proud and disrespectful, as well as the skilled and unpredictable. Chakuna knows no mercy, though, and even those she comes to respect eventually face her blade. She spares the people of Oselo alone, even as her peoples' fear of her grows.

entries.name: Chakuna's Tale
entries: For untold generations, Darklord Urik von Kharkov ruled Valachan from Castle Pantara and hunted the people of Valachan for sport. Yearly, the Darklord's hunts grew in carnage, with his favorite prey being the shape-shifters of Oselo. Von Kharkov viewed himself as the domain's greatest hunter, a title he confirmed every time he slaughtered one of Oselo's supernatural warriors.

Chakuna was a werepanther warrior of Oselo and, like most of her people, was a skillful hunter. Seeing that von Kharkov eventually would slay her entire village, she swore to save Oselo. Realizing it would take a monster to slay a monster, she left her people and sought the deepest secrets of her deadly homeland. When she returned, she volunteered to let von Kharkov hunt her. During the murderous game that followed, Chakuna burned Castle Pantara and took the former Darklord's head.

Valachan is no mundane land, though. A Domain of Dread demands a Darklord, and Valachan will be ruled only by its greatest hunter. Refusing to let von Kharkov return, Chakuna made a pact with the domain itself. Within the depths of the mazelike Yaguara Mountains, Chakuna ripped her own heart from her chest and consumed the heart of Urik von Kharkov. Doing so, she stole von Kharkov's powers. Yet to retain them, she must feed the land constantly with the blood of the hunted. If she doesn't, the plants and animals of Valachan grow murderous and howl for von Kharkov's return. So Chakuna continues a cycle of bloodshed, embracing the violence of her peoples' onetime oppressor to ensure her bitter domination of her vicious realm.

entries.name: Chakuna's Domain
entries: Valachan is altered by Chakuna's presence, creating the following effects.

items.name: Closing the Borders
items.entry: While in her domain, Chakuna can close or open the borders to Valachan at will. While the borders are closed, shadowy beasts skulk or soar amid the Mists surrounding Valachan. A creature that enters the Mists for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there takes {@damage 1d10} Piercing damage.

items.name: Heightened Senses
items.entry: Beasts and Monstrosities have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom checks.

items.name: Whispering Shadows
items.entry: Humanoids subtract {@dice 1d8} from any ability checks they make when they take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action.

entries: If Chakuna dies, these effects end immediately.

entries.name: Chakuna's Lair
entries: Built above the overgrown ruins of von Kharkov's Castle Pantara, the Pantara Lodge is a maze of rope bridges, mighty boughs, and structures built among the branches of gigantic trees. Chakuna leaves those who die in the {@area Trial of Hearts|28e|x} without demonstrating skill or honor dangling from the branches of her home.

entries.name: Pantara Lodge
entries: The Pantara Lodge rises 80 feet above the rainforest floor. The site is depicted on {@book map 2.35|RHW|2|Map 2.35: Pantara Lodge} and has the following features:

items.name: Chakuna's Chambers
items.entry: In her quarters, Chakuna studies lore from other realms, fruitlessly seeking ways to placate her domain without further bloodshed. Secret doors hidden here lead to her meditation chamber and a room where the undying head of Urik von Kharkov spits curses and plots his return.

items.name: Great Hall and Banquet Room
items.entry: Chakuna welcomes guests in the lodge's great hall. Prior to the Trial of Hearts every moon, she hosts a lavish meal for her prey.

items.name: Trophy Gallery
items.entry: The trophy gallery holds the skulls of deadly beasts, the weapons of those who failed to murder Chakuna, and the stuffed corpses of the Darklord's rivals—animals and people alike.

entries.name: Adventures in Valachan
entries: Adventures set in Valachan involve merciless wilderness, predatory animals, and deadly hunts. Use this section to develop horror adventures in this domain.

entries.title: Lethal traps and hidden snares threaten participants in the Trial of Hearts

entries.name: Horror in Valachan
entries: Adventures in Valachan often include the following elements.

entries.name: Trial of Hearts Rules
entries: Visitors to Valachan likely are forced to participate in the Trial of Hearts, a dangerous game where the domain's Darklord hunts them. On the night of the full moon, {@creature Chakuna|RHW} selects those she considers worthy to participate. At the Pantara Lodge, she shows her guests every courtesy, provides them with a map of the domain, and sets out these rules for the trial:

items: Participants can divide into small groups or go it alone.
Once groups are established at the trial's start, alliances between contenders aren't permitted.
Participants must reach one of two shrines: either on Kiru Island or between the lakes called the Scars atop Anquara Plateau.

Participants can leave the Pantara Lodge at dawn. The following dusk, Chakuna pursues the contenders with hunting partners of her choice, attempting to slay any participants she encounters outside a shrine.
Only one person or group can occupy a shrine.
Participants can kill one another for any reason—particularly to take a shrine.
The trial doesn't stop for any reason until Chakuna arrives at both shrines and acknowledges any winners there.
The Darklord's servants escort winners to the village of Shuaran, and from there, out of Valachan.
Losers rot where they fall.
Rule breakers are killed.

entries: Chakuna wants the trial to be challenging. If she catches a competitor too soon, she might release them if they sacrifice an ally, a limb, or a valuable weapon. Anyone who dies in the trial feeds the domain's bloodlust—even those who fall to a threat other than the Darklord herself.

entries.name: Valachan Exploration
entries: Valachan is a dense and deadly wilderness. When creating adventures here, refer to {@book chapter 2|XDMG|1|Running Exploration} of the {@book Dungeon Master's Guide|XDMG} for details on travel and journeys with stages.

Characters use the rules in the "{@book Travel Pace|XPHB|1|Travel Pace}" section of the {@book Player's Handbook|XPHB}. Valachan's forests prevent a party from traveling at faster than a Normal pace, and their density makes them {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}, reducing travel pace by half. This means most characters can move through the domain at a Normal pace of 1½ miles per hour and 12 miles per day.

entries.name: Flying
entries: Difficult Terrain slows flying creatures in Valachan, due to the strong winds above the forest canopy. Flying creatures are also easy to spot, attracting the attention of {@creature Chimera|XMM|Chimeras}, {@creature Pteranodon|XMM|Pteranodons}, and condor-like {@creature Roc|XMM|Rocs}. Chakuna might stipulate that flight is off-limits during the Trial of Hearts. If she does, the Mists aid in enforcing this rule.

entries.name: Navigation
entries: It's easy to get lost in Valachan's jungles. Unless they use gorges and waterways for guidance, characters must make {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Survival|XPHB}) checks to avoid getting lost. Those who fail spend {@dice 1d4} hours wandering and risk encountering deadly creatures (see "{@area Trial of Hearts|295|x}" for suggestions). Characters should make this check once during each stage of a journey, or after dramatic or disorienting encounters.

entries.name: Trial Route: Kiru Island
entries: An approximately 20-mile route from the Pantara Lodge to Kiru Island runs along the gorge south of the lodge to the Eirubamba River, then along the coast to Kiru Island. At a Normal pace, this route takes a day and a half (13 hours) to complete. Roughly 10 miles of this route follows the river. Characters who use a raft can move at Fast pace along the river. Rapids make the river {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}, but even so, moving at this pace reduces the journey to just over a day (11 hours).

entries.name: Trial Route: The Scars
entries: Participants in the Trial of Hearts who travel from the Pantara Lodge to the Scars on Anquara Plateau have a route of approximately 20 miles if they follow the Shadowtaker Lakes east to the river called Killer's Road, then ascend Anquara Plateau. Characters can follow most of this route at a Normal pace, but 2 miles involve climbing into the mountains—a Slow pace through Difficult Terrain, reducing movement to 1 mile per hour. This route takes over a day and a half (14 hours) to complete.

entries.title: Displacer beasts and other deadly feline hunters stalk the bridges of the Pantara Lodge

entries.name: Valachan Campaign Arc
entries: Campaigns set in Valachan often feature the Trial of Hearts and Chakuna's connection to her domain. A campaign here might follow this outline:

items.name: Levels 1–3
items.entry: Consider starting this campaign in another domain or world. During their travels, the characters become shipwrecked.

items.name: Level 4–6
items.entry: The characters wash up on the shores of Valachan. They must find ways to survive the rainforest and fend off a pack of {@creature Displacer Beast|XMM|Displacer Beasts} that stalks them. They eventually find shelter in Oselo or Shuaran.

items.name: Levels 7–9
items.entry: Having heard of their exploits, {@creature Chakuna|RHW} invites the characters to the Pantara Lodge, offering to help them leave if they participate in the Trial of Hearts (see the adventure below). Before the trial ensues, the characters might learn secrets from the head of Urik von Kharkov.

items.name: Levels 10+
items.entry: If the characters survive the trial, Chakuna helps them leave the domain. As they characters travel out of Valachan, locals from Shuaran or Oselo beg the characters to stay and slay Chakuna. Should the characters kill Chakuna, chaos erupts and Urik von Kharkov strives to retake power in Chakuna's absence.

entries.name: Trial of Hearts
entries: {@i Adventure for Level 8 Characters}

items.name: Situation
items.entry: To escape Valachan, the characters must participate in the Trial of Hearts.

items.name: Hook
items.entry: The hunter {@creature Chakuna|RHW} (see earlier in {@area this chapter|287|x}) invites characters to Pantara Lodge to discuss helping them leave the domain.

entries.name: Encounters
entries: The adventure uses {@book map 2.35|RHW|2|Map 2.35: Pantara Lodge} and consists of these encounters.

entries.name: Pantara Lodge
entries: Chakuna seems welcoming and polite, inviting characters to relax before a feast where she'll explain how to leave Valachan. Note the {@variantrule Indifferent [Attitude]|XPHB|Indifferent} {@creature Displacer Beast|XMM|Displacer Beasts} that prowl the lodge—Chakuna's guards and hunting companions. The characters receive a comfortable guest room where they can rest before dinner.

entries.name: Chakuna's Guests
entries: The characters aren't Chakuna's only guests. There are three other groups:

items.name: Lizardfolk
items.entry: Tolocwa ({@creature Lizardfolk Sovereign|XMM}) and three lizardfolk (Medium {@creature Warrior Veteran|XMM|Warrior Veterans}) are their tribe's best hunters.

items.name: Pirates
items.entry: The human Rakengel (Medium {@creature Pirate Captain|XMM}) and three {@creature Pirate|XMM|Pirates} from his crew were recently shipwrecked.

items.name: Thieves
items.entry: Aian, Citana, and Lesar are {@creature Weretiger|XMM|Weretigers} from Oselo who were caught stealing a rare herb.

entries.name: Trial Rules
entries: After Chakuna's guests have all gathered for a meal of vegetarian dishes and mineral water, Chakuna does the following:

items: Explains the trial, outlining the rules from the "{@area Trial of Hearts Rules|28e|x}" section.
Insists this is the only way to leave Valachan.
Notes that the group could attack her, but they would face her {@creature Displacer Beast|XMM} allies. If they want to fight, they should wait for the trial.

entries: The pirate group speaks up in protest, but growls from Chakuna's allies silence them. The Darklord answers questions, then encourages her guests to rest, as the trial starts at dawn. Those who leave before then violate the rules.

entries.name: Cheating
entries: Chakuna catches anyone who tries to leave the lodge early or otherwise cheat. She encourages them to obey the rules and calls on six {@creature Displacer Beast|XMM|Displacer Beasts} to enforce them. She calls the monsters off before anyone dies.

entries.name: Choosing a Route
entries: Have the characters choose a shrine as their destination and determine their route (see "{@area Valachan Exploration|28f|x}").

entries.name: Trial Encounters
entries: The trial is divided into three phases, including a night camping in the jungle. As you narrate the characters' journey through the wilderness, roll on the following table for an encounter that happens during each of the first two phases and another that occurs while camping.

entries.caption: Jungle Events
rows: An ambush by the thieves (see above).
Chakuna, who runs past covered in blood; then four {@creature Displacer Beast|XMM|Displacer Beasts} attack.
Flooding or rock falls. A character must succeed on a {@dc 15} Wisdom ({@skill Survival|XPHB}) check or 2 hours are added to the journey.
Four {@creature Swarm of Ravenous Insects|RHW|Swarms of Ravenous Insects}.*
Three {@creature Zombie Plague Spreader|RHW|Zombie Plague Spreaders}* or a {@creature Relentless Slasher|RHW}* that survived a previous trial.
A {@creature Hydra|XMM} or two {@creature Shambling Mound|XMM|Shambling Mounds}.
A {@creature Tyrannosaurus Rex|XMM} or two {@creature Giant Crocodile|XMM|Giant Crocodiles}.
A murdered member of another group. Roll a die; if you roll an even number, the corpse has a {@item Potion of Healing|XDMG}.

entries.name: Chakuna During the Trial
entries: Chakuna might appear and menace characters, but don't track her travels or have her fight the characters directly.

entries.name: Kiru Island Event
entries: During the last phase of their journey, groups traveling to Kiru Island must find a way to cross Sangui Cove. While crossing, they are attacked by a {@creature Giant Shark|XMM} and a {@creature Giant Crocodile|XMM}.

entries.name: Scars Event
entries: During the last phase of their journey, groups traveling to the Scars must climb 120 feet up Anquara Plateau. While they do, a {@creature Giant Ape|XMM} attacks them from above.

entries.name: Trial Shrine
entries: At both destinations, the trial shrine is a wooden platform bearing a statue of a panther. It hangs at the end of a 200-foot-long rope bridge suspended 60 feet over a spiked pit. Those who fall into the pit take 21 ({@dice 6d6}) Bludgeoning damage plus 36 ({@dice 8d8}) Piercing damage.

entries.name: Rival
entries: If the characters reached the shrine in less than 15 hours, they are the first ones there. After {@dice 2d4} hours, the pirate group arrives at the Kiru Island shrine, and the lizardfolk group arrives at the Scars shrine. Both groups are missing any members who died in previous encounters, but they're {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} and fight the party. If the characters took longer than 15 hours, this rival group arrives first.

entries.name: Conclusion
entries: An hour after the group contends with its rivals, Chakuna arrives. If the characters followed the rules of the Trial of Hearts, she pronounces them victors and upholds her end of the bargain—helping them leave Valachan. If they broke the trial's rules in even the slightest way, Chakuna knows it and fights them. In either case, each character earns a bonus 2,100 XP.

entries.name: Other Domains of Dread
entries: None can say how many domains drift through the Mists. This section hints at the multitudes of additional Domains of Dread. Detail and explore them in your adventures, or use them as examples when creating your own domains using {@book chapter 4|RHW|4}.

entries.name: The Agency

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: Flimira Vhage

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Detective work, memory loss

entries: Everything inside the office of the Agency is a monotone gray. Anyone who passes the frosted glass door into this single-room domain is expected by Flimira "Flintlock" Vhage, the detective agency's owner. From here, Flimira collects cryptic correspondence relating to mysteries across the Domains of Dread. She enlists agents to investigate these cases, then report back to her. However, she never reveals her own past as a detective-turned-criminal, her involvement in every case her agency investigates, or that the Agency exists within her mind.

entries.name: Bluetspur

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: The God-Brain

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Abductions, false memories, illithids

entries: The entity that would become the God-Brain of Bluetspur was an illithid elder brain whose existence-violating experiments revealed a malignant truth of the multiverse. This secret manifested in the elder brain's mind as an alien disease. Terrified of learning this pernicious, devouring knowledge themselves, the other illithid elder brains banished their peer from existence. In actuality, the Dark Powers claimed the afflicted elder brain and granted it an alien domain. Now called the God-Brain by the mind flayers and stranger entities that serve it, this elder brain pursues radical methods of saving itself from the thoughts destroying it. Its servants claim trespassers and beings from other domains, violating their minds with their bizarre experiments.

entries.name: Carnival

items.name: Darklords:
items.entry: Isolde and {@item Nepenthe|VRGR}

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Bargains, misfits, wandering exiles

entries: The Carnival is a wandering domain that appears within other Domains of Dread. Before the Carnival appears, mysterious fliers blow into communities near where it's about to arrive. When it arrives, the Carnival sets up a surreal wonderland of entertainment and oddities staffed by performers, outcasts, and cursed individuals from countless lands. Attractions include grand shows at the big top, sideshow performances, and the Hall of Horrors. The Carnival's mysterious leader is Isolde, a good but wrathful warrior of the {@book Feywild|XDMG|5|Feywild} who's cursed to lead the Carnival even as she hunts an elusive foe across the Domains of Dread. She carries the bladelike {@i Nepenthe}, a weapon that hungers for blood. Although driven by vengeance and painful memories, Isolde protects the Carnival at all costs.

entries.name: Cyre 1313, the Mourning Rail

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: The Last Passenger

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Escape from disaster, lightning rail

entries: The disaster known as the Mourning numbers among the greatest tragedies to befall the world of Eberron—a mysterious calamity that killed nearly everyone in the land of Cyre. In the nation's capital of Metrol, some citizens foresaw the coming devastation and sought to escape on lightning rails: elemental-powered engines capable of pulling trains of passenger carts. As scared innocents packed Metrol's last lightning rail, known as Cyre 1313, the evacuation was delayed at the demand of a late-arriving VIP. Hundreds were forced from passenger carts to admit and maintain the secrecy of this last passenger and their retinue. When the lightning rail finally departed, it was too late—the Mourning overtook the train and its escapees. But even as it did so, the Mists claimed Cyre 1313 and all aboard it. Now, the last lightning rail from Metrol hurtles through the Mists as a traveling domain.

Those on board fear the disaster pursuing them, the mysterious passenger seated in the train's foremost cart, and the necrotic energy now infusing the engine's elemental spirit. Yet none of the passengers realizes that their endless quest for escape is pointless, as Cyre 1313 carries only the dead.

entries.name: Forlorn

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: Tristen ApBlanc

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Life and death, strange invention

entries: Tristen ApBlanc was born amid tragedy, the son of a vampire father and a human noble. Fearful villagers murdered his parents, leaving the boy to be adopted by druids. But during his teenage years, his {@race dhampir|RHW} nature revealed itself. When the druids discovered the youth's hunger for blood, they cast him out. Tristen grew enraged and slaughtered them, draining them all. The sacred waters of the druids' rituals had infused their blood, though, making it poison to the dhampir. Tristen died with his adopted family, but as the Mists closed in around their sacred stone circle, he rose as a ghost.

Unexpectedly, with the dawn, the Darklord's dhampir body was restored. Now, he lives by day, a perpetually young, charming, invention-obsessed dhampir dwelling in Castle Tristenoira, the smoking fortress his goblin servants built over the druid circle of his onetime family. At night, though, the dhampir dies a painful death, then rises in his spectral form to scour all that is green and vibrant from his land.

entries.name: Ghastria

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: Stezen d'Polarno

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Cursed art, dour population

entries: A notorious hedonist, Marquis Stezen d'Polarno enjoyed popularity among his noble peers but craved immortality. A mysterious artist offered him eternity by painting Stezen's portrait on a magical canvas. But the artist didn't mention that the painting would strip Stezen of his love of life and natural charm. Despite his dulled existence, the marquis enjoys one reprieve: Once every season, when he shows his portrait to an audience, it consumes their souls and refreshes his thrill for life. Stezen's artistic predations captured the attention of the Dark Powers, which drew his lands into the Mists. Now, Ghastria is a fertile island that, like its lord, lacks an essential vim—except for once per season, when vigor fleetingly returns.

entries.name: G'henna

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: Yagno Petrovna

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Corrupt theocracy, false deity

entries: Born of a Barovian family, Yagno Petrovna went missing on the slopes of Mount Ghakis as a youth. As a violent storm rose, he took shelter in a mysterious ruin and was found weeks later wandering the hills, babbling about an amber idol and the god he'd discovered: Zhakata the Provider and the Devourer. His family sought to help him, but when they learned Yagno had been secretly sacrificing people to his fictitious god, they chased him into the Mists. When he emerged, the domain of G'henna sprawled before him.

Life is hard in G'henna, a rocky land home to fierce, ravenous animals. The domain's starving people worship the bestial god Zhakata and regularly travel to the cathedral-city of Zhukar. There, they offer their crops in sacrifice and hear Zhakata's will through the revered prophet Yagno Petrovna.

entries.name: I'Cath

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: Tsien Chiang

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Labyrinth, inescapable dreamworld

entries: In her youth, Tsien Chiang accidentally slew her gold dragon mentor to create a magic, wish-granting bell capable of driving invaders from her homeland. Her scheme worked, and the people made Chiang their queen. Over time, though, the people bristled under Chiang's rule and rose up in revolt. Slaughter and loss on both sides led Chiang to ring her magic bell again, but this time the bell cracked, and the Mists spilled forth. When they faded, Tsien Chiang was the ruler of I'Cath, a labyrinthine, nightmare city where the people sleep endlessly. In their dreams, they toil forever in a fantasy version of the city, working to maintain the perfectionism of their harsh queen.

entries.name: Invidia

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: Gabrielle Aderre

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Bad parents, possessed children

entries: Gabrielle Aderre is convinced that her son, Malocchio, is destined for greatness. From her estate outside the village of Karina, she employs an endless string of servants to provide Malocchio with the best possible upbringing. Inevitably, though, every servant flees or vanishes, either as a result of Gabrielle's unreasonable expectations or the deadly tricks and accidents that frequently occur around the child. Gabrielle isn't content to leave her son's grand destiny to chance, though. Using her precious bone spirit board, she calls on supernatural forces to guide and protect her son. Spirits, angels, fiends, and worse answer her summons—but as long as they chart Malocchio's path to glory, the Darklord eagerly accepts their gifts.

entries.name: Kalidnay

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: Thakok-An

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Magical apocalypse, obsession

entries: The city of Kalidnay was once the seat of power of Kalid-Ma, one of the monstrous sorcerer-kings of Athas. Thakok-An, the greatest of Kalid-Ma's enforcers, desired the sorcerer-king so greatly that when she learned of a plot against her master, she didn't reveal it. Instead, she performed a magical rite to grant Kalid-Ma even greater power, sacrificing her own family to do so. Her ritual went wrong, though, and life-draining magic washed over Kalidnay, destroying the city and draining life from nearly all its residents. Awaking amid the ruins, Thakok-An found Kalidnay surrounded by an endless sea of silt and mist. Worse, rather than being empowered, Kalid-Ma languished in a cursed torpor, the sorcerer-king's vast magic bleeding away.

Now Thakok-An obsesses over her sleeping king. From Kalidnay's survivors, she's created a new cult of servants. Following Thakok-An's wishes, these templars condemn trespassers in Kalidnay to ritual combat in the arena, where the lives of the slain sustain the secretly withering sorcerer-king.

entries.name: Keening

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: Tristessa

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Banshee, silent village

entries: The forbidding land of Keening is dominated by Mount Lament, at whose base lies the village of Anwrtyn. The people of Anwrtyn deafen themselves so they won't hear the shrieking of the banshee Tristessa, a mournful spirit who roams Mount Lament and whose wail carries through the night. In life, Tristessa dwelled under Mount Arak in Tepest (detailed earlier in {@book this book|RHW|2}), but she was exiled for crimes against her people. In death, she endlessly seeks to reunite with the family slain by her misdeeds.

entries.title: The deadly shrieks of the banshee Tristessa echo through the mountainous domain of Keening

entries.name: Klorr

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: Klorr

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Impending doom, surreal environment

entries: Klorr is the end of worlds. Here, shattered islands drift through a misty netherworld, caught in a swirling, clockwork spiral that ends at the unignorable, burning eye called Klorr. Thirteen stars orbit this sunlike sphere, one winking out every hour. Each time one of the stars dies, one of the domain's ruined islands is drawn into Klorr and consumed by flames. Afterward, each other island wrenches ahead, then halts, one hour closer to the same doom. When all thirteen stars have winked out, they return hourly to the sky.

Those who dwell on the crumbling islands trapped in the domain count the hours until their end. Few know how they came to Klorr or when new islands get added to the cycle, only that the Mists closed in and doomed them. Amid the realm's surreal skies float the ruins of lost domains—among them, a city of skulls, a realm overrun by dogs, and an array of bloody, iron arenas. Echoes of domains that exist elsewhere in the Domains of Dread also drift here, including at least one duplicate of Castle Ravenloft. Those cast away amid this orderly apocalypse grow ever more desperate to defy the doomsday clock and the will of a hidden Darklord, the obsessed clockmaker named Klorr.

entries.name: Markovia

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: Frantisek Markov

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Depraved science, sapient animals

entries: Dr. Frantisek Markov is a genius—but less so than yesterday. His Markov Formula grants him unparalleled intellect, but it insidiously steals more than it gives, making him increasingly dull-witted and bestial in form. Despairing, the doctor fled to a tropical island he dubbed Markovia, where he tests new versions of his formula on the local fauna in hopes of recovering his waning genius. As a result of these tests, animals across Markovia now possess sapience and have been deluded into worshipping Markov as their god.

entries.name: Nightmare Lands

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: The Nightmare Court

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Nightmares, recurring dreams

entries: Any who sleep might become forever trapped in the Nightmare Lands, a phantasmagoric realm whose features shift endlessly. Those who visit and escape speak of malicious wildernesses; the empty city called Nod; and uncountable drifting spheres, each containing a stranger's unending nightmare. Insidious entities called the Nightmare Court rule the domain. No one knows how many members make up the court, but they include the tragically graceful Ghost Dancer, the tomb-bound Hypnos, the witch Mullonga, the trickster Morpheus, and the embodiment of terror known as the Nightmare Man. These beings, artisans of nightmares, visit terrors on any whose sleeping minds brush against the domain.

The Nightmare Court's members share one commonality: All are the living nightmares of Caroline Dinwiddy, a potent psychic who has repressed memories of her own heartless deeds. These memories torment her sleeping mind, creating the Nightmare Court. Deep within the City of Nod, inside a warped reimagining of the clinic where she once worked, Caroline sleeps without waking, refusing to face the terrors her dreams unleash on innocents across the Domains of Dread and beyond.

entries.name: Niranjan

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: Sarthak

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Asceticism, brainwashing, shadows

entries: The Ashram of Niranjan is a vibrant vihara, or monastery, on a misty chain of islands. Here the elderly scholar Niranjan leads ascetics in practicing Ramsana, a way of life prioritizing nonattachment to the material world. In truth, Niranjan is Sarthak, a wicked bronze dragon who sends agents into the Mists bearing his philosophical writings. These works promise escape and peace to any who adopt their teachings and search the Mists for their source. Those who come to the ashram must divest themselves of worldly goods, which become part of Sarthak's hidden hoard. Sarthak then helps his victims enter a trance that causes their souls to slip away from their bodies. Sarthak consumes these souls and replaces them with shadows, leaving the victims' bodies under his control.

entries.name: Nova Vaasa

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: Myar Hiregaard

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Nomadic riders, transformation

entries: An unparalleled warrior, Myar Hiregaard united the nomadic tribes of the vast plains of Nova Vaasa. But, while respected as a soldier, Hiregaard made a poor peacetime leader. When brutal games could no longer keep her interest, she incited hostilities between two of her vassal tribes, then led her own forces to crush them. Subtly, she did this again and again.

After Hiregaard's greatest massacre, the Mists enfolded all of Nova Vaasa, splitting the Darklord's personality in two when they did. Now she rules her people with strict fairness—but when her bloodlust is piqued, she transforms into the raging knight called Malkan and sows discord across the plains.

entries.name: Odaire

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: Maligno

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Evil toys, village of children

entries: The toymaker Guiseppe had his wish for a family granted when his creation, the marionette Figlio, came to life. A proud father, the toymaker presented his son to all the people of his village, Odaire. The local children loved Figlio, but their skeptical parents said the marionette was nothing but a toy. Over time, this doubt enraged Figlio, and the marionette convinced Guiseppe to craft siblings for him. Then, when the time was right, Guiseppe's creations did away with all the adults in Odaire.
Claimed by the Mists, Odaire is a village populated only by children and ruled by Figlio the {@creature Carrionette|RHW} (see {@area chapter 5|39b|x}), who now calls himself Maligno.

entries.name: Richemulot

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: Jacqueline Renier

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Martial law, plague, wererats

entries: The once-beautiful cities of Richemulot crumble as season after season of the Gnawing Plague afflicts the domain. Countless commoners and aristocrats have died, causing rule to settle with the vain noble Jacqueline Renier. Unbeknownst to the people, Jacqueline is a wererat whose servants create and spread the plague. She loathes what her domain has become but refuses to cede power back to the people by ending the ongoing disaster. Jacqueline's obedient state police, the Casques Silencieux, enforce martial law and quarantines across the domain.

entries.name: Rider's Bridge

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: The Headless Rider

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Haunted bridge, murderous legend

entries: Nearly every domain knows some version of the apparition called the Headless Rider. It appears as a mercenary in dark armor in Mordent, a ghostly cataphract in Har'Akir, and a mutated centaur in Lamordia. But in each incarnation, certain details remain true: the rider lacks its head, it appears on a prominent bridge, and it decapitates victims as it endlessly searches for its own head. Anyone who escapes the Headless Rider might find a different domain on the opposite side of the spirit's bridge. (See "{@area Dullahan|3a1|x}" in {@book chapter 5|RHW|5} for more details.)

entries.name: Risibilos

items.name: Darklord:
items.entry: Doerdon

items.name: Hallmarks:
items.entry: Misdirection, ventriloquism

entries: The Risibilos is a small music hall, similar to those found in any decent-sized city. Its lord, Doerdon, was once a king—one so thoroughly humorless that he forbade his subjects the privilege of laughter, on pain of death. He now finds himself cursed to entertain others—a task he is utterly unqualified for. Fortunately, the Mists delivered him a partner, a ventriloquist's dummy carved in the likeness of {@creature Strahd von Zarovich|RHW}.
